ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT, KENTUCKY , ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE
SECOND , AND ENDED ON WEDNESDAY , MARCH THE
FIFTEENTH , 1934 .
CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT to empower and direct the Commissioner of Agricul-
ture, Labor, and Statistics , to establish and promulgate official
standard grades for tobacco in green order ; to provide for the
inspection , grading and marketing of tobacco in green order ,
and to prohibit violations of this Act and to prescribe penal-
ties , and to fix a charge of not to exceed one- half of one cent
per hundred pounds to defray expenses of administering this
Act and to be paid by the purchaser .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. For the purpose of this Act the terms :
(a) "Tobacco in green order " means any tobacco
in green order sold in Kentucky .
(b ) " Seller " means any association , corporation ,
warehouseman , and/or person and/or his or its rep-
resentative offering for sale or selling tobacco in
green order in this State .
(c) " Commissioner ' means the Commissioner
of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics .
2. The Commissioner is hereby empowered
and directed in his discretion :
(a) To establish and promulgate from time to
time official standard grades for any kinds of to-

bacco in green order produced in this state which

CHAPTER 2 .
AN ACT providing that the Commissioner of Agriculture , Lahor
and Statistics and the Kentucky Board of Agriculture shall
have exclusive jurisdiction , supervision and control of the Ken-
tucky State Fair and its grounds ; providing that the Commis-
sioner of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics shall have the
power of Sheriffs and Constables and that all peace officers on
said grounds shall perform their duties under his control and
supervision ; providing that the Kentucky State Fair shall have
a prior lien upon the property of any concessionaire or ex-
hibitor for any indebtedness due the Kentucky State Fair ;
and providing for the promulgation of rules and regulations
for the operation of said Fair .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 1. In addition to the power, duties and author-
ity now vested in the Commissioner of Agriculture ,
Labor and Statistics and the Kentucky State Board
of Agriculture over the Kentucky State Fair , its
grounds , sites , property , buildings and concessions ,
which are or may be a part of or incident to the
operation of the Kentucky State Fair or the Ken-
tucky State Fair Grounds , the Commissioner of
Agriculture , Labor and Statistics shall be the chief
peace officer and in charge of said Kentucky State
Fair and the Kentucky State Fair Grounds , and
shall have the power of sheriffs and constables and
all peace officers who may be upon said grounds
shall be under his control and supervision and shall
execute their duties subject to his approval and
control .
§ 2. The Commissioner of Agriculture , Labor
and Statistics and the Kentucky State Board of
Agriculture shall have exclusive control of conces-
sions , exhibitions , shows , entertainments and at-
tractions , both on and off the mid-way and any
place within the confines of the grounds of the Ken-
tucky State Fair .
§ 3. The Kentucky State Fair shall have a prior
lien upon the property of any concessionaire , exhib-
itor or person immediately upon coming or being
brought on the grounds to secure existing indebted-
ness as well as that which might afterwards arise ,
with power in the Commissioner of Agriculture to
sell the same and apply the proceeds of sale to the
satisfaction of the indebtedness upon the giving of
ten days ' notice to the owner or agent of the owner ,
and in the event this cannot be done then by posting
a notice in the office of the Secretary of the Kentucky
State Fair on the grounds or such other place as the
Secretary's office may be located , that the sale of
said property is to be made ; and the Commissioner

CHAPTER 3 510
of Agriculture shall have the right to bid and buy
in the property offered for sale for the use and
benefit of the Kentucky State Fair.
§ 1. The Commissioner of Agriculture shall
have the right to delegate the powers hereby
granted to the Secretary of the Kentucky State
Fair ; and during the absence of the Commissioner
from the grounds , the said Secretary shall have full
power and right to act ; and the Commissioner of

mulgate and issue such rules and regulations for

CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT appropriating $ 100,000.00 out of the general expenditure
fund for the purpose of paying salaries and other expenses
incident to holding the 1934 legislative session ; and declaring
an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the gen-
eral expenditure fund of the Commonwealth the sum
of $ 100,000.00 for the purpose of paying salaries and
other expenses incident to the holding of the 1934
legislative session .
§ 2. The appropriation thus made is to supple-
ment the remainder left to the credit of the legisla-
tive session budget , as provided by Chapter 25 , Sec-
tion 76 of the Acts of the 1932 General Assembly
which was depleted by the 1933 extraordinary ses-
sion of the General Assembly.
Therefore , an emergency is declared to exist , and
this Act shall take effect and be in full force and
effect from and after its passage and approval .
Approved March 17 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 7.
AN ACT relating to the present employees of the General As-
sembly providing compensation for their services in the Gen-
eral Assembly and for payment thereof to the Chief Clerk of
the Senate and the Chief Clerk of the House of Representa-
tives , respectfully, to be distributed by them to all of said em-
ployees and officers , prorated according to the amount pro-
vided by House Bill No. two hundred forty -five of the Acts of
the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred and
thirty -four, and authorizing and directing the Auditor of
Public Accounts to draw his warrants on the Treasurer for
the total amount of such compensation payable to the chief
clerk of the Senate and the chief clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives , and declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , the present employees and officers of the General
Assembly have rendered faithful services , and whereas , they
have had to employ assistants to aid them in their respective
duties , and ,
WHEREAS , the General Assembly deems that the compensa-
tion fixed by House Bill Number two hundred forty-five of the
Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
and thirty -four is inadequate , now therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The following amount is hereby appro-
priated to the employees of the Senate and
House of Representatives , respectively , as addi-
tional compensation to that provided in House Bill
number two hundred forty -five of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
and thirty -four ; to the employees and officers of the
Senate , four thousand eight hundred eighty dollars ;
to the employees and officers of the House of Rep-
resentatives , three thousand one hundred fifty -nine
dollars . The amount hereinabove provided to be
paid for the benefit of the employees and officers of
the Senate shall be paid to the Chief Clerk of the
Senate , to be distributed by him to all of said of-
ficers and employees of the Senate , prorated on

CHAPTER 7 11
amounts authorized to be paid to them by House
Bill number two hundred forty -five of the Acts of
the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
and thirty -four , and the Auditor of Public Accounts
hereby is authorized and directed , upon requisition
made by the Chief Clerk of the Senate , to draw a
warrant on the Treasurer in favor of said Chief
Clerk in the amount of four thousand eight hundred
dollars to be districted ( distributed ) by said Chief
Clerk as herein provided . The amount hereinabove
provided to be paid for the benefit of the employees
and officers of the House of Representatives shall
be paid to the Chief Clerk of the House of Repre-
sentatives to be distributed by him to all of said
employees and officers of the House of Representa-
tives , prorated on amounts authorized to be paid to
them by said House Bill number two hundred forty-
five of the Acts of the General Assembly of one
thousand nine hundred and thirty -four , and the
Auditor of Public Accounts hereby is authorized
and directed upon requisition made by the Chief

Clerk of the House of Representatives in the

dollars , to be distributed by said Chief Clerk as

five of the Acts of the General Assembly of one

amount hereby appropriated , his legal salary , or at

least not less than six dollars per day , and there is

ALL ACTS or parts of Acts in conflict herewith ,

CHAPTER 9.
AN ACT appropriating Five Thousand Dollars out of the general
fund for the purpose of erecting statute ( statue ) of Jefferson
Davis , to be located upon completion in the rotunda of the
capitol building at Frankfort , Kentucky ; providing that the
additional funds may be donated by any person or organization
for the purpose of supplementing the sum appropriated ; cre-
ating a commission for the purpose of carrying out the pro-
visions of this Act ; repealing all laws in conflict .

CHAPTER 10 13
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
general fund the sum of Five Thousand Dollars to
be used for the erection of a statute ( statue ) of
Jefferson Davis , President of the Confederacy , bids
for which are hereby authorized . Said statute
(statue ) is to be made of marble , or some other suit-
able stone, the same size and proportion as that of
Abraham Lincoln , now standing in the rotunda of
the capitol building . Said statute ( statue ) of Jef-
ferson Davis is to be located in the rotunda of the
capitol building at Frankfort , Kentucky , beside said
statute ( statue ) of Abraham Lincoln .
$ 2 . To carry out the purposes of this act there
is hereby created the following Commission to be
composed of five members to be appointed by the
Governor to wit : two members from a list submit-
ted by the United Daughters of the Confederacy ;
two members from a list to be submitted by the
Sons of the Confederacy ; one member appointed
from the State at large.
§3 . Nothing in this act is to be construed to pre-
vent any person or organization from supplement-
ing or adding to the sum herein appropriated by a
like amount or some other sum.
$ 4.
Alllaws
orparts
oflaws
inconflict here-
with are hereby repealed
.
Neither approved nor disapproved
.

session , and chapter eighteen ( 18 ) of the Acts of the General
Assembly at its 1932 session , all of same pertaining to banks
and banking .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That chapter sixteen ( 16 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly at its 1932 session , and chapter
eighteen ( 18 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly
at its 1932 session , be and the same are hereby
repealed .
2. That section eighteen ( 18 ) of chapter sev-
enteen ( 17 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly at
its 1932 session , approved March 24 , 1932 , be
amended and re -enacted so that when amended and
re-enacted shall read as follows :
The Banking and Securities Commissioner may
under his hand and official seal appoint one or more
special deputy banking and securities commission-
ers as agent or agents to assist him in the duty of
liquidating and distributing the assets of any in-
solvent bank , the certificates of appointment to be
filed in the office of the Banking and Securities Com-
missioner , and a certified copy in the office of the
clerk of the circuit court in the county in which the
office of such insolvent bank is located .
The Banking and Securities Commissioner may ,
from time to time , authorize a special deputy com-
missioner to perform such duties connected with
the liquidation and distribution as the Banking and
Securities Commissioner may deem proper . The
Banking and Securities Commissioner may employ
such counsel , and procure such expert assistance
and advice as may be necessary in the liquidation
and distribution of the assets of such bank , and
may retain such officers or employees of such bank
as he may deem necessary . He shall require from a
special deputy commissioner such surety for the

CHAPTER 10 15
faithful discharge of his duties as he may deem
proper ; provided , however , it shall not be for less
than ten per cent ( 10 % ) of the assets of the bank
when such assets amount to one million dollars
($1,000,000.00 ) or more , and not less than fifteen
per cent (15 % ) of the assets of such bank when such
assets amount to five hundred thousand dollars
($500,000.00 ) or more and less than one million dol-
lars ( $ 1,000,000.00 ) , and for not less than twenty -five
per cent ( 25 % ) of the assets of such bank when
such assets amount to less than five hundred thou-
sand dollars ($500,000.00 ) .
The surety bond of a special deputy banking and
securities commissioner above required shall be an
annual bond , and a new bond shall be executed at
the end of each yearly period and no former bond
shall be executed or accepted in the form of a re-
newal bond . The Banking and Securities Commis-
sioner shall have the power and authority and it
shall be his duty to reduce the amount of the afore-
said surety bond at the end of each and every yearly
period according to and in the amount of the per-
centages herein provided of the assets of the bank
remaining in the hands of such special deputy bank-
ing and securities commissioner at the time such
yearly bond is executed and at the end of the yearly
period . The bank in liquidation shall pay the
premium upon the surety bond of the special deputy
banking and securities commissioner herein pro-
vided for , and same shall be charged as an expense
of liquidation . In the event any special deputy
banking and securities commissioner shall for any
reason vacate his office before the annual bond ap-
proved and provided for expires , then there shall
be paid to the bank in liquidation the unearned por-
tion of the premium thereon .

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Whenever, under the provisions of this act , the
banking and securities commissioner , in person or
by a deputy or by a state bank examiner , shall take
charge of any bank , or the court shall appoint a
receiver as in this act is provided, such person shall
be allowed for his services not exceeding the sum of
two dollars per hour for not exceeding ten hours
per day for the time actually engaged by him in
labor in the liquidation of the bank . If such per-
son be a special deputy , appointed by the commis-
sioner , or a receiver , appointed by the court , such
allowance shall be paid to him as his compensation ,
and if such person shall be the banking and securi-
ties commissioner or his deputy or a state bank ex-
aminer , the amount so allowed as compensation
shall be paid into the state treasury ; and the com-
missioner shall fix the compensation of the counsel
and any special assistants subject to review by the
court , and all such expenses shall be paid out of
the bank so liquidated.
Whenever the banking and securities commis-
sioner , in person , or by deputy or by state bank ex-
aminer , shall take charge of any bank under the
provisions of this act , he shall within thirty days
thereafter , file in the office of the clerk of the circuit
court of the county in which the bank is located , a
detailed statement in the form prescribed in section
10 of this act , of the assets and liabilities of such
bank , and thereafter upon the first day of the term
of each succeeding session of the circuit court of
such county , and every sixty days in counties hav-
ing circuit courts of continuous sessions , he shall
file a report of his acts and doings in the adminis-
tration of such bank since his last report ; and any
person , firm or corporation may , by petition ad-
dressed to the circuit court , have any act of such
commissioner reviewed by the court, in the same

CHAPTER 12.
AN ACT to amend Section eight hundred sixty-five , of Chapter
one hundred forty-four of the Acts of one thousand nine hun-
dred eighteen , as amended by Chapter twenty-five of the Acts
of one thousand nine hundred twenty-six , and by Chapter
twelve of the Acts of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ,
being Section eight hundred sixty-five of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , of one thousand nine hundred thirty , relating to
Building and Loan Associations , the lending of money ac-
cumulated to members , investment of surplus and providing
for the investment of surplus in the bonds of the Home
Owner's Loan Corporation and for the exchange of real estate
and other investments or securities held by them for the
bonds of the Home Owner's Loan Corporation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section eight hundred sixty -five of
Chapter one hundred forty - four of the Acts of one

CHAPTER 12 23
thousand nine hundred eighteen , as amended by
Chapter twenty -five of the Acts of one thousand
nine hundred twenty -six and by Chapter twelve of
the Acts of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ,
being Section eight hundred sixty-five of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes of one thousand nine hundred
thirty , be , and the same is hereby amended by add-
ing the words " Or in the bonds of the Home Own-
er's Loan Corporation , " immediately after the
words : " Acts of Congress ' where said words first
appear in said section , and by adding at the end of
said section the following words : " Building and
Loan Associations may exchange any real estate ,
real estate mortgage notes , bonds , or any other
investments or securities held by them for the bonds
of the Home Owner's Loan Corporation , taking the
said bonds at par in lieu of cash and may take the
said bonds at their face value in reduction , or in
payment of indebtedness due to any building and
loan association , or in payment for , or on account
of, the purchase price on a sale or transfer of any
property , investment, or asset of the building and
loan association , " so that said section , when so
amended , shall read as follows :
"The moneys accumulated after due allowance
made for necessary and proper expenses and for
withdrawal of shares , shall at each monthly or
weekly meeting be offered to members according
to their priority or right to a loan as fixed by the
by-laws . Each member whose application for a loan
has been approved shall be entitled , upon giving
proper security and complying with the by-laws , to
receive a loan equal par value of each share or frac-
tional share held by him or such part as the by-
laws allow, provided , however , that the by-laws
may restrict the maximum amount of loan to which
members may be entitled . If a balance of money re-

24 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
mains after the weekly or monthly loans are
granted , the directors may invest the same in good
and safe bonds , or in such other securities in which
banks are now permitted to invest, or in the bonds.
or debentures of any Home Loan Bank established
by Act of Congress , or in the bonds of the Home
Owner's Loan Corporation . Building and Loan As-
sociations operating under this Act may subscribe
for and invest their funds in the stock of any Home
Loan Bank established by Act of Congress in such
amounts and upon such terms as may be permitted
by the laws and regulations governing said bank .
Building and Loan Associations may exchange any
real estate , real estate mortgage notes , bonds , or
any other investments or securities held by them for
the bonds of the Home Owner's Loan Corporation ,
taking the said bonds at par in lieu of cash , and
may take the said bonds at their face value in reduc-
tion, or in payment of indebtedness due to any build-
ing and loan association , or in payment for , or on
account of , the purchase price on a sale or transfer
of any property , investment , or asset of the building
and loan association . "
§ 2. All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict here-
with , to the extent of such conflict , are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 3. Whereas , in view of financial conditions
generally and the difficulty in obtaining security and
protection of funds , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 13.
AN ACT to amend Section 4706 , of Chapter 141 of the Acts of
1918, amending Chapter 122 of the Acts of 1892 , and as
amended by Chapter 91 of the Acts of 1932 ; being Section
4706, Chapter 128 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , re-
lating to the investment of funds of Fiduciaríes and providing
for the investment by persons or corporations holding funds
in a fiduciary capacity for loan or investment in the bonds of
any Federal Home Loan Bank and the bonds of the Home
Owners ' Loan Corporation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4706 , Chapter 141 , of the Acts
of 1918 , amending Chapter 122 of the Acts of 1892 ,
and as amended by Chapter 91 of the Acts of 1932 ,
being Section 4706 of Chapter 128 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes of 1930 , be , and the same is , hereby
amended by adding after the words , " Approved
July 17, 1916 " the following words : "And the
bonds of any Federal Home Loan Bank and the
bonds of the Home Owners ' Loan Corporation " so
that said Section when amended shall read as fol-
lows :
"It shall be lawful for persons or corporations
holding funds in a fiduciary capacity for loan or
investment , to invest the same in bonds or other
interest bearing obligations of the Government of
the United States of America ; bonds or State war-
rants and other interest bearing obligations of the
State of Kentucky ; securities authorized by an Act
of Congress entitled ' The Federal Farm Loan
Act , ' and approved July 17 , 1916 ; and the bonds
of any Federal Home Loan Bank and the bonds of
the Home Owners ' Loan Corporation ; and real
estate , real estate mortgage notes , bonds , and other
interest bearing or dividend paying securities which
would be regarded by prudent business men as safe

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
investments ; and to make loans with such securi-
ties as collateral ; and a fiduciary shall account for
all interest or profit received .
Where securities originally received by a fiduci-
ary do not meet the requirements for the investment
of trust funds as provided in this statute , or where
securities in which trust funds have been properly
invested thereafter become ineligible for investment
of trust funds as provided in this statute , then such
ineligible securities shall be sold at such time , and
upon such terms and conditions , or shall be held or
otherwise handled in such a manner , as prudent
business men would , under the particular circum-
stances , consider proper , and a fiduciary shall be
responsible for otherwise acting . "
§ 2. All acts and parts of acts in conflict here-
with , to the extent of such conflict , are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 3. Whereas , in view of financial conditions
generally and the difficulty in obtaining security and
protection of funds , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor.
Approved March 14, 1934

in the bonds of any Federal Home Loan Bank and the bonds

4444

CHAPTER 14 27
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section five hundred eighty -two of
Chapter one hundred seventy -one of the Acts of
one thousand eight hundred ninety -three , as
amended by Chapter thirteen of the Acts of one
thousand nine hundred twenty- eight being Section
five hundred eighty-two of Article two , of Chapter
thirty -two of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of one
thousand nine hundred thirty , be , and the same is ,
hereby amended by adding after the words " five
hundred seventy -nine " the following words , " but it
may invest in the bonds of any Federal Home Loan
Bank or in the bonds of the Home Owners ' Loan
Corporation , " and by adding at the end of the said
section the following : " Banks may exchange any
real estate , real estate mortgage notes , bond or any
other investments or securities held by them for
the bonds of the Home Owners ' Loan Corporation ,
taking the said bonds at par in lieu of cash and may
take the said bonds at their face value in reduction
or in payment of indebtedness due to any bank , or
in payment for , or on account of , the purchase price
on a sale or transfer of any property , investment or
asset of the bank, " so that said section when so
amended shall read as follows :
"No bank shall employ its moneys , directly or in-
directly, in any enterprise or business , except as
provided in Section five hundred seventy -nine , but
it may invest in the bonds of any Federal Home
Loan Bank or in the bonds of the Home Owners '
Loan Corporation ; and it may hold such personal
property as has been transferred to it as collateral
for the payment of any debts , and may acquire title
to and hold such real estate as may be necessary
for the transaction of its legitimate business , and
for a period not longer than ten years such other

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
real estate as shall be conveyed to it in satisfaction
of debts previously contracted in the course of its
business , or that it may purchase under a judgment
in its favor .
Banks may exchange any real estate , real estate
mortgage notes , bonds or any other investments or
securities by them for the bonds of the Home Own-
ers ' Loan Corporation , taking the said bonds at par
in lieu of cash and may take the said bonds at their
face value in reduction or in payment of indebted-
ness due to any bank , or in payment for , or on ac-
count of, the purchase price on a sale or transfer of
any property , investment or asset of the bank . " "
§ 2. All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict here-
with , to the extent of such conflict , are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 3. Whereas , in view of financial conditions
generally and the difficulty in obtaining security and
protection of funds , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall become effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval of the Gov-
ernor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT to define , license , and regulate the business of making
loans or advancements in the amount or of the value of three
hundred dollars ( $300 ) or less , secured or unsecured , at a
greater rate of interest , consideration , or charge than six per
centum (6 % ) per annum , prescribing the rates of interest and
charges therefor and penalties for the violation thereof , regu-
lating the assignment of wages or salaries , earned or to be
earned , when given as security for any such loan or as consid-
eration for a payment of three hundred dollars ( $300 ) or less ,
providing for the administration of this act , authorizing the
making of examinations and investigations and the publication
of reports thereof, providing for a review of decisions and find-
ings of the Banking Commissioner under this Act , and repealing
so much of Section 4224a , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ( 1930 )
as applies to persons licensed under this Act , and limiting the
application of Sections 2218 and 2219 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes ( 1930 ) , and repealing all Acts and parts of Acts
whether general , special , or local , which relate to the same
subject matter as this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The following terms , as used in this Act ,
shall have the meaning stated , unless the context
requires a different meaning :
(a) "Person " includes individuals ,individuals , partner-
ships , associations , and corporations .
(b) " Licensee " means a person licensed under
this Act .
(c) "Commissioner " means the " Banking Com-
missioner of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . "

this Act and without first obtaining a license from

engage in the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the
business of making loans in the amount or of the
value of three hundred dollars ( $ 300 ) or less and
charge, contract for , or receive , directly or indi-
rectly , or by any device , subterfuge , or pretense
whatsoever , a greater rate of interest, or considera-

all the provisions of this Act applicable to the orig-
inal issuance of licenses . Whenever a licensee shall
desire to change his place of business to another
location within the same municipality the Commis-
sioner , on application , if he finds that the interests
of the community will be served thereby , shall in-
dorse on the license a transfer to the new place of
business , with the date of transfer , which indorse-
ment shall be authority for the operation of such
business under such license at such new location .
No change in the place of business of a licensee to a
location outside of the original municipality shall
be permitted under the same license .
(c) No licensee shall conduct the business au-
thorized by this Act in any office , room, or place of
business in which any other business is solicited or
engaged in , or in association or conjunction there-
with , except upon a written authorization from the
Commissioner. No such authorization shall be
granted unless the Commissioner finds that the so-
liciting of or engaging in such other business in the
same office , room, or place of business in which the
business of making loans under this Act is conduct-
ed will not facilitate evasions of this Act . No licensee
shall make any loan under this Act under any other
name or at any other place of business than that
stated in the license .

CHAPTER 17 37
(d ) Each license shall remain in full force and
effect until it is surrendered by the licensee or sus-
pended or revoked as hereinafter provided . Each
licensee shall, on or before the twentieth day of
each December , pay to the Commissioner the annual
license fee for the next succeeding calendar year .
( e) The Commissioner shall , upon ten days '
written notice to the licensee stating that he con-
templates the revocation of his license , and , in gen-
eral , the grounds therefor , revoke such license , after
reasonable opportunity has been afforded to the
licensee to be heard , if the Commissioner finds (1 )
that the licensee has failed to pay his annual license
fee or to maintain in effect the bond or bonds re-
quired under this Act , or ( 2 ) that the licensee has
violated any provision of this Act or has failed to
comply with any rule or regulation lawfully made
pursuant thereto , or ( 3 ) that any fact or condition
then exists which clearly would warrant the Com-
missioner in refusing to issue a license on an orig-
inal application . The Commissioner may, without
notice or hearing , suspend any license for a period
not exceeding thirty days , pending investigation .
The Commissioner shall have authority to reinstate
suspended licenses or to issue new licenses to a
licensee whose license or licenses shall have been
revoked if no fact or condition then exists which
clearly would have warranted the Commissioner in
refusing originally to issue such license under this
Act. Any licensee may surrender any license

5. No licensee shall advertise , print , display ,
publish , distribute , or broadcast , or permit to be ad-
vertised , printed , displayed , published , distributed ,
or broadcast , any statement or representation which
refers to the supervision by the Commonwealth of
Kentucky of the business licensed hereunder . Nor
shall any licensee or any other person knowingly in
any such manner make or permit to be made any
statement or representation with regard to the
rates , terms , or conditions for making loans in the
sum of three hundred dollars ( $ 300 ) or less , which
is false , misleading, or deceptive .
§ 6. ( a) ( 1 ) Every licensee hereunder may
lend any sum of money not exceeding three hundred
dollars ( $ 300 ) in amount and may charge , contract
for , and receive thereon interest at a rate not ex-
ceeding three and one-half per centum ( 32 % ) per
month on any part of the unpaid principal balance
of the loan not in excess of one hundred and fifty
dollars ($150 ) and two and one-half per centum
(22 % ) per month on any remainder of the unpaid
principal balance of the loan .
(2 ) No amount whatsoever shall be paid , de-
ducted , or received in advance . Interest shall not
be compounded and shall be computed only on un-
paid principal balances . The maximum interest

CHAPTER 17 39
permitted on loans made under this Act shall be
computed on the basis of the number of days ac-
tually elapsed and for the purpose of such compu-
tations a month shall be any period of thirty con-
secutive days . In addition to the interest herein
provided for no further or other charge or amount
whatsoever for any examination , service , broker-
age, commission , expense , fee , or bonus or other
thing or otherwise shall be directly or indirectly
charged , contracted for , or received , except how-
ever , the lawful fees , if any , actually and neces-
sarily paid out by the licensee to any public official
for filing or recording in any public office any in-
strument securing the loan .
(3 ) No person who is a licensee under this Act
shall induce or permit any borrower or borrowers
to split up or divide any loan or loans , nor permit
any one borrower or any husband or wife individ-
ually or together to become indebted to him under
more than one contract of loan at the same time if
the actual amount of the indebtedness on any one
of such contracts is in the amount or of the value of
three hundred dollars ( $300 ) or less and there is
charged , contracted for , or received thereon , di-
rectly or indirectly , by any device , subterfuge , or
pretense whatsoever , any interest, or consideration
therefor greater than six per centum (6% ) per
annum .
(b) If any interest , consideration , or amount in
excess of the interest authorized by this Section is
charged , contracted for , or received by any licensee
under any contract of loan or loan transaction , such

(d ) The borrower may pay all or any part of

( e ) No licensee shall take any confession of

that does not accurately disclose the actual amount
of the loan, the time for which it is made , and the

the execution of such instrument . No instrument
taken under authority of this Act shall be nego-
tiable .

and shall maintain at all times assets of at least
twenty thousand dollars ($20,000 ) either in liquid
form available for or actually used in the making of
loans under this Act at the location specified in the
license .
(g) No licensee shall take any lien upon real
estate as security for any loan under this Act , ex-
cept such lien as may be obtained by virtue of a
judgment or decree .

8. No licensee shall, directly or indirectly ,

rectly or contingently , or both , to the licensee at any

( $ 300 ) for principal .
§ 9. The payment of three hundred dollars
( $ 300 ) or less in money , credit , goods , or things in
action , as consideration for any sale , assignment , or
order for the payment of wages , salary , commis-
sions , or other compensation for services , whether
earned or to be earned , shall be deemed for the
purposes of regulation under this Act a loan se-
cured by such assignment , and the amount by which
such assigned compensation exceeds the amount of
such consideration actually paid shall be deemed
for the purposes of regulation under this Act in-
terest or charges upon such loan from the date of
such payment to the date such compensation is pay-
able . Such transaction shall be governed by and
subject to the provisions of this Act .
§ 10. ( a ) The provisions of this Act shall be
enforced by the Commissioner , who is authorized to
make such rules and regulations , in addition hereto
and not inconsistent herewith , as may be necessary
for the proper conduct of the business licensed here-
under and for the enforcement of this Act . The
Commissioner shall make an annual examination
of the affairs , business , office , and records of every
licensee , and such further examinations or investi-

CHAPTER 17 43
gations as he shall deem necessary for the purpose
of discovering violations of this Act or of securing
information necessary for its proper enforcement .
For every examination made under this Act the
Commissioner shall charge the same fees as are
allowed by statute for the examination of banks .
For the purpose of making such examinations or in-
vestigations the Commissioner and his duly desig-
nated representatives shall have authority to re-
quire the attendance of and to examine under cath
all persons whomsoever , whose testimony he may
require , relative to the loans or business of any
such licensee , and shall have free access to the ac-
counts , papers , records , files , safes , vaults , offices ,
and places of business used in connection with any
business conducted under any license issued in ac-
cordance with this Act .
(b) All moneys collected by the Commissioner
under the provisions of this Act for license fees
and examinations or otherwise shall be paid as col-
lected into the State Treasury and then credited to
an account of the Banking Department .
(c) Each licensee shall annually on or before
the fifteenth day of March file with the Commis-
sioner a report giving such information as the Com-
missioner may require relevant to the business and
operations during the preceding calendar year of
each licensed place of business conducted by such
licensee in the Commonwealth . Such report shall
be made under oath and in the form prescribed by
the Commissioner . The Commissioner shall make
and publish annually an analysis and recapitulation
of such reports .
(d) Each licensee shall keep and use in his busi-
ness and shall preserve for at least two years after
making the final entry on any loan recorded therein ,
such books , accounts , records , or card systems as will

enable the Commissioner to determine whether such
licensee is complying with the provisions of this
Act and with the rules and regulations made pur-
suant thereto .
§ 11. Any individual or any member , officer , di-
rector, agent or employee of any association , part-
nership , or corporation , who shall violate or par-
ticipate in the violation of any of the provisions of
Sections 2, 4 ( c ) , 5 , 6 ( a ) , 6 ( c ) , 6 ( d ) , 6 ( e ) , 6 (g) , 7 ,
10 ( c ) , or 10 ( d ) of this Act , shall , upon conviction
thereof, be punished by a fine of not more than
three hundred dollars ($300 ) or by imprisonment
for not more than six months , or both .
Any contract of loan not invalid for any other
reason , in the making or collection of which any act
shall have been done which constitutes a misde-
meanor under this Section , shall be void and the
lender shall have no right to collect or receive any
principal , interest or charges whatsoever .
§ 12. This Act shall not apply to any person ,
partnership , association , or corporation doing busi-
ness under and as permitted by any law of this
Commonwealth or of the United States relating to
banks , savings banks , trust companies , trust bank-
ing and title insurance companies , building and
loan associations , co -operative marketing associa-
tions, credit unions , or licensed pawnbrokers , nor to
any person , partnership , association or corporation
engaged in the business of purchasing direct from
dealers , notes arising from the sale of motor ve-
hicles or of financing or refinancing individuals
through the medium of loans upon the sole se-
curity of liens upon motor vehicles .
§ 13. ( a ) So much of Section 4224a , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes (1930 ) as applies to persons
licensed under this Act is hereby repealed . This
Act shall not be construed as repealing or amend-

ing Section 2218 or Section 2219 , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes ( 1930 ) but said Sections shall not
apply to loans made under authority of this Act.
(b) All other Acts and parts of Acts , whether
general , special , or local, which relate to the same
subject matter as this Act are hereby repealed .
§ 14. No assignment of or order for payment of
any salary , wages , commissions , or other compensa-
tion for services , earned or to be earned , when taken
as security for any loan made under this Act , shall

Under any assignment of or order for the payment

collectible from the employer of the borrower more
than a sum equal to ten per centum ( 10 % ) of the
amount then owing the borrower for any such sal-
ary , wages , commissions or other compensation for
services .
§ 15. If any provision of this Act , or the appli-

held invalid , the remainder of the Act, and the ap-
plication of such provision to other persons or cir-
cumstances , shall not be affected thereby .

CHAPTER 18 .
AN ACT to amend Sections 864 , 864a , 864b and 865a of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , providing for the method of with-
drawal of shares in building and loan associations and making
of loans by building and loan associations , and repealing acts
or parts of acts in conflict herewith .

46 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections 864 , 864a , 864b and 865a of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , be and are hereby
amended by striking out all of Sections 864 , 864a ,
864b and 865a of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of
1930 , so that when amended and re-enacted said
sections shall read as follows :
Section 864. Withdrawals . A member may with-
draw his or her unpledged shares at any time by
giving thirty days ' written notice of his or her
desire to do so . Upon the filing of such written ap-
plication to withdraw , the association shall number
and file the same in the order received and shall ,
after thirty days from the receipt of such applica-
tion to withdraw , either pay the holder the value
thereof , in part or in full as requested , or apply at
least one-half of the receipts of the association as
hereinafter provided from its shareholders and bor-
rowers to the withdrawal of such shares in numer-
ical order. Shareholders filing written application
for the withdrawal of their shares shall remain
shareholders until paid , and shall not become
creditors .
Provided , further that an association shall not be
required to pay out on withdrawals more than one-
half of the net weekly receipts of such association
after the payment of current dividends , and after
making provision for anticipated dividends and the
payment of said dividends, repaying money bor-
rowed , and after paying administration expenses of
the association , taxes , insurance and repairs on
property owned , municipal assessments , and any
other necessary disbursements .
Provided , further that should one member apply
for a withdrawal of more than five hundred
($500.00 ) dollars , he shall be paid $ 500.00 in order

CHAPTER 18 47
when reached , and his application shall be charged
with such amount as paid and shall be renumbered
and placed at the end of the list of applications to
withdraw , and thereafter upon again being reached ,
shall be paid a like amount , but not to exceed the
value of his shares , and until paid in full shall con-
tinue to be so paid , renumbered , and replaced at the
end of the list until paid in full . A member with-
drawing , when paid , shall receive the full amount
paid in as dues or paid-up stock together with only
such dividends as have been declared and credited
on said shares less all fines , interest or other
charges .
Provided further , subject to the approval of the
Board of Directors any borrowing member , who
shall fully satisfy the directors of his inability to
pay, may be permitted to withdraw from his or her
dues a sum sufficient to be applied to the payment
of taxes and assessments for municipal improve-
ments , and that the dues so withdrawn shall be ap-
plied
forsuch purpose under the supervision
ofthe
association
, and that the receipts evidencing such
payments shall be filed in the office of the associa-
tion with the note and mortgage
ofthe members

in

ofthe money paid
inby

,

of

allfully paid

48 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
prepaid or matured shares that may be outstanding
shall first be retired .
Section 864b . Matured Shares . When the dues
paid in with the dividends credited on any un-
pledged shares reach the par value of shares as
fixed by the articles of incorporation , all payments
of dues shall cease , and if the member so desires he
or she shall be entitled to receive the value as fixed
by the articles of incorporation with interest at such
rate as may be fixed by the Board of Directors .
Payments on withdrawal of matured shares shall
be otherwise subject to the same restrictions and
limitations as payments on other shares as set out
in Section 864 .
Section 865a . Loans ; How Secured ; Penalties
and Failure to Offer Good Security . For every loan
made a note secured by first mortgage on real estate
shall be given accompanied by a transfer and pledge
of shares of the borrowers . The shares so pledged
shall be held by the corporation as collateral secu-
rity for the performance of the conditions of said
note and mortgage . The note and mortgage shall
provide the number of shares pledged and the
amount of money advanced thereon and shall be
conditioned for the payment as provided in the by-
laws , of the monthly or weekly dues on said shares
and the interest and premiums , if any , upon the
loan , together with all fines , upon payments in
arrears until said shares shall reach the value fixed
by articles of incorporation or the by-laws or said
loan is otherwise canceled and discharged .
Paid up , installment and other shares without
other security may , at the discretion of the direc
tors , be pledged as security for loans to an amount
not exceeding 80 per cent of their value , as de-
termined at the time of application for said loan .

CHAPTER 19 49
If the borrower neglects to offer security satis-
factory to the directors within the time prescribed
by the by-laws , his right to the loan shall be for-
feited and he may, in the discretion of the board of
directors , be charged with one month's interest at
the rate for which said money is loaned together
with all expenses , if any, incurred and the money
appropriated for the loan be reloaned at the next
or any subsequent meeting of the Board of Di-
rectors .
Section 865b . All acts or parts of acts in conflict
herewith be and the same are hereby repealed . The
general powers granted herein shall not be re-
stricted by the giving of special powers .
Whereas no provision now exists for the pay-
ment of withdrawals of small amounts out of turn ,
and in all associations in the State of Kentucky
there are members who are holding investments of
less than $ 100.00 who are actually in dire distress
under the present economic conditions , it is there-
fore declared that an emergency exists and this act
shall take effect from and after its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved March 15 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 19 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section 165A-33 of the
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition , 1933 supplement , being
section 34, of Chapter 17 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of Kentucky of 1932.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 165A -33 of the Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's Edition , 1933 Supplement , being Section 34,
of Chapter 17 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of Kentucky of 1932 , be repealed , amended and re-

50 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
enacted so that when amended and re - enacted said
section shall read as follows :
" 165A - 33 . Registration of dealers and sales-
men : No dealer or salesman shall engage in busi-
ness in this state as such dealer or salesman or sell
any securities , including securities exempted in
Section Twenty -five ( K. S. Sec . 165A - 24 ) of this Act ,
except in transactions exempt under Section Twen-
ty -six ( K. S. Sec . 165A -25 ) of this Act , unless he has
been registered as a dealer or salesman in the office
of the commissioner pursuant to the provisions of
this Section .
Every dealer subject to the provisions of this Act
before engaging in business in this State shall file
in the office of the Commissioner an application for
registration in writing in such form as the commis-
sioner may prescribe , duly certified by oath , which
shall state the principal office of the applicant ,
wherever situated , and the location of the principal
office and all branch offices in this State , if any , the
name or style of doing business , the names , resi-
dences and business addresses of all persons inter-
ested in the business as principals , co -partners ,
officers and directors , specifying as to each his ca-
pacity and title , the general plan and character of
business and length of time the dealer has been
engaged in such business . The Commissioner may
also require such additional information as to the
applicant's previous history , record and association
as he may deem necessary to establish the good re-
pute in business of the applicant .
Every such dealer shall file with his application
an irrevocable written consent to the service of
process upon the Banking and Securities Commis-
sioner in actions against such dealer in manner and
form as herein provided in Section Thirty -two ( K.
S. Sec . 165A - 31 ) .

CHAPTER 19 51

cation a bond , with good and sufficient surety , which
bond shall be approved by the Commissioner , run-

56 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4134 of Kentucky Statutes ( Car-
roll's 1930 Edition ) relating to bonds of sheriffs
and sureties thereon , be amended and re -enacted , so
tha said section , when amended and re-enacted , shall
read as follows , to -wit :
4134. The county court may require the sheriff
to give an additional bond or bonds , with good sure-
ty , to be approved by the county court whenever it
may deem the interest of the state , or county de-
mands ; and the sureties on all the bonds executed by
the sheriff shall be jointly and severally liable for
any default of the sheriff during the year in which
said bond may be executed , whether the liability
accrues before or after the execution of such bond
or bonds : Provided , that a surety on any bond exe-
cuted by the sheriff shall not in any event be liable
for any act or default of the sheriff occurring in
any calendar year other than that within which the
bond or bonds shall have been executed ; and pro-
vided, further that neither the sheriff nor a surety
or sureties on any bond or bonds executed by the
sheriff shall be liable for any act or default of the
sheriff unless the act or default of the sheriff giving
rise to a claim upon such bond shall have been
given to the surety or sureties by the State Auditor ,
the judge of the county court the county attorney or
other person asserting such claim ninety days after
such discovery or at the latest within the period of
one year from and after the end of the year within
which such bond or bonds shall have been executed .
Whereas , many sheriffs are required to execute
bonds within a short time , and in many instances
sureties are unwilling to execute such bonds until
this statute as amended shall have gone into effect ,
i

CHAPTER 21 57
and many of the sheriffs will be unable to secure
bonds until the provisions of this statute as amend-
ed , shall have gone into effect , and emergency is
hereby declared to exist , and this act shall become
effective from and after its passage and approval
as required by law .
Approved March 21 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 24 .
AN ACT relating to the fiscal administration of counties ; pro-
viding for a uniform budget system in each county of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , for the administration of the fiscal
affairs of the counties ; prescribing the duties of the county
judge , county court clerk , county treasurer , county attorney
and other officers with reference thereto ; creating a county
budget commission , and prescribing its duties ; providing for
the submission to and examination by the State Inspector and
Examiner of budget reports covering every proposed budget
of each county government , and for review thereof by him ;
prohibiting the levying of taxes , the incurring of obligations ,

CHAPTER 24 63
and the authorizing of expenditures except in accordance with
the appropriations provided by such budget's authorizing ap-
propriations for contingent purposes and transfers between
appropriations ; declaring of no effect all tax levies and in-
debtedness incurred in contravention of the act ; fixing liability
for any expenditure or obligation so made or incurred ; pro-
hibiting disbursements except by warrants under appropria-
tions against cash actually available ; repealing all laws and
parts of laws in conflict with the provisions of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. The fiscal affairs of each county of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , except those pertain-
ing to education shall be administered by the fiscal
court under a uniform budget system . The county
budget shall provide for all the funds to be expend-
ed by the county from current revenue for each
fiscal year . For the purposes of this Act , the State
budget officer shall classify the several counties
upon the basis of their populations and expenditures
or upon some other proper basis .
2. County budget commission ; appointment ;
terms of office ; qualifications .
There is hereby created in each county of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky a County Budget Com-
mission . The county budget commission shall be
composed of the County Judge , County Attorney
and one member appointed by the fiscal court by an
order entered on the county court order book . The
appointive member shall be at least thirty years of
age , a citizen , resident and taxpayer of the county .
He shall not hold any other public office at the time
of appointment nor for a period of at least two
years prior to this appointment , but shall be eligible
for
re-appointment
tothis office
. The term
ofhist
office shall
bethe same
asthe terms
ofoffice
ofthe

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
members of the fiscal court making the appoint-
ment .
§ 3. Duties of the commission ; general budget
funds . It shall be the duty of the county budget
commission at a meeting or meetings to be held not
later than the first day of May each year , to make
a detailed investigation of each separate activity of
the county for which county funds are to be expend-
ed by the fiscal court. For the purpose of main-
taining a uniform system all expenditures shall be
classified into budget units including
( 1 ) General expenses of county government
( 2) Protection to persons and property
(3) General health and sanitation
(4 ) Highways and bridges
( 5 ) Charities , hospitals , infirmaries , and correc-
tions
( 6 ) Recreational
(7) New property and improvements
( 8 ) Interest and debt payments
( 9 ) Miscellaneous and contingent .
Each county budget commission shall adopt such
budget units as may be required by the activities of
the county , and , when necessary may sub -divide the
various budget units into separate budget funds so
that the cost of each class of expenditures can be
ascertained at any time and regulated by the com-
mission according to the financial conditions and
the needs of the county .
The funds set apart in the county budget for a
budget unit , or any subdivision thereof, shall con-
stitute a separate budget fund and shall be ap-
propriated and accounted for separately .
The county budget commission in addition to pre-
paring a reasonable estimate of the funds actually
needed for both general and special purposes shall

CHAPTER 24 65
prepare an estimated statement of receipts to be
anticipated from the various sources of income .

and Examiner shall have full power and authority
to supervise and direct the form and classification

5. All proposed county budgets
, adopted by thecounty budget commission and approved by the

66 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fication shall be submitted to the fiscal court at the
time fixed for the adoption of the county levy and
budget for approval or rejection and not later than
July first of each year . The commission shall post
or cause to be posted in a conspicuous place in the
court house near the front door a copy of the pro-
posed budget for at least ten days preceding the
time of final approval by the fiscal court and pub-
lished in a newspaper of general circulation in the
county at least ten days before final approval .
In case the fiscal court shall reject any part of the
proposed budget , then the fiscal court itself shall
make such changes in the nature and amount of
funds as will meet the approval of a majority of
the Court . The fiscal court shall have no power or
authority to make any change in the form or classifi-
cation of the budget units or sub -divisions thereof
as approved by the State Inspector and Examiner .
Any taxpayer or group of taxpayers shall have
the right to petition the fiscal court in respect to the
budget or any part thereof before final approval .
$ 6. The Budget.--The county budget as ap-
proved and adopted shall provide for all funds to be
expended by the county from the current revenue
for the fiscal year for which the budget is adopted
and any appropriation or claim allowed by the fiscal
court in excess of any budget fund shall be null and
void and shall be construed as a violation of this
Act by each member of the fiscal court so voting for
said illegal appropriation or claim .
The fiscal court with the approval of the com-
mission shall have power and authority to transfer
money from one budget fund to another in order
to provide for emergencies but the order of the
Court approving such transfer shall show the nature
of the emergency and the reason for making such

CHAPTER 24 67
transfer . The fiscal court itself shall have no power
to make such transfer without approval by the com-
mission . Neither the court nor the commission shall
have any power or authority to transfer any monies
from any sinking fund or special funds raised for
a specific purpose until the obligations or purposes
for which said funds were raised have been satis-
fied .
The county budget shall have a fund known as the
'Sinking Fund Principal Account " out of which
the bonded indebtedness shall be paid and to which
annually there shall be allocated a sum equal to the
proportional yearly amount necessary to retire the
honds at maturity . There shall likewise be a fund
in the budget known as the " Sinking Fund Interest
Account " to which there shall be allocated annually
an amount equal to the interest on the bonded in-
debtedness maturing during the current budget year .
7. Budget Records . In addition to the usual
records of the fiscal court , the clerk of the fiscal
court shall keep an appropriation ledger , either in
loose leaf or bound form , containing separate ac-
counts for each budget fund . Each separate ac-
count shall show in detail the amount of the original
appropriation , any and all transfers to or from the
budget fund,

68 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and to make such investigations as may be neces-
sary to determine the status or condition of the
financial affairs of the county . If he shall find that
any county officer , or any member of the commis-
sion is willfully violating or neglecting his duties
under the provisions of this Act , he shall have the
authority to institute legal proceedings in the
Franklin Circuit Court to enforce compliance with
the law. As state budget officer it shall be his
duty to cooperate with the commission and the clerk
of the fiscal court in establishing and maintaining
an efficient and economical control of the fiscal af-
fairs of the county .
The fiscal court of any county shall have power
and authority to anticipate the revenues of the
county for the fiscal year and borrow money upon
the faith thereof , to meet the current expenses of
the county as they accrue , or to pay off and dis-
charge interest -bearing county warrants becoming
due before sufficient revenues have accrued to pay
off and discharge said interest -bearing warrants .
All notes of the county issued under this section in
excess of the budget fund from which they are pay-
able shall be null and void .
Notes issued under this section shall not be in
the aggregate in excess of ninety per cent . ( 90% )
of the amount of the budget fund out of which they
are payable , and the county treasurer shall be liable
on his official bond for the amount of the excess of
any note of the county countersigned by him in ex-
cess of ninety per cent . (90 % ) of the budget fund
out of which said note is payable . Any note issued
in excess of ninety per cent . ( 90 % ) of any budget
fund of the county shall be null and void as to the
county . All notes so issued shall be countersigned
by the county treasurer .

CHAPTER 24 69
It shall be the duty of the State Inspector and
Examiner to install in each county of the Common-
wealth a uniform system of accounting and book-
keeping for the administration of the fiscal affairs
of the county under the provisions of this act .
All books , receipts , blanks , records , county war-
rant book , budget forms , and all other forms re-
quired to be used by county officials in the admin-
istration of the fiscal affairs of the county under
the provisions of this act shall be provided and paid
for by the county and approved by the State In-
spector and Examiner in his capacity as supervisor
of county budgets as to form and classification .
$ 8. Quarterly Statements .-At the end of each
three months period a statement showing actual re-
ceipts to date from the various budget sources and
a statement showing the total appropriations to
budget funds , the total expenditures to date there-
from , and the balance in the fund shall be posted.
by the County Judge in a conspicuous place in the
court house near the front door for at least ten
consecutive days .
9. Claim Procedure .-All orders of the fiscal
court approving claims shall designate the budget
fund from which each claim must be paid and each
county warrant shall show in written form the
budget unit from which it is to be paid .
County10. Treasurer's Statements .- The
Treasurer shall file with the clerk of the fiscal court
a monthly statement containing a list of warrantspaid by him during the month and showing all cash
receipts, the cash balance at the beginning and the
close of the month and certify that each warrant or
contract is within the budget appropriation ; other-wise , any such warrant or contract shall be nulland void .

70 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 11. Penalties for Violation .-Any county of-
ficer or any member of the commission willfully
violating the provisions of the Act shall be deemed
guilty of a misdemeanor and , upon conviction , shall
be fined in any sum not less than fifty dollars
($50.00 ) nor more than two hundred dollars
($200.00 ) . In addition the county treasurer shall
be liable on his official bond for the amount of any
county warrant willfully or negligently signed or
counter -signed by him under the provisions of this
act in excess of the budget fund out of which said
warrant is payable .
§ 12. It is declared to be the legislative intent
to enact each clause , paragraph , and section of this
act separately and if any clause , paragraph , or sec-
tion should be declared invalid the invalidity of such
clause , paragraph , or section shall not affect the re-
maining clauses , paragraphs , or sections .
§ 13. All laws and parts of laws in conflict with
the provisions of this act , are hereby repealed .
§ 14. This act shall take effect on the first day
of July , 1934 , with respect to the budget for the
fiscal year beginning with the first day of July , 1935 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 25
AN ACT providing for an adequate , unified , and integrated sys-
tem of financial and business administration for the State
government , embracing budgeting , accounting , pre -auditing,
treasury administration , post -auditing , and fiscal reporting ;
prescribing additional duties for the State Inspector and
Examiner ; creating the office of State Budget Officer and
prescribing his duties ; providing for clerical and other help
and for the fixing of salaries , providing penalties for the viola-
tion of the provisions of this Act , and repealing all laws and
parts of laws inconsistent herewith.

CHAPTER 25 71
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I- SHORT TITLE AND DEFINITIONS .
1. Short Title . This act shall be known , and
may be referred to , as the " Budget and Financial
Administration Act of 1934. "
$ 2 . Definitions of Terms . For the purposes of
this act and unless otherwise required by the con-
text :
(a) "Appropriation " means an authorization
by the General Assembly to a budget unit to expend
from public funds , a sum of money not in excess of
the sum specified , for the purposes specified in such
authorization , under the procedure herein pre-
scribed .
(b) " Appropriation act " means an act of the
General Assembly that authorizes the expenditure
of State money.
(c) " Budget " means the complete financial plan
for each fiscal year as proposed in the budget report
and modified and adopted by means of the appro-
priation and revenue acts .
(d) " Budget class " means one of the kinds
of expenditure denoting a class of services or com-
modities purchased or properties acquired as speci-
fied in the classification of expenditures prescribed
as provided herein , for use in expenditure account-
ing , in the making of budget estimates , and in thebudget reports and budgets .
(e) " Budget estimates " mean the statements
with its accompanying explanations , as provided
herein, in which a budget unit states its financial re-
quirements and requests appropriations .
(f) " Budget report " means the recommenda-
tions of the Governor to the General Assembly , as

72 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to financial plans and expenditures to be authorized ,
with the accompanying statements and explanations ,
prepared as provided herein .
(g) " Budget unit " means a department or other
unit of organization , for which separate appropria-
tions are made from those for any other organiza-
tion unit .
(h ) " Capital outlays " means the exchanges of
values involved in acquiring lands , buildings , equip-
ment , or other permanent properties , or in their
construction or development or permanent improve-
ment .
(i) " Disbursements " mean cash actually paid
out for any or all purposes .
(j ) " Employee " means the lawful incumbent
of a position as defined herein .
(k) " Expenditures " mean expenses and capital
outlays .
(1) "Expenses " mean values consumed in main-
taining , managing and operating government serv-
ices , in contributing to the cost of maintaining and
operating public services supported in whole or in
part by the Commonwealth , and in providing in ac-
cordance with law for any grants , aids , relief , or
compensation to individuals , whether such values
are represented by cash paid out or by obligations
to pay cash , or partly by each .
(m) "Position " means any office or employ-
ment , or combination of them , in the service of the
Commonwealth , involving duties to be performed
and responsibilities to be exercised by one person.
(n ) " Receipts " mean cash actually received
from any or all sources .
( o) " Revenues " mean values accruing without
resultant increase in liabilities or decrease in assets ,
whether such values are represented by cash actual-

CHAPTER 25 73
ly received or by amounts due and payable , or partly
by each .
(p ) " Operating receipts " mean cash received
by a budget unit for services rendered , or from the
sale of materials , goods , or supplies created by the
budget unit .
( q ) "Revolving Fund Account " means a State
treasury account accruing to the credit of a budget.
unit from operating receipts , fees , gifts , or appro-
priations which may be used in defraying main
tenance and operating expenses of activities and
agencies which are in whole or in part self support-
ing.

ARTICLE III-BUDGETING .
§ 1. Expenditures to be authorized by Annual
or Biennial Appropriation Acts . All expenditures
of the Commonwealth and of all of the budget units
thereof shall be made by virtue of the authority of
an annual or biennial appropriation act or appro-
priation acts , which shall be based upon a budget
prepared as set forth in this act .
§ 2. Biennial Budget Report . The Governor , by
and with the advice and assistance of the State
Budget Officer and the Board of Finance shall bien-
nially prepare and submit to each and every regular
session of the General Assembly, a budget report
presenting a complete financial plan for each fiscal
year of the ensuing biennium , for adoption either

CHAPTER 25 75
with or without amendments and modifications by
the General Assembly . Said budget report shall be
prepared as set forth in this act .
3. The Appropriation and Revenue Acts , the
State Budget and Budgetary Allotment . The finan-
cial plan for each fiscal year as presented in the
budget report with such modifications as may be de-
termined by the General Assembly , shall be adopted
and made effective by the passage of an appropria-
tion act or acts and such revenue and other acts as
may be necessary for the purpose . The appropria-
tion act or acts shall authorize the maximum amount
to be expended in addition to the net balance of the
operating receipts by each budget unit for all its
ordinary recurring expenses of operation and main-
tenance , and for each item of extraordinary ex-
penses and capital outlay for the year , in accord-
ance with the detailed financial plan set forth in the
budget report as modified by the General Assembly.
§ 4. Contents of Biennial Budget Report . Each
biennial budget report shall present a complete
financial plan for each fiscal year of the ensuing
biennium . It shall include :

nished by the State Budget Officer to aid the admin-

$ 6. Heads of Budget Units to Submit Budget
Statements and Other Data on Prescribed Forms.
The head of each budget unit , not later than the
fifteenth day of November of each odd - numbered
year , shall submit to the State Budget Officer , esti-
mates of the financial requirements and of the re-
ceipts of each budget unit for the next two fiscal
years , on the forms and in the manner prescribed
by the State Budget Officer , and accompanied by
such explanatory data as may be required by him,
together with such additional information as the
head of the budget unit may wish to submit . The
estimates so submitted shall bear the approval of
the boards or commissions of budget units for which
such boards or commissions are constituted .
7. Continuous Financial Planning . The State
Budget Officer shall have in continuous process of
preparation and revision , in the light of his direct
studies of the operations , plans , and needs of budget
units and of the existing and prospective sources of
income , a tentative budget report for the next en-
suing biennium for which a budget report is re-
quired to be prepared . Upon receipt of the esti-
mates of the several budget units , he shall check
these estimates in the light of his own information ,
and shall make such further inquiries and investi-
gations and revise his tentative budget report as he
deems warranted . Such tentative budget report ,
when approved by the Board of Finance , shall be
certified to the Governor , together with the budget
statements provided in Section 6.
$ 8. Duties of the Governor in Preparing the
Budget Report . The Governor , during the progress

CHAPTER 25 81
of preparation of the Budget report and prior to its
submission to the General Assembly , shall examine
the statements and estimates and shall make or
cause to be made such further investigations by the
State Budget Officer , with such hearings before the
Governor , the Board of Finance , or other agency ,
as he may deem advisable , and shall direct such
changes or revisions in policy and program and in
specific details , as he may find warranted .
$ 9. Means of Informing Governor -Elect as to
Budget Proposals . The Governor -Elect shall be en-
titled to examine the budget report in process and
the State Budget Officer shall provide him with
every practicable facility for familiarizing himself
with its contents . Such Governor -Elect shall be en-
titled to a seat in all hearings thereon . He shall be
furnished a copy of the budget report as approved
by the Governor .
$ 10 . Budget Report to be Printed and Trans-
mitted to House of Representatives . The State
Budget Officer shall have the budget report , as ap-
proved by the Governor , printed in such number
of copies as the Governor may order , and copies
thereof shall be transmitted to the House of Repre-
sentatives not later than the third Monday after the
convening of the General Assembly in regular ses-
sion .
§ 11. State Budget Officer to Serve Appropria-
tion Committees . From the time of the transmis-
sion of the budget report to the House of Repre-
sentatives until the approriation bill or bills shall
have been finally disposed of, the State Budget Of-
ficer , in person or by an assistant shall be at the
disposal of the General Assembly and the appropria-
tion committees thereof , and shall devote so much
of his time as may be required to the work of the

82 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
appropriation committees , under the direction of the
respective chairman.
§ 12. Appropriation of Preceding Year Con-
tinued Under Certain Conditions . In the event
that the General Assembly shall fail to make an
appropriation for any fiscal year for any purpose
or purposes required to be executed by provisions
of existing laws , or the Governor shall veto an ap-
propriation essential to the carrying out of any such
purpose or purposes , then and in that event , an ap-
propriation equal in amount to the appropriation
made or deemed to have been made under the pro-
visions of this section , for such purpose or purposes
for the fiscal year next preceding , exclusive of any
such appropriations for extraordinary expenses and
capital outlays , shall be deemed to have been made.
and shall continue available from year to year until
an appropriation shall have been made as provided
in this act and shall become effective . In case any
such appropriation for the fiscal year next preceding
is applicable alike for ordinary recurrent expenses ,
extraordinary expenses , and capital outlays , the
Board of Finance shall determine in what propor .
tion thereof shall be deemed under the provisions
of this section to have been made by the General
Assembly for the next fiscal year . Each appropria-
tion or the proportion of each appropriation that
shall be continued under the provisions of this sec-
tion shall be included in the budget by the State
Budget Officer and shall be made available for ex-
penditure by allotments as . in this act provided .
§ 13. Transfers Among Appropriations for Dif-
ferent Budget Classes of One Department . Trans-
fers among appropriations to the several budget
classes in any one department of the Government
service may be authorized by the Board of Finance ;
and the Auditor of Public Accounts , and the State

CHAPTER 25 83
Treasurer , shall be advised immediately of such
authorizations .

§ 15. Authorization of Capital Outlays over
$10,000.00 by Board of Finance . No work shall be
commenced and no contract shall be entered into
for the improvement of lands or the construction ,
alteration , reconstruction , or repair of any building
or other structure , sewage disposal or water supply
system , or any road or bridge, except roads and
bridges under the jurisdiction of the State Depart-
ment of Highways , involving an expenditure of more
than Ten Thousand Dollars ( $ 10,000.00 ) from the
State Treasury under any appropriation made by
the General Assembly, until the plans and specifica-
tions , estimates of cost , and the bids received , cover-
ing the entire work contemplated , and the proposed
contract or contracts therefor , have been submitted
to the Board of Finance , and the execution of such
contract or contracts , or the undertaking of such
work has been approved and authorized by such
Board . The board shall not so approve nor author-
ize any contract nor the undertaking of such work
in any such case in which it finds from the plans ,

wise : ( a ) that the cost of completing the work con-
templated will exceed the amount of appropriation
therefor , or (b ) that the work contemplated will be
insufficient for the purposes for which said appro-
priations were made, or ( c ) that after providing
for the ordinary recurring expenses of government
and for debt service , and for payments under exist-
ing allotments for extraordinary expenses and capi-
tal outlays , cash will not be available in the Treas-
ury promptly to pay for such work during the bien-
nium.

§ 18. Allotments to Govern Expenditures : Per-
missible Transfers Within Allotments . The heads of
the budget units shall hold to the work plans and
detailed expenditure estimates as authorized and
approved on advices of allotments , except as devia-
tions therefrom are made necessary by changes in
conditions of operations , to meet unforseen con-
. tingencies , and to correct errors . In no case shall
obligations be incurred or expenditures made in
excess of the total amounts allotted . Transfers
from an allowance for one budget class to an allow-
ance for another budget class within the same bud-
get unit , may be authorized by the administrative
head of the budget unit to which the allotment was
made , if approved by the Board of Finance , and
provided that the Auditor of Public Accounts and
the State Budget Officer shall be promptly advised
of all such transfers thus authorized .
$ 19. Interpretation of Items in Appropriation
Acts . All questions which may arise as to the mean-
ing of items specified in any appropriation act or in
the budget , shall be decided by the Board of Finance
in accordance with the detailed estimates or other
information embodied in the budget report as con-
sidered by the General Assembly, and the decision
of the Board shall be final and conclusive .
§ 20. Head of any Budget Unit May Appeal to
Board of Finance : When . The head of any unit ag-
grieved by any decision of the State Budget Officer
as to the amount of any allotment for a specified
period or as to the purposes for which monies al-
lotted may be expended , may appeal therefrom to
the Board of Finance , and the decision of such
Board shall be final and conclusive .
