$ 2. Form of Commitment Vouchers to be Pre-
scribed by State Budget Officer : Forms to be Used ,
How . It shall be the duty of the State Budget Of-
ficer to prescribe a form of advice of employment ,
a form of purchase order and a form of requisition
for the reservation of funds . All commitments shall
be authorized on such forms by the heads of the
budget units in conformity with the provisions of
this act , and copies of all such authorizations shall
be transmitted immediately to the Auditor of Public
Accounts and to the State Budget Officer .
§ 3. Designation of Officers or Employees Au-
thorized to Sign Commitment Vouchers . It shall be
the duty of the administrative head of each budget
unit to designate the officer or employee authorized
to sign advices of employment , copies of purchase
orders and contracts , requisitions for reservation of
funds , and letters of travel authorization for travel
outside of Kentucky , and no such advice , order , con-
tract or requisition shall be honored as a commit-
ment statement unless such designation with a copy
of the signature of such officer shall be on file in the
office of the State Budget Officer .
$ 4. Duty of the Auditor with Respect to Au-
thorized Commitments . Every advice of employ-
ment , copy of purchase order or contract requisi-
tion for reservation of funds , or letter of travel au-
thorization for travel outside of Kentucky , provided
for under this act , immediately upon its receipt in
the office of the Auditor , shall be stamped with the
date of its receipt and be examined by the Auditor
or an assistant designated by him, within the next

shall ascertain whether or not the proposed expen-

allotments to which it is proposed to charge them ,
and that the amounts involved do not exceed the un-

proposed expenditure cannot be approved by the
Auditor under the provisions of this Act , he shall
promptly notify the head of the budget unit con-
cerned that the proposed expenditure is disallowed .

§ 7. Anticipated Expenses ; Powers of Board of
Finance . The Board of Finance is hereby authorized
to grant special authority for any budget unit , dur-
ing any fiscal year , to make specific purchases of sup-
plies or materials to be used in the ensuing fiscal
year or to enter into contracts in anticipation of ap-
propriations already made for the next ensuing
fiscal year , or in anticipation of appropriations to
be made for the ensuing fiscal year for any purpose
for which there is existing law . Purchases or con-
tracts made before the close of a fiscal year under
such authority shall be treated as deferred charges.
to the apropriations and expenses of the next fiscal
year .
ARTICLE V- STATEMENTS OF INDEBTEDNESS AND
CERTIFICATION OF CLAIMS
§ 1. State Budget Officer to Prescribe Forms of
Staicments of Indebtedness ; Forms to be Used-
How. It shall be the duty of the State Budget
Officer to prescribe the forms upon which shall be
rendered all statements of indebtedness chargeable
against the appropriations and allotments to any
budget unit , which forms of statement of indebted-
ness shall be the only forms which shall be used for
presentation of claims for payment of such indebt-
edness of the Commonwealth , provided that a spe-

CHAPTER 25 91
1
cial form of statement of indebtedness or payroll
shall be required for all salaries or compensations
that have been previously fixed and determined and
are covered by appropriations , and further pro-
vided that the Budget Officer shall prescribe appro-
priate forms for statements of indebtedness of one
budget unit to another budget unit , designed to
effect settlement thereof through debit and credit
entries in the general accounts of the State . State-
ments of indebtedness other than for the salaries of
regular employees of the Commonwealth shall not
be accepted for audit and payment unless such
statement of indebtedness indicates the serial num-
ber of the purchase order , contract , requisition for
reservation of funds or letter of travel authoriza-
tion for travel outside of Kentucky , under which
such indebtedness was incurred .
$ 2 . Pre-Audit of Statements of Indebtedness .
The State Budget Officer shall prescribe the forms
on which statements of indebtedness of every kind ,
including indebtedness for the salaries of officers
and employees , shall be presented , and it shall be
the duty of the Auditor to examine all such state-
ments so presented and if found correct , to file them
and issue warrants to the State Treasurer . All
claims against the State shall be paid by the State
Treasurer on the warrants of the Auditor of Public
Accounts . Payments may be made direct by the
State Treasurer to the persons entitled to receive
them , by mail or otherwise , or through the head of
the budget units which incurred the expenditures .
The Auditor of Public Accounts shall not certify
any claim whatsoever , to the State Treasure until
he is satisfied that to meet said claim , there is a
sufficient balance of the sum appropriated to the
budget unit from which such claim may be paid .

92 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
$ 3. Compromise of Disputed Claims . The
Board of Finance , upon the request of the Auditor
and approval of the Attorney General , may author-
ize the compromise of any disputed claim by or
against the Commonwealth , or any budget unit
thereof, and shall certify to the Auditor of Public
Accounts, the amount to be received or paid under
such compromise .
§ 4. No Public Money to be Withdrawn from
Treasury Except as Provided . No public money
shall be withdrawn from the Treasury for any pur-
pose whatsoever unless it shall have been appropri-
ated by the General Assembly or be a part of a Re-
volving Fund, and allotted as in this act provided ,
and for the purpose for which its withdrawal is pro-
posed , and unless the expenditure thereof has been
authorized by the administrative head of the budget
unit and the State Budget Officer advised of such
authorization , and unless a statement of indebted-
ness has been submitted to , and approved by , the
Auditor , and then only on the warrant of the Au-
ditor of Public Accounts , as in this act provided .
The provisions of this Section shall not be con-
strued to apply to withdrawals of funds from State
Depository banks for immediate re-deposit in other
State depository banks .
5. Warrants to Specify Appropriations
Against which Drawn ; Acceptance of Warrants by
Treasurer . Each warrant of the Auditor of Pub-
lic Accounts upon the State Treasury shall specify
the particular appropriations against which it is
drawn , and no money shall be disbursed by the
Treasurer , unless the warrant contains such speci-
fications . All warrants of the Auditor of Public
Accounts upon the Treasury , issued in accordance
with the provisions of this act , shall, when signed
by the Auditor or an assistant designated by him

$ 8. Authorization of Forms to be Used in Fi-
nancial and Business Administration . All blank
forms used or that shall hereafter be proposed for
use by any or all budget units for any of the pur-
poses herein specified shall be examined by the
State Budget Officer and shall be designed or re-
designed under his supervision and direction with a
view to providing for the simplification , unification ,
and integration of all financial and business admin-
istrative records and procedures . It shall also be
the duty of the State Budget Officer to prescribe ,
and enforce adherence to , standard sizes , colors ,
and designs , of forms including styles of type and
qualities and grades of paper . This section shall
be liberally construed to apply to all forms for use
in State budgeting , accounting , pre -auditing , Treas-
ury administration , personnel administration , pur-
chasing , property control , and any other matter of
financial and business administration of the State
or any budget unit thereof .
ARTICLE VI- TREASURY ADMINISTRATION
§ 1. Collection of Fees and Other Amounts Due
the State Through the Several Budget Units . Fees
for licenses , permits , certificates of any kind , and
all other funds due any budget unit shall be col-
lected at the time such licenses , permits , or certifi-
cates are issued , by the budget units issuing them
and such other monies accruing to the several

CHAPTER 25 95
budget units or to the Commonwealth through the
several budget units shall also , so far as practicable ,
be collected at the times the services are rendered
or at the times of accrual by the budget units ren-
dering the services or assessing the charges or other-
wise giving rise to the claims in favor of the com-
monwealth ; and when collections cannot be so ef-
fected , it shall be the duty of the budget units con-
cerned , to render bills to the persons liable for the
amounts due or accruing , and to collect such bills .
All such bills or statements of indebtedness to the
State shall be rendered on forms prescribed by the
State Budget Officer . Each of the prescribed forms
shall carry notice to the effect that checks and
money orders be made payable to the State Treas-
urer . The blank forms shall be numbered and shall
be issued to the budget units by the State Budget
Officer, who shall record the numbers of those issued
to each budget unit and shall hold the administra-
tive head therefor accountable for each form so
issued and recorded . Copies of

§ 3. Auditor to Keep Record of Collection of
Revenues . It shall be the duty of the Auditor of
Public Accounts to keep up to date , a completely
detailed list of all sources from which monies accrue
to the State , classified according to the budget units
and other agencies responsible for the collection c
public funds , so as to show for each of the several
budget units and each of the other revenue - collect-
ing agencies of the State : (1 ) the several kinds of
taxes , fees , permits , and other public monies col-
lected or to be collected ; ( 2 ) the name of the per-
son responsible for collecting public monies ; and
(3) the name of the employee or employees actually
engaged in collecting , handling and depositing pub-
lic monies . The Board of Finance shall see that
collections are vigorously prosecuted , and to this
end , it is empowered to supervise and direct the
collecting and money -handling activities of all said

CHAPTER 25 97
collecting units , agencies , and employees , and to
take such steps as may be deemed necessary to en-
force the provisions of this act with respect to the
collecting and depositing of public monies .
§ 4. State Depository Bank : How Selected .
The State Treasurer shall recommend State Deposi-
tory Banks , but they shall not be so designated nor
receive deposits of State funds until approved by
the Board of Finance . A list of the depository banks
so designated shall be certified to the State Budget
Officer and a copy thereof shall also be filed with
the Auditor of Public Accounts . All public monies
of the Commonwealth now in , or hereafter received
into the Treasury shall be deposited in the banks
so designated and in no other bank or banks .
$ 5 . Receipts of the Budget Units to be De-
posited Directly in the Treasury . No receipts from
any source of State monies , or monies for which the
Commonwealth is responsible , shall be held, or
used , or deposited in any personal or special bank
account temporarily or otherwise , by any agent or
employee of any budget unit , to meet expenditures

$ 7. State Budget Officer to Prescribe Form of
Treasury Pay -In Voucher : Use of such Vouchers .
The State Budget Officer shall prescribe the form
of pay-in voucher , which shall be the only form
used in depositing public monies in the Treasury .
The blank forms shall be numbered and issued to
the budget units by the State Budget Officer , who
shall record the numbers issued to each budget unit.
and the administrative head thereof shall be held
accountable for each form so issued and recorded .
§ 8. Transfer of Depositories of Public Monies
Among Depository Banks . Whenever for any rea-
son the State Treasurer shall decide to transfer any
amount or amounts of deposits of public monies
from one or more State depository banks to one or

§ 10. Special Deposit Trust Fund. There is
hereby created a special deposit fund consisting of
all monies received by the Commonwealth or any
department or officers thereof , as guarantees for
the payment of any costs , charges or damages ac-
cruing or liable to accrue to the Commonwealth , or
for the performance of any specific act , including
all monies deposited as bail to secure the liberation
of persons accused of public offenses , all monies
deposited by the bidders on contracts to insure their
entering into contracts awarded them , and all
monies deposited to indemnify persons whose prop-
erty may be damaged or destroyed by the opera-
tions of the depositor . All such monies shall be paid
to the State Treasurer , in the manner provided by
this act for the deposit of public monies and shall
be deposited by the Treasurer as a trust fund in a
separate account in a designated depository bank.
The money so deposited may be returned to the de-positors without specific appropriation , allotment , or
authorization for expenditure therefor , should they
�

CHAPTER 25 101
become entitled to the return thereof, in the same
manner as other claims against the Commonwealth
may be paid , or may, upon default of any depositor
and upon certificate to such effect by the adminis-
trative officer having charge of the matter, be de-
clared by the Auditor of Public Accounts to be for-
feited in whole or in part and thereupon be trans-
ferred to the extent so forfeited , to the general
fund . The interest on bank deposits of this fund
shall accrue to the general fund .
$ 11 . List of Warrants Issued to be Furnished
to the State Budget Officer . All warrants issued by
the Auditor shall be submitted to the Treasurer ,
accompanied by a list or lists of all the warrants so
submitted at any one time showing, for each war-
rant the name of the payee , the amount thereof , the
fund against which drawn , and each list shall show
the total of the amounts of all the warrants listed .
The Auditor of Public Accounts shall promptly file
copies of such lists with the State Budget Officer .
§ 12. Monthly Statements from State Deposi-
tories . The president or cashier of each State de-
pository shall see to it that , on the first day of each
month , a statement over his official signature is
furnished to the Auditor of Public Accounts and
the State Budget Officer , showing the amounts of
public monies received and paid out of said de-
pository during the preceding month and the bal-
ance then remaining to the credit of the Treasury .
Any State depository which fails or refuses to com-ply, in any month , with the provisions of this sec-

§ 13. Auditor of Public Accounts to Reconcile
Bank Statements and Treasurer's and State Budget
Officer's Accounts with Depositories . It shall be
the duty of the Auditor of Public Accounts , during
the first ten days of each month , to reconcile the
statements furnished by State Depositories , the
Treasurer's accounts , and the State Budget Of-
ficer's accounts ; to examine all paid warrants to de-
termine that they are authentic and properly en-
dorsed ; to compare each paid warrant with the de-
scription thereof in list of warrants issued , as sub-
mitted by him to the Treasurer ; to check off each
paid warrant on said lists by entering opposite the
description thereof the month and year of payment ;
and to compile a detailed list of outstanding war-
rants and file copies thereof with the Treasurer and
the State Budget Officer . All paid warrants after
being verified , checked , and recorded as paid as
herein provided , shall be deposited with the
Treasurer .

§ 2. Treasurer to Keep Cash Accounts . The
accounting system prescribed and installed by the
State Budget Officer hereunder , shall provide for
the treasurer to continue to keep accounts with re-
ceipts , payments , and State depositories as here .
tofore provided by law .
$ 3 . State Budget Officer Independently to Keep
Cash Accounts . The State Budget Officer shall keep
controlling accounts with receipts , payments , and
State depositories entirely independent of those
maintained by the Treasurer . The figures for the en-
tries required to open such accounts shall be se-
cured by the State Budget Officer from the Treas-
urer . Thereafter the information required for the
keeping of such accounts shall be secured from the
pay -in vouchers , lists of warrants drawn under ap-
propriations , and vouchers requesting transfers of
deposits among State depositories provided for in
this Act .
4. Prescribed Accounting System to Provide
for Settling Transactions Between Budget Units .
The accounting system prescribed and installed
by the State Budget Officer pursuant to the provi-

CHAPTER 25 105
sions of this act , shall provide for the settlement of
transactions between budget units by the making
of debit and credit entries on his books on the basis
of adequate expenditure vouchers , without the is-
suance of warrants .
5. Fiscal Reporting . It shall be the duty of
the State Budget Officer :
( a ) Within ten days after the close of each
month , to furnish each budget unit with copies of
his active accounts with the appropriations and al-
lotments in favor of such unit , showing all the trans-
actions recorded in the previous month and the bal-
ances as of the beginning and end of that month .
(b) Within ten days after the close of each
month , to prepare and transmit to the Governor , to
each member of the Board of Finance , a report em-
bodying : A summary statement of the actual finan-
cial condition of each fund as of the close of the
previous month , a summary statement showing the
receipts and payments of each fund for the previous
month , and for the current fiscal year to the close
of the previous month , and a summary budget
statement for each fund showing the available cash
balance as of the end of the previous month , the
amount of cash estimated to be realized from each
source during the remaining months of the fiscal
year , the total means of financing , the amount of the
unallotted balances of allotments , the estimated
amount of unliquidated commitments , and the esti-
mated cash surplus or deficit as the case may be .
(e) On or before the first day of October in each
fiscal year, to file with the Governor a complete
report of the financial transactions of the preceding
fiscal year and of the financial condition of the Com-
monwealth as of the end of that year , with such
comments , and supplementary data as he may deem

106 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
necessary to make his report complete and easy to
understand .
ARTICLE VIII-POST-AUDITING
§ 1. Periodic and Special Audits ; Filing of Audit
Reports . It shall be the duty of the State Inspector
and Examiner to audit the accounts , records and
transactions of the budget units , not less frequently
than once in each fiscal year . Such auditing shall
extend to private and semi -private agencies receiv-
ing State aid or having responsibility for handling
any State funds . Copies of the audit reports shall
be filed with the departments , institutions , boards ,
and other public offices and agencies audited , and a
copy of each report shall be filed with the Gover-
nor . He shall make special audits and investigations
at such times as may be required by the Governor ,
and shall audit periodically the revenue collections
of all kinds accruing to the Commonwealth , to ascer-
tain whether the revenue due and owing to the Com-
monwealth is being properly and completely col-
lected , and if he shall find from said audit or audits
that the collections are not being satisfactorily
made , he shall report that fact to the authority
whose duty it is to make such collections . All audit
reports of the examiners shall be public documents
to which taxpayers shall have access .
§ 2. Audits of the Accounts of the Auditor and
the Treasurer . The State Inspector and Examiner
shall audit the accounts of the Auditor of Public
Accounts and of the Treasurer not less frequently
than once each month .
ARTICLE IX- GENERAL PROVISIONS
1. Board of Finance to Investigate Alleged
Mismanagements . Powers and Duties of Board in

CHAPTER 25 107
Case of Mismanagements . The Board of Finance
is empowered , and it shall be its duty to investi-
gate any alleged mismanagement of any of the af-
fairs of the Commonwealth by any officer or em-
ployee or governing body responsible for the carry-
ing out of any State function or the management
of State funds ; to recommend to the Governor that
he proceed as provided by law for the removal of
any unnecessary officer or employee and of any of-
ficer or employee whom the Board shall find has
grossly mismanaged any State affairs .
$ 2 . Powers of State Budget Officer and Board
of Finance to Conduct Examinations and Compel At-
tendance and Testimony of Witnesses . For the pur-
pose of enforcing the provisions of this Act, the

who may be designated as his agent shall have free
access during business hours to all books , reports ,
documents , papers , accounts , and letters in the

cases .
$ 3. Penalty for Violating Law. Any officer ,
agent or employee of any budget unit who shall
willfully fail or refuse to comply with any of the pro-
visions of this Act or who shall so expend any money
in violation of any of the provisions of this Act ,
shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and be sub-
ject to indictment in the Franklin Circuit Court , and
upon conviction shall be fined in any sum of not less
than fifty ($50.00 ) dollars , nor more than Five
Hundred ( $500.00 ) Dollars , for each offense .
§ 4. Repeal of Inconsistent Provisions ; Consti-
tutionality of Act . It is hereby declared to be the
intention and purpose of the General Assembly, in
the passage of this Act , to create an adequate , uni-
fied , and integrated system of financial administra-
tion and business management for the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , to be used by all the agencies
of the Commonwealth to promote efficiency and
economy , in lieu of the various systems now au-
thorized by law, and therefore , all laws or parts
of laws in conflict with the provisions of this Act ,
are hereby [ repealed ] to the extent of such conflict .
§ 5. Construction of Law ; Effect of Partial In-
validity . This Act shall be liberally construed , and
if any section , paragraph , or provision thereof shall
be declared unconstitutional , the invalidity of such
section , paragraph or provision of this act shall not
affect the remaining sections , paragraphs or pro-

CHAPTER 26 109
visions of this act ; and it is declared to be the legis-
lative intent to enact each provision , paragraph , and
section of this act separately.
§ 6. The provisions of this Act shall be and be-
come effective at 12:00 o'clock midnight , June 30 ,
1934 .
Approved March 17 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 26.
AN ACT to amend and re-enact sections 3 , 5, 6, 8, 9 , 13 and 15 ,
of Chapter 155, of the Acts of 1920 , entitled "An Act to estab-
lish an Athletic Board of Control and prescribing the duties
and power of same and to license and regulate the giving or
holding of boxing or sparring contests or exhibitions in this
state, providing for the payment of a tax and license , and
prohibiting any betting or gaming at any such contest , and
providing of penalty for violation of this act," to reduce the
license fee and bond provided for therein , to extend and
modify the provisions thereof to include wrestling contests or
exhibitions and to provide for decisions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 1 , Chapter 155 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1920 , be and the same is
hereby repealed , amended and re- enacted so that
when said section is amended and re -enacted the
same shall read as follows :
State Athletic Board of Control ; Members ;
Terms ; Organization .- That there is hereby estab-
lished a State Athletic Board of Control . Within
thirty days after this act takes effect the Governor
shall appoint three persons to be members of such
board , who shall hold office for the terms of four
years following the date of their appointments . Pro-
vided , however , that no two members of said board
shall be residents of the same Congressional Dis-

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
trict. The Governor shall also appoint their suc-
cessors each successive fourth year . The board
shall maintain a general office for the transaction of
its business . The members of the board shall , at
their meeting after their appointment , elect one of
their number chairmen of the board , shall adopt the
seal for the board and may make such rules for the
administration of their office , not inconsistent here-
with , as they may deem expedient ; and they may
hereafter amend and abrogate such rules . Two of
the members of the board shall constitute a quorum
to do business ; and the concurrence of at least two
members of the board shall be necessary to render
a choice or decision by the board . Provided that the
next preceding provision shall not become effective
until the expiration of the terms of the present
board.
That section 3 , Chapter 155 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of 1920 , be and the same is
hereby repealed and re - enacted so that said section
when amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
" The board shall have and hereby is vested with
the sole direction , management and control of and
jurisdiction over , all boxing and sparring matches ,
wrestling matches and exhibitions to be conducted ,
held or given within the State by any person , club ,
corporation or association ; and no boxing or spar-
ring match , wrestling match , or exhibition shall be
conducted , held or given within the state except pur-
suant to its authority and in accordance with the
provisions of this act . The board may, in its dis-
cretion , issue , and at its pleasure revoke , a license
to conduct , hold or give boxing or sparring matches
wrestling matches , or exhibitions , to any person ,
club , corporation or association . Every license
shall be subject to such rules and regulations and

CHAPTER 26 111

Every application for a license , as herein provided
for , shall be in writing and shall be addressed to
the board and shall be verified by some officer of the
club , corporation or association on whose behalf
the application may be made . It shall contain a re-
cital of such facts , as under the provisions hereof ,
will show the applicant entitled to receive a license ,
and in addition thereto , such facts and recitals as
the board may by rule require to be shown . "
Provided , however, the Athletic Board of Control
shall issue without the payment of any tax or license
a permit to , hold boxing and sparring matches
wrestling matches or exhibitions , to any accredited
college , university , school or Young Men's Christian

116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 28 .
AN ACT to assist disabled ex-service men and women of the
World War and veterans of other wars in obtaining from the
Federal Government the relief authorized by Acts of Congress ,
and continuing in existence for that purpose the Kentucky
Disabled Ex -Service Men's Board and appropriating funds
therefor ; providing maintenance of an office and stenographic
help to carry on the work of said Board .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service
Men's Board , created by Act approved February
27 , 1922 , and recreated by Acts of the General As-
sembly approved March 21 , 1924 , February 24 , 1926,
February 16 , 1928 , March 11 , 1930 , and by an Act of
the General Assembly 1932 , be continued in exist-
ence for an additional period of two ( 2 ) years for
the purpose of assisting ex- service men and women
of the World War and their dependents , and vet-
erans of other wars and their dependents who are
residents of Kentucky , and who are or may be en-
titled to aid from the United States Government
pursuant to federal legislation for the benefit of vet-
erans and their dependents , in preparing and pre-
senting their said claims to the proper federal agen-
cies and authorities and in securing and expediting
action thereon ; to relieve and prevent congestion
and delay in the proper consideration , rating and
final adjudication of said claims and to , as far as
possible , cause every disabled veteran and the de-
pendents of every disabled veteran to receive
promptly and regularly the maximum relief and
benefits to which they are entitled under the federal
law .
§ 2. Said Kentucky Disabled Ex- Service Men's
Board shall, during the time for which its existence

CHAPTER 28 117
c
в
ce
I
I
0
is extended by Section 1 of this Act , be composed of
three ( 3 ) members : One of whom shall be the Lieu-
tenant Governor of Kentucky , exofficio , another of
whom shall be the State Commander of the Ameri-
can Legion , Department of Kentucky , exofficio , and
the third member shall be elected annually by these
two members . The members of the Board shall
serve without salary or compensation , but they shall
be paid their actual and necessary traveling ex-
penses while in the discharge of their official duties .
3. The words , " veterans " and " ex- service
men and women , " as used in this Act , include any
individual who served on active duty in the army ,
navy , or marine corps of the United States and who
has received an honorable discharge from such
service .
§ 4. Said Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's
Board shall elect a Secretary and such assistants as
may be found necessary whose duties shall be to pre-
pare in proper form , present and prosecute the
claims of disabled ex-service men and women and
their dependents with the United States Veterans '
Administration and other federal agencies , and to
secure and expedite action on said claims ; to keep
in contact with the United States Veterans ' Admin-
istration so that the status of any claims may be
known at any time ; to continue to survey the State
to make certain that no disabled man or his depend-
ents are neglected , and to perform such other duties
as the Board may direct . The secretary and such
assistants as may be authorized shall be paid a sal-
ary fixed by the Board and shall be paid actual and
necessary traveling expenses while engaged in the
performance of duties as the Board may direct .
§ 5. For the purpose of carrying out the provi-
sions of this Act , there is hereby appropriated out of

118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
State funds , not otherwise appropriated , to the Ken-
tucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's Board , the sum of
Twenty Thousand ( $ 20,000.00 ) Dollars per year for
two ( 2 ) years . The Board shall keep an accurate
and detailed account of all receipts and disburse-
ments , and its books shall be subject to examination
and audit by the State Inspector and Examiner .
Any part of the fund unused at the expiration of the
period for which the Board is created shall be
turned back to the general State fund .
§ 6. The Kentucky Disabled Ex- Service Men's
Board shall have the power to expend such reason-
able sums as may be necessary to employ clerical
and stenographic assistants , who shall be paid their
actual and necessary traveling expenses while en-

§ 8. All laws and parts of laws inconsistent here-
with are hereby repealed .

.
AN ACT authorizing and directing the Governor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky to sign for and on behalf of the Common-
wealth
aCompact entitled
"Compact
ofFair Competition for
the Prison Industries of the United States of America
"; re-
serving the right
towithdraw therefrom and authorizing and
impowering the Governor
ofthe Commonwealth
toact for and
on behalf of the Commonwealth and to do each and every-
thing necessary and requisite
tocarry out the terms
ofsaid
Compact
; declaring
anemergency
toexist and repealing all
conflicting laws
.
Be
itenacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth
ofKentucky
:
That the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky be and
he
ishereby authorized and direct-
ed
tosign for and
onbehalf
ofthe Commonwealth
ofKentucky
, subject
tothe limitations hereinafter
stated
,
"Compact
ofFair Competition for thePrison Industries
ofthe United States of Amer-

126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ica," which said Compact is in words and figures as
follows :
COMPACT OF FAIR COMPETITION FOR THE PRISON IN-
DUSTRIES OF THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
THIS AGREEMENT made and entered into jointly
and severally by and between the several States sig-
natory hereto , as of the date of execution of each of
said several states .
WITNESSETH that whereas , under date of October
21 , 1933 , the President of the United States ap-
proved the Code of Fair Competition for the Retail
Trade under the National Industrial Recovery Act,
which said Code of Fair Competition contained ,
among other things , the following :
"Pending the formulation of a compact or code
between the several states of the United States to
insure the manufacture and sale of prison -made
goods on a fair competitive basis with goods not so
produced , the following provisions of this Section
will be stayed for ninety ( 90 ) days , or further at the
discretion of the Administrator .
( 1 ) Where any penal , reformatory or correc-
tional institution , either by subscribing to the code.
or compact hereinbefore referred to , or by a binding
agreement of any other nature , satisfies the Admin-
istrator that merchandise produced in such institu-
tion or by the inmates thereof will not be sold except
upon a fair competitive basis with similar merchan-
dise not so produced , the provisions of paragraph
( 2 ) hereof shall not apply to any merchandise pro-
duced in such manner in the institutions covered by
such agreement .
( 2 ) Except as provided in the foregoing para-
graph , no retailer shall knowingly buy or contract
to buy any merchandise produced in whole or in

CHAPTER 31 127
t
e
1
2
part in a penal , reformatory or correctional institu-
tion . After May 31 , 1934 , no retailer shall know-
ingly sell or offer for sale such merchandise . Noth-
ing in this Section , however , shall affect contracts ,
which the retailer does not have the option to can-
cel , made with respect to such merchandise before
the approval of this Compact by the President of
the United States . '" "
WHEREAS , prior to the presentation of the above
described Retail Code to the President of the United
States for his approval the several States of the
United States had begun to formulate a Compact
of Fair Competition for said prison industries , and
WHEREAS , it is the desire of the signatory States
to continue the use of the most important instrumen-
tality for the rehabilitation of prisoners — namely ,
productive labor-and to achieve this purpose in
a manner which will comply with the fair methods.
of competition desired by modern commerce and
particularly in accordance with the National Indus-
trial Recovery Act , and whereas these states desire
to apply to the prison labor problem a system of
rules , uniform in principle and equitable to all , and
WHEREAS , it is equally the desire of the States
signatory hereto that the provisions hereof estab-
lishing uniform principles will facilitate the main-
tenance of fair competition in American industry
and will aid in effectuating the administration of
the National Industrial Recovery Act .
Now, THEREFORE , for and in consideration of the
premises and of the mutual benefits to be derived.
herefrom the states signatory hereto , jointly and
severally agree as follows :
ARTICLE I
As used herein the following terms have the mean-
ing as shown unless the contrary appears :

128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The term "penal or correctional institution "
shall include any prison , penitentiary , reformatory ,
workhouse , jail or other institution wholly tax- sup-
ported and maintained for the incarceration or im-
prisonment of persons convicted of an offense
against the laws of the United States , any state or
its political subdivisions, but shall not include insti-
tutions maintained under the jurisdiction of private
charitable or religious organizations .
The term " prison industry " as used herein shall
mean any industry or trade in whatever manner op-
erated for the mining , manufacture , production
and/or distribution of products , in which are em-
ployed inmates of penal or correctional institutions
or other persons who at the time of employment
are serving a sentence imposed by a court of the
United States or any state : Provided , that no agri-
cultural activities not a part of any trade or indus-
try shall be considered a prison industry as herein
defined .
"Products " shall mean all goods , wares , merchan-
dise and minerals manufactured , produced or mined
in whole or in part by prison industry for other than
State use .
ARTICLE II
Hours of Labor . The hours of labor in prison in-
dustries shall be not more than those prescribed by
the applicable Code adopted under the laws of the
United States governing each particular industry ,
but in no case shall any inmate be required or per-
mitted to work in any prison industry more than
forty hours in any one week .
ARTICLE III
The hours of operation of productive machinery
shall be not more than is prescribed in the code of
the competing private domestic industry .

CHAPTER 31 129
ARTICLE IV
Child Labor. No person under the age of sixteen
years shall be employed in any prison industry . No
person under the age of eighteen years shall be em-
ployed at operations or occupations which are haz-
ardous in nature or dangerous to health .
ARTICLE V
Section A. Prison products , when sold by the
prison or through a contractor, whether sold direct
or through any agency , shall be sold not lower than
the fair current price prevailing in the market in
which the product is customarily sold to whole-
salers , retailers , or consumers as the case may be .
Section B. No penal or correctional institution or
agency thereof shall enter into any contract for the
labor of prisoners which does not insure a return
from the contractor to the state or its political sub-
division and/or the prisoner of an amount equal in
value to the cost per unit of product for labor and
overhead necessarily paid in competing domestic
private industry on the comparable product : Pro-
vided , that the imposition or collection of such
amounts or charges shall not be such as will require
the sale of prison products at a higher price than
specified in Section A hereof.
ARTICLE VI
State Use Products and Public Works : The re-
strictions in this compact shall not apply to goods ,
wares or merchandise manufactured , produced or
mined by any penal or correctional institution which
are solely for the use of tax- supported institutions ,
agencies , departments , or activities of any state or
its political sub-division , nor shall they apply to the
Acts-5

130 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
construction of public works or ways financed
wholly from funds of the state or its political sub-
division ,
ARTICLE VII
Further to effectuate the policies of this compact ,
a committee ( hereinafter called the Prison Labor
Authority ) is hereby constituted to cooperate with
prison industry , private industry , the several states
signatory hereto , and the United States in the ad-
ministration of this Compact .
( 1 ) Organization and Constitution of the Prison
Labor Authority : The Prison Labor Authority
shall consist of nine members , six ( 6 ) of whom shall
be elected annually by representatives of the states
signatory hereto , and three (3 ) to be appointed by
the President to represent labor , industry and con-
sumers , respectively .
( 2 ) The Prison Labor Authority shall have the
following duties and powers , subject to the right of
the President , on review to disapprove or modify
any action taken by the Prison Labor Authority
( The President may delegate any of the functions
and powers under this compact to such officers ,
agents and employees as he may designate or ap-
point ) :
(a) Generally to administer this Compact .
(b) To make rules and regulations for the con-
duct of business by the Prison Labor Authority , to
appoint a secretary and/or any personnel and gen-
erally to prescribe such rules , orders and regula-
tions as may be necessary .
( c) To formulate uniform cost -finding systems
and require the installation of such system or some
system comparable therewith .

CHAPTER 31 131
(d) To determine , after conferring with the
Code Authority of the industry affected and upon
request of any person or firm affected , the prices ,
charges and amounts provided for in Article V , Sec-
tions A and B hereof ; such determination to be sub-
ject to appeal to the President of the United States .
In establishing fair current prices , charges and
amounts , the Prison Labor Authority shall take into
consideration all factors necessary to insure the
marketing of prison products on a fair competitive
basis . It may consider , among other factors , the
extent to which monopolistic practices exist in any
trade or industry in which the prison industry op-
erates , the degree to which prison products may be
discriminated against , the ability of the prison in-
dustry to adjust its operations and production to
meet changing styles, designs , and/or other condi-
tions beyond its control , and any restrictions placed
upon the marketing of prison products . The fair
current price may not be such as will effectually pre-
vent the sale of prison products or destroy existing
markets nor shall it permit the sale of prison prod-
ucts at such prices or in such manner or in such
quantities as will depress the standards , wages or
working conditions of the competing private indus-
try or defeat the purposes sought to be obtained
through adherence by a competing private industry
to a code of fair competition under the National In-
dustrial Recovery Act .
(e) To require such reports and statistics from
States signatory hereto as may be necessary to ef-
fectuate the policies of this Compact .
(f) After an investigation , which shall include
a consideration of economic , trade, and market con-
ditions , and under such rules as it may prescribe :
(1) formulate such regulations as may be consistent

132 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
with statutory provisions and as may be necessary
to assure a diversification of the output of prison
industries in fair proportion to the production of
the industries affected , ( 2 ) prohibit the expansion
of any existing prison industry which bears a dis-
proportionate share of competition .
(g) To hear and adjust complaints arising un-
der this Compact made by prison administrators or
prison heads , and call to the attention of the Presi-
dent any unfair trade practices designed to discrim-
inate against prison made goods or hamper the ob-
jective sought in the preamble of this Compact
ARTICLE VIII
Reports : All states signatory hereto agree to
furnish reports to the National Recovery Adminis-
tration covering data on prison industries which the
laws of the United States require from competing
private industries under the National Recovery Act.
ARTICLE IX
Authority for Execution : The representatives of
the States signatory hereto being in some instances
restricted in their action by statutes which cannot
immedately be modified , enter into this agreement
in their own right and subscribe to it in so far as
the laws and regulations of the sovereign states
thay represent will permit , and they further pledge
themselves to cooperate to secure the adoption of
such changes in the laws and regulations by the
properly constituted authorities as may be neces-
sary to permit them to adhere fully to this compact ,
but nothing in this compact shall affect one way or
another any Federal Act relating to prison -made
products or any State Act passed or effective under
any such Federal Act , if said Acts are held constitu-

CHAPTER 31 1.33
tional and valid . It is specifically understood that
by signing this Compact no signatory hereto is
thereby estopped from asserting the unconstitution-
ality or invalidity of any of said Acts .
ARTICLE X
Due to the impracticability of promptly obtaining
the execution by all of the signatory states on one
document , it is agreed that the signatory states
hereto may execute identical copies of this Compact
which execution shall be as effective as though all
signatory states executed the same document .
ARTICLE XI
The effective date of this Compact shall be Janu-
ary 1 , 1934, as to all states which have signed at
that time and as to all states signing thereafter it
shall take effect as of the date of such signing .
APPROVAL
1934 .
The foregoing Compact of Fair competition FOR
PRISON INDUSTRIES is hereby approved for and on
behalf of the State of
by
its legally constituted authority .
APPROVED By :
Title
Any authority given in this Act is given with the
understanding and reservation that the Governor of
this Commonwealth , or his successor in office , or the
General Assembly may , at any time , and after due

134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
notice in writing to the parties and signatories to
said Compact withdraw therefrom .
The Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
is hereby authorized , empowered and directed to do
each and everything necessary and requisite to
carry out the terms , agreements , stipulations or re-
quirements of the Compacts of Fair Competition
for the Prison Industries of the United States of
America for and on behalf of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
WHEREAS , there is no authority existing in any of-
ficial of this Commonwealth to sign for and bind the
Commonwealth to said above Compact , and
WHEREAS , until the State of Kentucky becomes a
party to said Compact , it is unable to operate its
prison shops and factories or to lease its prisoners ,
and
WHEREAS , due to the idleness of said prisoners the
Commonwealth of Kentucky is losing approximately
$1,000 a day in revenue , and
WHEREAS , said prisoners while idle and unem-
ployed are dangerous and difficult to control, an
emergency is hereby declared to exist and this Act
shall be effective on and after its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor.
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of said conflict .
Approved February 9, 1934 .
CHAPTER 32 .
AN ACT to appropriate eight hundred dollars ( $ 800.00 ) each per
annum for the care of each of the five living Confederate
Veterans confined in the Confederate Home at Pewee Valley ,
Kentucky , from the State Treasury ; providing for a Board to
administer same ; authorizing the Board to contract for the
care and maintenance of said Confederate Veterans .

CHAPTER 32 135
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That whereas there are now living five Con-
federate Veterans who are inmates of the Confed-
crate Home at Pewee Valley , Kentucky , and in
order to care for and maintain each of the five liv-
ing Confederate Veterans , there is hereby appro-
priated from the State Treasury out of funds not
otherwise appropriated the sum of eight hundred
dollars ($800.00 ) per annum for the care and main-
tenance of each of said living Confederate Veterans .
In the event of the death of any one or more of said
five living Confederate Veterans during a fiscal
year, the remaining unused portion of the said eight
hundred dollars shall not be drawn from the State
Treasury ; and upon the death of all five of said liv-
ing Confederate Veterans who are now confined
at the Confederate Home, this appropriation shall
cease .
§ 2. There is hereby created a Board composed
of three persons who are members of the Daughters
of the Confederacy who shall be appointed for a
term of four years by the Governor of the Common-
wealth and who shall serve without compensation
and whose duty it shall be to supervise the care ,
treatment and maintenance of the five living Con-
federate Veterans now living at the Confederate
Home, and to make and enter into contracts with
proper parties for the care and treatment of said
Confederate Veterans . Said contracts for such pur-
poses shall be made with the approval of the State
Inspector and Examiner . It shall be the duty of
said Board to present itemized claims for the ex-
penditure of said appropriation to the Auditor of
Public Accounts showing the amount of the claim,
to whom payable and the name of the Confederate

136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Veteran for whom the expenditure is made , and
when audited by the Auditor he shall draw his war-
rant upon the State Treasury therefor .
§ 3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
§ 4. Whereas , it is the purpose and intention of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky to sell, dispose of
and convey the Confederate Home at Pewee Valley ,
Kentucky , and the equipment connected therewith ,
and when done the five living Confederate Veterans
who are confined at said Home will be without care ,
attention or comfort, an emergency is declared to
exist and this act shall become effective from and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 17 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 33.
AN ACT to appropriate one hundred dollars per annum from the
State Treasury for the care and upkeep of the Confederate
Cemetery at Pewee Valley , Kentucky , and to provide for its
expenditure .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

State Treasury the sum of one hundred dollars
($100.00 ) per annum out of funds not otherwise ap-
propriated for the care and upkeep of the Con-
federate Cemetery at Pewee Valley , Kentucky .

at the direction of a Board composed of three mem-
bers of the Daughters of the Confederacy , who shall
be appointed for a four-year term by the Governor
of the Commonwealth .
§ 3. All bills when created by said Board shall
be authorized and signed by the members of said

CHAPTER 34 137
Roard, who shall present same to the Auditor of

rect, the said Auditor shall draw his warrant upon
the State Treasurer in payment of said claim.

draw his warrant and the Treasurer his check for
payment of such claim against and from said sum
when same is set apart by the State Budget Act,
and not until so done,
§ 5. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Approved March 19 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 35.
AN ACT adopting as the law of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
the edition of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes compiled and edited
by William Edward Baldwin and Richard Priest Dietzman ,
issued in the year 1930 , and the Supplements thereto compiled
and edited by William Edward Baldwin and Richard Priest
Dietzman , issued in 1930 , 1932 and 1933 , and the Supplement
thereto compiled and edited by William Edward Baldwin and
Richard Priest Dietzman , issued in the year 1933 and known
as Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes Service .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the edition of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes compiled and edited by William Edward
Baldwin and Richard Priest Deitzman , issued in
the year 1930 , and the Supplements thereto compiled
and edited by William Edward Baldwin and Rich-
ard Priest Deitzman , issued in the years 1930 , 1932
and 1933 , and the Supplement thereto compiled and
edited by William Edward Baldwin and Richard
Priest Dietzman , issued in the year 1933 and known
as Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes Service , be and the
same are hereby adopted as the law of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , and in all actions and pro-

CHAPTER 36 141
ceedings the laws contianed therein from §1 to §4987 ,
inclusive , including amendatory or supplemental
sections may be cited or referred to as the law of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky by reference to any
citation of said sections , and any of the sections
therein may be amended or repealed by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the
manner now provided by referring to and citation
of the section of said statutes without giving the
date or title of the act from which the section is
taken .
§ 2. Whereas this act will save a great deal of
labor and time in the investigation and enactment
of laws, and will prevent confusion and uncertainty ,
an emergency is declared to exist , and this shall take
effect and be in force from and after its approval
by the Governor .
§ 3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict with this
act are hereby repealed .
Approved February 8, 1934 .
CHAPTER 36 .
AN ACT to amend Section 965 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930
Edition , as amended by Chapter 28 of the General Assembly
of 1930, and changing the time of holding court in the Thirty-
first Judicial District composed of Floyd and Knott counties ,
and fixing the time therefor , relating to circuit court and de-
claring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 965 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , as amended by Chapter 28
of the General Assembly of 1930 , be and the same
is amended with reference to the Thirty -first Ju-
dicial District of Kentucky , and such amendment
shall read as follows :

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
That a circuit court shall be held in Floyd County,
as follows :
At Prestonsburg , Kentucky , beginning on the first
Monday in January and continuing twenty- four
( 24 ) juridical days ; beginning on the first Monday
in February and continuing twenty -four ( 24 ) jurid-
ical days ; beginning on the first Monday in April
and continuing thirty -six (36 ) juridical days ; be-
ginning on the first Monday in September and con-
tinuing twenty-four ( 24 ) juridical days ; and begin-
ning on the first Monday in October and continuing
twenty-four ( 24 ) juridical days .
That a circuit court shall be held in Knott County ,
as follows :
At Hindman , Kentucky , beginning on the first
Monday in March and continuing twenty -four ( 24 )
juridical days ; beginning on the second Monday in
July and continuing eighteen ( 18 ) juridical days ;
and beginning on the second Monday in November
and continuing twenty -four ( 24 ) juridical days .
§ 2. Owing to the congested condition of the
docket in said Thirty-first Judicial District , and the

will become effective upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .

with are repealed .

CHAPTER 38 .
AN ACT providing for verdicts of juries in civil cases in inferior
courts by less than unanimous verdict.

144 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
be-§ 1. That , in courts in this Commonwealth ,
low the circuit courts , in cases in which trials are
had by jury of six persons , a verdict , in civil cases
may be rendered by Five (5) or more of the jury
agreeing ; and , in cases in which trial is had by
twelve jurors, a verdict may be returned by nine or
more of the jury agreeing . When less than all agree ,

hibited class .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 39 .
AN ACT to create a committee of four, one to be appointed by
the Speaker of the House, one to be appointed by the Lieu-
tenant Governor , one to be appointed by the Chief Justice of
the Court of Appeals , and one to be appointed by the President
of the State Bar Association , to authorize the publishers of
Carroll's 1930 Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , to revise
the Statutes of Kentucky , and to report to the next session of
the General Assembly , and making an appropriation to defray
the expenses thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That a committee of four is hereby created for
the purpose and with the powers hereinafter de-
fined . One of said committee shall be appointed by
the Speaker of the House of Representatives , one

CHAPTER 39 145
shall be appointed by the Lieutenant Governor , one
shall be appointed by the Chief Justice of the Court
of Appeals , and one shall be appointed by the Presi-
dent of the Kentucky State Bar Association .
Vacancies in the committee shall be filled by the
same appointing power authorized to make the
original appointments .
The said Committee shall be called a Committec
on Revision of the Statutes of Kentucky , and its
members shall serve without pay, but shall be al-
lowed and paid the actual expenses incurred . The
members ' actual expenses shall be paid out of the
appropriation hereinafter made upon vouchers ap-
proved by the committee .
Said committee is hereby authorized and em
powered to authorize the publishers of Carroll's
1930 Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , to re-
vise the Statutes of Kentucky , without pay.
It shall be the duty of the publishers of Carroll's
1930 Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , to
formulate, prepare and execute a definite plan for
the revision , classification , arrangement , printing ,
annotating and binding of the Statutes of a general
nature and of the session laws , and to prepare and
present to the proper committee of each house such
hills as may be required to harmonize and perfect
the statutes , including revisions , repeals and other
changes necessary to effect the purpose of clarify-
ing, simplifying and codifying the laws of the State .
Said committee shall make a report to the next
session of the General Assembly of the work done
by it, and present a completed copy of the publish-
ers ' official revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
The sum of $ 500.00 is hereby appropriated and
made available out of any funds not otherwise ap-
propriated to pay the expenses of said committee .

146 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
This act shall be in full force and effect from and
after its passage and approval as required by law .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 40 .
AN ACT to provide to each Division of the Chancery Branch of
the Circuit Courts in counties having a population of 200,000
or more , comprising a separate Judicial District and having
six judges , or more , a special clerical assistant to the respec-
tive judges of said respective Chancery Divisions of said Court
in the performance of their judicial work ; fixing the com-
pensation of such clerical assistant and directing the same to
be paid out of the county treasury of such county and direct-
ing the method and manner by which the same shall be certi-
fied by the said Court to the Fiscal Court of said county for
payment .
WHEREAS , the judicial duties and functions of the Judges of
the several Divisions of the Chancery Branch of Circuit Courts
in counties having a population of 200,000 inhabitants , or more ,
comprising a separate Judicial District , and having six Circuit
Judges , or more , and the amount and nature of litigation in
said Divisions of said Chancery Branch of said courts are such
as to require the services of a clerical assistant to perform
stenographic and other clerical assistance to each of said re-
spective Judges of said Branch of said Court , in the perform-
ance of their judicial duties and functions and is necessary to.
enable said Chancellors to perform their judicial duties and
functions with the dispatch and in the manner required by
the public interest and the interests of the litigants in said
Court ; Therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Cirenit Courts in counties having a popu-
lation of 200,000 , or more , comprising a separate
Judicial District , and having six Judges , or more ,
may, by order in General Term , any four of said
Judges concurring, appoint a clerical assistant to
each of the Judges of the respective Divisions of

CHAPTER 41 147
the Chancery Branch of said Court , whose duties
shall be to perform such stenographic and other
clerical services for said respective Judges thereof
as may be assigned or directed by said Judges , in
the performance of their judicial duties or func-
tions ; and said Court shall, by order in General
Term , any four of said Judges concurring , fix the
compensation or salary of each of said clerical as-
sistants at not exceeding $1,500.00 per annum , to be
paid monthly out of the County treasury of said
county .
When an attested copy of said order of the Gen-
eral Term is presented to the Fiscal Court of said
county , the said Fiscal Court of such county shall
immediately enter an order directing the treasurer
to and the treasurer shall pay out of the funds of
said county to said clerical assistant , in equal month-
ly installments , the compensation so fixed by the
said circuit judges in General Term .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 42 .
AN ACT to authorize the Judges of the Circuit Courts to post-
pone rendition of judgment , to probate the offenders , and to
make rules and regulations for the arrest of the defendants
and enforcement of judgment rendered in certain criminal
cases .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. What prisoners may be put upon probation .
In prosecutions for crime , in the Circuit Courts of
this state , except as hereinafter provided , where
the defendant has pleaded or been found guilty , and
the defendant has never before been convicted of a
felony , either in this state or elsewhere , and it ap-

CHAPTER 42 149
pears to the satisfaction of the court that the char-
acter of the defendant and circumstances of the case
are such that he is not likely again to engage in an
offensive course of conduct , and that the public
good does not demand or require that he shall suffer
the penalty imposed by law, such court may post-
pone the rendition of judgment on such terms and
conditions as the court deems proper . Whenever
in the opinion of the court the sentence should no
longer be postponed , the court shall have power to
cause a warrant to issue for the defendant and he
may thereupon on his arrest be sentenced and such
sentence carried into immediate execution .
§ 2. Juvenile Delinquents . This act shall not
affect the laws providing the method of dealing with
juvenile delinquents , nor shall detention in the in-
stitution for such juvenile delinquents be considered
an imprisonment .
§ 3. Exceptions Thereto . No person convicted
of murder , arson , burglary of an inhabited dwelling
house , incest , sodomy , rape without consent , assault
with intent to rape , robbery, or assault with intent
to rob or embezzlement , shall have the benefit of
probation
COMMITTEE AMENDMENT .
§ 4. A judgment of probation under the provi-
sions of this act shall be subject to review by the
Court of Appeals . The Commonwealth's Attorney
may except to any judgment of probation under the
provisions of this act , without executing bond , and
prosecute and appeal therefrom , in the name of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky to the Court of Ap-
peals .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 44 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 2525 of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , prescribing the time within
which persons under a disability must bring actions other
than those for the recovery of real estate by excluding from
the category of such persons married women , and prescribing
the effective date of such amendment .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 2525 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 Edition , be amended by striking there-
from the words " married woman " and that said
section as thus amended be re -enacted to read as
follows :
"If a person entitled to bring any of the actions

for a penalty or forfeiture , was , at the time the
cause of action accrued , an infant , or of unsound
mind, the action may be brought within the like num-
ber of years after the removal of such disability or

is allowed to a person having no such impediment to
bring the same after the right accrued . ”

CHAPTER 46 151

AN ACT to amend Section 2506 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 , Edition , prescribing the time within which persons under

cluding married women from the category of such persons
and to re -enact the same as thus amended , and prescribing the

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the

Statutes , 1930 Edition , be amended by striking there-
From the words " married woman " and that said

CHAPTER 46 .
AN ACT to repeal and re-enact Section 794 of Baldwin's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to shooting or throwing
at or into railroad engines , cars , stations , waiting rooms ,
automobiles or motor vehicles , etc.

152 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 50 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , as
amended and re - enacted at the 1924 session , being
Section 794 of Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed and re -enacted , so that as re-enacted said
law shall read as follows :
"Any person who shall recklessly throw any
stone , stick , club or other missle at or into , or shoot
at or in to any engine of any railroad train in this
state , or any car attached thereto , or any station ,
station platform or waiting room , on or in which
engine , car , station , station platform or waiting
room where there may be any passenger or other
person , or at or into any automobile or other motor
vehicle , stationary or traveling upon any public
highway in this Commonwealth , shall be guilty of a
misdemeanor , and upon conviction thereof shall be
fined not less than one hundred nor more than five
hundred dollars , or imprisoned in the county jail
for not less than six months nor more than twelve ;
and if such stick , stone , club or missle so thrown
or shot so fired was calculated to produce death or
great bodily harm, and any person or passenger on
or in said engine , car attached thereto , station , sta-
tion platform or waiting room , automobile or motor
vehicle , shall be injured or wounded , said person
so throwing same or shooting shall be deemed guilty
of a felony and upon conviction thereof shall be im-
prisoned in the penitentiary not less than one or
more than five years ; and , should death ensue from
such throwing or shooting within one year there-
after , the person guilty of same as herein provided
shall be deemed guilty of murder .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

States of America or tokens or tickets authorized
or sold or issued by the owner or lessee or licensee
of such machine , box , receptacle or appliance in the
furtherance of or in connection with the sale , use
or enjoyment of property or services or facilities of
communication or transportation , shall use any
token , ticket, slug , simulated coin or blank or device
or substance not authorized or issued or sold by the
owner or lessee or licensee of such lawful machines .
box , receptacle or appliance , shall be guilty of the
fraudulent use thereof. " Fraudulent " manufacture
or sale or gift or use within the meaning of this act
shall be the manufacture or sale or use by any per-
son who knows or has reasonable cause to believe
that the same will violate the provisions or spirit
of this act .
2. Any person violating this Act or any por-
tion thereof shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction thereof shall be punished by
imprisonment for not more than ninety days or by a
fine of not more than one hundred dollars , or both
such fine and imprisonment , at the discretion of the
court or the jury trying the case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 49.
AN ACT to amend and re-enact section one thousand three hun-
dred twenty -one , of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand
nine hundred thirty edition , and repealing all laws and parts
of laws in conflict with this act .

156 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section one thousand three hundred
twenty-one, Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , One
thousand nine hundred thirty edition , be and the
same is amended and re - enacted and as amended
and re-enacted shall read as follows :
No work or business shall be done on the Sab-
bath day , except the ordinary household offices , or
other work of necessity or charity , or work required
in the maintenance or operation of a ferry , skiff ,
steamboat or steam or street railroad , telegraph and
telephone companies ; public service or public utility
plants or systems , or taxi . If any person on the
Sabbath day shall himself be found at his own , or
at any other trade or calling or shall employ his
apprentices , or other person , in labor or other busi-
ness , whether the same be for profit or amusement ,
unless such as is permitted above , he shall be fined
not less than two nor more than fifty dollars for
each offense . Every person or apprentice so em-
ployed shall be deemed a separate offense . Persons
who are members of a religious society who observe
as a Sabbath any other day in the week than Sun-
day, shall not be liable to the penalty prescribed in
this section , if they observe as a Sabbath one day in
each seven , as herein provided . Provided that ama-
teur sports , athletic games , operation of moving pic-
ture show , chautauqua , filling stations , or opera shall
not be construed a work , labor , trade, business or
calling within the meaning of this section .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
this act are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 51 157
CHAPTER 50.
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Section 1160 , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's edition 1930 , relating to assault with intent to
rob , and fixing a penalty therefor.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 1160 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
edition 1930 , be amended and re-enacted so that
when so amended and re -enacted same shall read as
follows :
If any person , with an offensive weapon or instru-
ment , shall unlawfully and maliciously assault , or
in or by any forcible and violent manner , demand
any money , goods or chattels , bond , bill , deed or
will, or other evidences of right , or other thing of
value of or from any other person , with an intent
to rob or commit a robbery upon such person , he
shall be guilty of a felony and upon conviction shall
be punished by confinement in the State Penitenti-
ary for twenty - one years or for life, or by death , in
the discretion of the jury .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 51 .
AN ACT to provide for a more adequate enforcement of all
laws ; especially those relating to bank robbery , banditry,
kidnapping, and similar offenses , also those relating to viola-
tions of traffic and motor vehicle regulations , and authorizing
for that purpose the creation and organization of an Active
Militia ; repealing and re- enacting Section 2711a -145 , Kentucky
Statutes , making the Active Militia a division of the Military
Department ; providing for an Executive Officer thereof, and
for an Executive Council and defining their powers and duties ;
defining the powers , duties and limitation of the officers and
members of the Active Militia ; providing for the organization ,
discipline , qualifications , conduct , control , and the equipment

158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of same ; providing for the payment of necessary salaries and
the payment of all other necessary expense incident to the
organization administration and proper maintenance of the
Active Militia out of any Budget appropriation made for the
present or any future fiscal year for the use of the Military
Department or the Adjutant General .
WHEREAS , a wave of banditry and crime has , for the past few
years been sweeping the country and organized bands of gun-
man have been terrorizing communities , murdering , pillaging,
kidnapping , robbing banks , and committing other outrageous
crimes , a daring bank robbery having been perpetrated in
broad daylight, under the very shadow of our State Capitol ,
and other similar robberies , having occurred in other cities
and towns in the Commonwealth , and
WHEREAS , our highways have become insecure and unsafe for
travel by reason of the recklessness of drivers , many of whom
have little regard for human life , and
WHEREAS , there are numerous other traffic and motor vehicle
violations of the law , the perpetrators of which cannot be
brought to justice , because of the inadequate number of peace
officers in the Commonwealth , and because of the fact that
regular peace officers cannot legally pursue or arrest the of-
fenders , guilty of misdemeanors beyond the limits of their
county , and
WHEREAS , an additional number of peace officers is urgently
required to enforce the laws hereinbefore set out , therefore :
Be it enacted by the Gencral Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. ACTIVE MILITIA AUTHORIZED :
Section 2711a - 145 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 Edition , 1932 Supplement , being Chapter 95 ,
Section 4 of the Acts of 1932 is hereby repealed and
re-enacted and said Section when re - enacted shall
read :
" The militia of the State shall consist of all able-
bodied males , who have or shall have declared their
intention to become citizens of the United States , be-
tween the ages of 18 and 45 , who are residents of the
State , and of such other persons as may upon their

CHAPTER 51 159
own application be enlisted or commissioned there .
in pursuant to the provisions of the law , subject
however , to such exemptions as are now or may be
hereafter created by the laws of the United States .
The militia of this State shall be divided into three
classes :
(a ) Part of the organized militia to be known
as the " Kentucky National Guard . "
(b ) Another part of the organized militia to be
known as the " Active Militia . " "
(c ) The reserve militia , consisting of all other
persons liable to military service .
The Governor shall be the Commander -in - Chief
of all three classes of the Militia .
2. GOVERNOR TO ORGANIZE ACTIVE MILITIA :
The Governor of the Commonwealth shall , and
he is hereby authorized to organize the Active
Militia in each County of the State , to consist of not
less than one nor more than five such militiamen for
each thousand of population , which number shall
upon order of the Governor , be apportioned to each
County . The Governor shall appoint and com-
mission one sober , discreet and qualified person , not
less than thirty years of age , in each County in the
State , to be the Senior Officer of the Active Militia
therein . The rank of the officer so appointed , to be
governed by the number of Active Militiamen ap-
portioned to the County.
§ 3. SELECTION OF PERSONNEL :
The officer in each County chosen and commis-
sioned by the Governor , together with the County
Judge , and the Sheriff shall compose a Board to
select the personnel for the unit or units of the
Active Militia therein. No person shall be accepted
as a member of the Active Militia who shall not have

160 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
received the written approval of at least two of the
three named officers . Such persons , after being ac-
cepted and sworn in as members of the Active
Militia , shall also be immediately discharged from
the service by the Adjutant General , upon the writ-
ten request of any two of the above named officials .
§ 4. QUALIFICATIONS :
No applicant for membership in the Active Militia
shall be appointed or sworn in , either as an officer
or an enlisted man , unless he be a citizen of the
State of Kentucky , and of good moral character , be-
tween the ages of 21 and 64 , and in good physical
condition.
$ 5. COMMISSIONS FOR OFFICERS AND ENLISTMENTS
AND DISCHARGES FOR ENLISTED MEN :
All officers shall be appointed and commissioned
by the Governor . All men shall be enlisted by the
Company Commanders , and will be discharged upon
the order of the Governor , for the same causes and
reasons as men are discharged from the National
Guard , or when the discharge is requested in writ
ing by two members of the County Board . Enlist-
ments shall be for a period of two years.
§ 6. OATH OF COMMISSIONED OFFICERS AND EN-
LISTED MEN :
All officers and enlisted men of the Active Militia,
in addition to taking the oath similar to that re-
quired to be taken by commissioned officers of the
National Guard , and/or enlisted men of the National
Guard , shall subscribe to the oath of office prescribed
by the Constitution of Kentucky , to be taken by
other peace officers of the Commonwealth and such
oaths when duly subscribed to and taken shall be
filed with and preserved by the Adjutant General
of Kentucky . The oath shall be administered to the

CHAPTER 51 161
Senior Officer in each County by the County Judge ,
County Clerk, or a Notary Public . Such Senior Of-
ficer may then administer oaths of enlistment and
thereafter Commanding Officers of units are author-
ized and empowered to administer the oaths of en-
listment and other oaths necessary in the military
service . The Adjutant General shall have printed
forms or oaths for officers and enlisted men , which
forms shall first be approved by the Executive Coun-
cil, and he shall
inaddition
tothese
, supply unit
commanders with all other forms for enlistment
, dis-
charge and other necessary administrative require-
rients
.

oforganization
