77. Treasurer
.-The State Treasurer shall be
the treasurer of the State Board of Education in its

CHAPTER 65 347
administration of the two colleges for colored per-
sons .
78. Meetings at institutions .- The State Board
of Education shall meet at least once each year at
the school plant of each of the colleges herewith
placed under its management and control , for the
purpose of inspecting the plant and observing the
work and the needs of the school .
79. Funds not to be diverted.-All appropria-
tions made by the General Assembly for the sup-
port of the said colleges for colored persons or for
the benefit thereof , and all grants , bequests , or
donations by any individual or corporation for spec-
ified use shall be applied to such use or uses and
no other .
80. Administration to be consistent with re-
mainder of act . In respects other than those spe-
cifically mentioned herein , the State Board of Edu-
cation , in its administration of the said colleges for
colored persons , shall be guided by existing legisla-
tion respecting its organization , duties , powers , and
activities.
Construction of Act
1. Construction of foregoing sections ; act sever-
able. The provisions of this act are severable , and
if any of its provisions shall be held unconstitu-
tional the decisions of the courts shall not affect
or impair any of the remaining provisions of this
act. It is hereby declared as a legislative intent
that this act would have been adopted had such un-
constitutional provisions not be included therein ,
and any school service prohibited or impaired by
such provisions shall be administered by the laws.
in effect at the time this act became operative .
Approved March 14 , 1934 .

348 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 66 .
AN ACT providing educational opportunities for the Orphans
of Soldiers , Sailors , and Marines who were killed in action or
died during the World War , and appropriating necessary
money therefor from General Fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The sum appropriated under the provisions
of the act shall be used for the sole purpose of pro-
viding for matriculation fees , board and room rent
and books and supplies for the use and benefit of the
children not under sixteen and not over twenty-two
years of age ; and who have for twelve months had
their domicile in the State of Kentucky , of those
World War Veterans who were killed in action or
died from other causes in the World War , from
April 6 , 1917 , to July 2 , 1921 , while serving in the
army, navy or marine corps of the United States
as a result of such service ; which orphans are at-
tending or may attend a state educational institu-
tion of a secondary or college grade . Said children
shall be admitted to state institutions of secondary
or college grade free of tuition .
§ 2. The amounts that may be or may become
due to any educational or training institution , not in
excess of the amount specified in section three here-
of , shall be payable to such institution or school from
the fund hereby created on vouchers approved by
the State Board of Education : Said Board shall
determine the eligibility of the children who make
application for the benefits provided for in this act ;
satisfy itself of the attendance of such children at
any such institution or school and of the accuracy
and reasonableness of the charge or charges submit-
ted to said Board by the authorities of any such in-
stitution , on account of the attendance thereat of

CHAPTER 67 349
any such children ; provided no member of said
Board or its Secretary shall receive any compen-
sation for such services .
3. The sum of two thousand four hundred dol-
lars, or so much thereof as may be necessary , is
hereby appropriated from the general fund for each
of the fiscal years ending June 30 , 1935 , and June
30 , 1936 , for carrying out the provisions of this act ;
provided not more than one hundred and fifty dol-
lars shall be paid under said provisions for any one
child for any one school year . And provided fur-
ther that in the event all of said fund is not con-
sumed in providing scholarships for war orphans
attending state owned institutions of learning , then
and in that event the State Board of Education , in
the exercise of a wise and sound discretion and on
recommendation of the Commander of the American
Legion , Department of Kentucky , may appropriate
said balance , if any, to eligible war orphans who
desire to enroll in vocational, technical or business
schools ; provided further that not more than six
scholarships in such schools may be so awarded in
any one year .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 67.
AN ACT providing for the extension of teacher's certificates in
the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; the basic credits upon which
such credit certificates shall be extended ; the length of time
and by whom extended ; and providing for the payment of fee
in connection with the extension of same .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That certificates issued to any teacher by any
credits from colleges recognized by the State De-

350 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
partment of Education , which certificate expires in
the year 1934 and which is renewable by the addi-
tional college work of 16 hours or more , shall be
extended one year beyond its expiration ; and such
extension shall be made or issued by the department
of certification of the State Department of Educa-
tion upon application from the teacher , together
with a signed statement from the superintendent of
the county or city in which said teacher making such
application taught, certifying that said teacher had
taught either in the school years 1932-1933 or the
school years 1933-1934 ; provided , however , such ap-
plication shall be accompanied by a fee of $ 2.00 for
the necessary expenses in connection with the exten-
sion of said certificate .
Approved March 19, 1934.
CHAPTER 68.
AN ACT to authorize boards of education or boards of trustees
of school districts , embracing cities of the first , second , third ,
fourth , fifth or sixth class , to convey real estate for school
sites to such cities and to lease from such cities elementary
and/or high school building at a rental which will amortize
the cost of the erection of such building on such site , and
declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , Many of the school districts of the State of Ken .
tucky are without proper and suitable school buildings and
other physical facilities thus endangering the health , safety ,
convenience and general welfare of the children thereof ; and,
WHEREAS , The existing codition of financial distress of such
school boards makes it impossible for them to obtain such
needed physical facilities without supplementary financial as-
sistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public
Works Administration of the National Industrial Recovery
Act is offering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of
30 per cent of the cost of labor and materials and a loan of
the remaining 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials,

CHAPTER 68 351
at 4 per cent interest , necessary for the erection of needed
public buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of education of the various school districts of the State of
Kentucky may qualify for financial assistance under regula-
tions for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the Fed-
eral Emergency Administration of Public Works under the
National Industrial Recovery Act , and ,
WHEREAS , The construction of adequate school buildings for
the children of the various school districts of the state is a
part of the National Recovery Program and is to provide for
the immediate employment of labor and the improvement of
the school property of the State of Kentucky . Now , There-
fore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That for the purpose of providing buildings
for elementary and/or high school purposes , boards
of education or boards of trustees of school dis-
tricts embracing cities of the first , second , third ,
fourth , fifth or sixth class , are hereby authorized
and empowered to convey a fee simple title with
covenant of general warranty of title , to a site for
any such building now held or hereafter acquired
by such boards of education or boards of trustees ,
to such cities .
§ 2. Every such city to which a building site for
a building has been conveyed , as provided in sec-
tion one hereof , shall enter into a contract or con-
tracts with some person or persons , corporation or
corporations , for the erection on said site of a build-
ing with the necessary appurtenances , according to
plans and specifications adopted by such city and
approved by such board and the Superintendent of
Public Instruction .
3. Immediately upon the approval of the plans
and specifications as provided in section two hereof
such board of education or board of trustees shall

352 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
offer to lease such building for a term of one or more
years from the time such building shall be completed
and ready for occupancy , which term shall be for a
number of years acceptable to such city . The said
lease by its terms shall give such lessee the right
and option to extend the term of such lease for a
term of one or more years from the expiration of the
original term of such lease and for one or more
years from the expiration of each extended term
of such lease , until the original term of such lease
shall have been extended for a total number of
years, not exceeding thirty years , at a rental which ,
if paid for the original term and for each of the
full number of years for which the term of said
lease may be extended , will amortize the total cost
of the erection of said building and appurtenances ,
provide an adequate maintenance fund and in ad-
dition thereto a sum sufficient to pay the cost of
insuring the building against loss or damage by fire
and wind storm or other calamity in such sum as
may be agreed by the parties thereto .
§ 4. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of
elementary and/or high school buildings by any
board of education or board of trustees of school
districts embracing cities of the first , second , third ,
fourth , fifth or sixth class and shall not be deemed
to include , alter , amend or repeal any other statute .
5. Whereas , many of the school districts to
which this act is applicable are now without ade-
quate elementary and/or high school buildings , thus
endangering the health , safety, convenience and gen-
eral welfare of the children thereof , now, therefore ,
an emergency is hereby declared to exist and this act
shall take effect immediately upon its passage and
approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 69 353
CHAPTER 69 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the first , second , third , fourth ,
fifth and sixth class , to acquire elementary and /or high school
buildings and to issue bonds therefor , payable from the rev-
enues of such building or buildings , and declaring an emer-
gency to exist .
WHEREAS , Many of the school districts of the State of Ken-
tucky are without proper and suitable school buildings and
other physical facilities thus endangering the health , safety ,
convenience and general welfare of the children thereof ; and ,
WHEREAS, The existing condition of financial distress of such
school boards makes it impossible for them to obtain such
needed physical facilities without supplementary financial as-
sistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public Works
Administration of the National Industrial Recovery Act is
offering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of 30 per
cent of the cost of labor and materials and a loan of the re-
maining 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials , at 4
per cent interest, necessary for the erection of needed public
buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of education of the various school districts of the State of
Kentucky may qualify for financial assistance under regula-
tions for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the Federal
Emergency Administration of Public Works under the Na-
tional Industrial Recovery Act , and ,
WHEREAS , The construction of adequate school buildings for
the children of the various school districts of the state is a
part of the National Recovery Program and is to provide for
the immediate employment of labor and the improvement of
the school property of the State of Kentucky ; NOW , THERE-
FORE,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. That cities of the first , second , third, fourth ,
fifth , and sixth class , whether organized under the
general law or special charter law , and including
such cities where the commission form of govern-
ment has been adopted , are hereby authorized and
Acts-12

354 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
empowered to establish and erect elementary and/or
high school buildings , together with necessary ap-
purtenances thereto within the corporate limits
under the provisions of this act for the purpose of
supplying the boards of education or the boards of
trustees of such city or town adequate buildings
necessary to carry out their corporate duties and
powers .
§ 2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
acquiring any such elementary and/or high school
buildings by construction and appurtenances there-
to , any such city may borrow money from the Pub-
lic Works Administration or other agency of the
Federal Government and issue negotiable bonds ,
provided no such bonds shall be issued unless and
until authorized by an ordinance specifying the pro-
posed undertaking , the amount of bonds to be is-
sued and the maximum rate of interest such bonds
are to bear, which shall not be more than six per
cent (6 % ) per annum . Such ordinance shall fur-
ther provide that the proposed elementary and/or
high school buildings and appurtenances thereto
are to be constructed or acquired pursuant to the
provisions of this act .
§ 3. All bonds under the provisions of this act
may be issued bearing interest at a rate not ex-
ceeding six per cent per annum , payable semi -an-
nually , and shall be executed in such manner and be
payable at such times , not exceeding thirty years
from date thereof , and at such place or places as
such common council , board of commissioners or
board of trustees shall determine .
4. Any and all such bonds shall have and are
hereby declared to have in the hands of bona fide
holders all of the qualities of negotiable instruments
under the law merchant , and shall not be subject to
taxation . In case any of the officers whose signa-

CHAPTER 69 355
tures or counter signatures appearing on the bonds
or coupons shall cease to be such officers before de-
livery of such bonds , such signatures or counter
signatures shall nevertheless be valid and sufficient
for all purposes the same as if they had remained
in office until such delivery . Such bonds shall be
sold in such manner and upon such terms as the
common council , board of commissioners or board
of trustees shall deem for the best interest of such
city . Such bonds when issued shall be payable sole-
ly from the revenue funds derived from such ele-
mentary and/or high school building as provided
in sections eight and nine hereof and shall not con-
stitute an indebtedness of such city within the mean-
ing of the constitutional provisions or limitations .
It shall be plainly stated on the face of such bond
that same has been issued under the provisions of
this act and that it does not constitute an indebted-
ness of such city within the meaning of any consti-
tutional provisions or limitations .
§ 5. All moneys received from any bonds issued
pursuant hereto shall be applied solely for the es-
tablishment or erections of such elementary and/or
high school building and necessary appurtenances
thereto , provided such moneys may be used also to
advance the payment of the interest on bonds during
the first three years following the date of such bonds .
And there shall be and there is hereby created a
stautory mortgage lien upon the elementary and/or
high school building and appurtenances so acquired
to and in favor of the holders of said bonds and each
of them and to and in favor of the holders of the
coupons of said bonds .
$ 6. The elementary and/or high school build-
ing so acquired , together with the appurtenances
thereto , shall remain subject to such statutory lien
until the payment in full of the principal and in-

356 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
terest of the bonds . Any holder of said bonds or of
any of the coupons may , either at law or in equity ,
protect and enforce the statutory mortgage lien
hereby conferred and may by suit , action , man-
damus , or other proceedings , enforce and compel
performance of all duties required by this act , in-
cluding the making and collecting of sufficient rates ,
the segregation of the income and revenue , and the
application thereof .
§ 7. If there be any default in the payment of
the principal or interest on any of said bonds , any
court having jurisdiction of the action may appoint
a receiver to adminster said elementary and/or high
school building on behalf of the city, with power
to charge and collect rentals sufficient to provide for
the payment of any bonds or obligations outstand-
ing against said elementary and/or high school
building and for the payment of the operating ex-
penses and apply the income and revenues in con-
formity with this act , and the ordinance referred
to in sections eight and nine hereof.
$ 8. When any such city shall desire to construct
such elementary and/or high school building , the
common council , board of commissioners or board
of trustees of such city shall , by ordinance , cause
plans and specifications for the elementary and/or
high school building to be constructed to be duly
made and filed in the office of the city clerk, which
plans and specifications shall give a full description
of the building to be constructed , the details thereof
and the manner of construction ,
The plans and specifications of such building shall
be prepared by an architect selected by the city and
approved by the board of education or board of
trustees of the school district and shall be submitted
to the board of education or board of trustees of the
school district of such city and to the Superintendent

CHAPTER 69 357
of Public Instruction for approval and if approved,
and if such board of education or board of trustees
shall offer to lease such building for a term of one
or more years from the time such building shall be
completed and ready for occupancy , with the right
and option in such lessee to extend the term of such
lease for a term of one or more years from the ex-
piration of the original term of such lease and for
one or more years from the expiration of each ex-
tended term of such lease , until the original term of
such lease shall have been extended for a total num-
ber of years , not exceeding thirty years , at a rental
which, if paid for the original term and for each of
the full number of years for which the term of said
lease may be extended , will amortize the total cost
of the erection of said building and appurtenances ,
provide an adequate maintenance fund and in ad-
dition thereto a sum sufficient to pay the cost of in-
suring the building erected against loss or damage
by fire and wind storm or . other calamity in such
sum as may be agreed by the parties thereto , the
common council , board of commissioners or board
of trustees shall by ordinance authorize the issuance
of bonds , in the manner and form as herein pro-
vided sufficient to pay the cost of constructing such
building, and shall cause the city clerk to advertise
for bids , and thereafter the common council , board
of commissioners , or board of trustees , by and
through the mayor or chairman of the board of
trustees of such city , may contract for the construc-
tion of such building .
$ 9. At or before the issuance of such bonds , the
common council , board of commissioners , or board
of trustees of such city shall , by ordinance , set aside
and pledge the income of such building into a sepa-
rate and special fund to be used and applied in pay-
ment of the cost thereof and in the maintenance

358 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY1
thereof . Said ordinance shall definitely fix and de-
termine the amount of revenue which shall be neces-
sary and be set aside and applied for the payment
of the principal and interest of the bonds , and the
balance of such income shall be set aside for the
reasonable and proper maintenance thereof , includ-
ing a sufficient sum to pay the cost of such insur-
ance as hereinabove provided . The rentals to be
charged for the use of such building shall be suf-
ficient to provide for the payment of interest upon
all bonds and to create a sinking fund , to pay the
principal thereof as and when the same becomes due
and to provide for the maintenance thereof includ-
ing the cost of insuring such building erected against
loss or damage by fire and wind storm or other
calamity .
10. If any surplus shall be accumulated in the
maintenance fund , which shall be equal to the cost
of maintaining such building during the remainder
of the calendar , or fiscal year, as may be provided
by the ordinance hereinbefore required and the cost
of maintaining and operating such building the suc-
ceeding like calendar or fiscal year , any excess over
such amount shall be transferred to the sinking
fund .
§ 11. Such city may issue refunding bonds for
the purpose of providing funds for the payment of
any outstanding bonds , in accordance with the pro-
cedure prescribed by this act . Such refunding bonds
shall be secured to the same extent and shall have
the same source of payment as the bonds which shall
have been thereby refunded .
§ 12. Should the common council , board of com-
missioners , or board of trustees find that the bonds
authorized will be insufficient to accomplish the pur-
pose desired , additional bonds may be authorized

CHAPTER 69 359
and issued subject to the procedure as herein re-
quired .
§ 13. Such city in acquiring any building under
the provisions of this act , may provide by ordinance
any such provision and stipulation for the admin-
istration of the income for the security of the bond-
holders as the common council , board of commis-
sioners , or board of trustees of such city may deem
necessary : Provided that the sinking fund , which
shall be provided by ordinance as hereinbefore re-
quired, shall be deposited in a depository selected
by such common council , board of commissioners , or
board of trustees , which deposit , where practicable ,
may be continuously secured by a pledge to the city .
of direct obligations of the United States of Ameri-
ca , exclusive of accrued interest , at all times at least
equal to the balance on deposit in such account , or
in such other manner acceptable to the purchaser
or holders of such bonds . Such securities shall either
be deposited with the city or be held by a trustee or
agent satisfactory to the common council , board of
commissioners , or board of trustees of such city .
Such sinking fund may be invested in direct obliga-
tions of the United States of America .
§ 14. When the board of education or board of
trustees of such school district of such city has paid
rent , in the manner and form as provided in this
act, sufficient to amortize the cost of the erection of
such building and appurtenances , to maintain such
building and pay the cost of insurance , such city
shall thereupon convey said premises to such board,
and shall transfer the balance , if any , remaining in
the funds herein provided to the account of such
board of education or board of trustees .
$ 15. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of
elementary and/or high school buildings by any

360 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
city of the first , second , third , fourth , fifth , or sixth
class and shall not be deemed to include , alter ,
amend or repeal any other statute . No proceeding
shall be required for the acquisition of any ele-
mentary and/or high school building hereunder or
the issuance of bonds hereunder except such as are
prescribed by this act , and provision in the general
laws of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or the char-
ter of any city to the contrary notwithstanding .
§ 16. Whereas , many of the cities to which this
act is applicable are now without adequate ele-
mentary and/or high school buildings , thus endan-
gering the health , safety , convenience and general
welfare of the children thereof , now, therefore , an
emergency is hereby declared to exist and this act
shall take effect immediately upon its passage and
approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 70 .
AN ACT to authorize boards of education or boards of trustees
of county school districts or independent graded common
school districts located outside of the incorporated cities of
the county to convey real estate for school sites to such coun-
ties and to lease from such counties elementary and /or high
school buildings at a rental which will amortize the cost of
the erection of such school buildings in such site , and de-
claring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , Many of the school districts of the State of Ken-
tucky are without proper and suitable school buildings and
other physical facilities thus endangering the health , safety ,
convenience and general welfare of the children thereof ; and ,
WHEREAS , The existing condition of financial distress of such
school boards makes it impossible for them to obtain such
needed physical facilities without supplementary financial as-
sistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public
Works Administration of the National Industrial Recovery

CHAPTER 70 361
Act is offering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of
30 per cent of the cost and labor and materials and a loan of
the remaining 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials ,
at 4 per cent , interest , necessary for the erection of needed
public buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of education of the various school districts of the State of
Kentucky may qualify for financial assistance under regula-
tions for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the Fed-
eral Emergency Administration of Public Works under the
National Industrial Recovery Act ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Construction of adequate school buildings for
the children of the various school districts of the state is a
part of the National Recovery Program and is to provide for
the immediate employment of labor and the improvement of
the school property of the State of Kentucky ; NOW THERE-
FORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That for the purpose of providing buildings
for elementary and/or high school purposes , boards
of education or boards of trustees of independent
graded common school districts located within coun-
ties , exclusive of incorporated cities of such coun-
ties, are hereby authorized and empowered to con-
vey a fee simple title with covenant of general war-
ranty of title , to a site for any such building now
held or hereafter acquired by such boards of edu-
cation or boards of trustees , to such counties .
§ 2. Every such county to which a building site
for a building has been conveyed , as provided in
section one hereof, shall enter into a contract or
contracts with some person or persons , corporation
or corporations , for the erection on said site of a
building with the necessary appurtenances accord-
ing to plans and specifications adopted by such
county and approved by the board of education or
the board of trustees of the independent graded

362 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
common school district in which such building is
proposed to be constructed and by the Superin-
tendent of Public Instruction .
§ 3. Immediately upon the approval of the plans
and specifications as provided in section two here-
of, such board of education or boards of trustees
shall offer to lease such building for a term of one
or more years from the time such building shall be
completed and ready for occupancy , which term
shall be for a number of years acceptable to such
county . The said lease by its terms shall give such
lessee the right and option to extend the term of
such lease for a term of one or more years from the
expiration of the original term of such lease and
for one or more years from the expiration of each
extended term of such lease , until the original term
of such lease shall have been extended for a total
number of years , not exceeding thirty years , at a
rental which , if paid for the original term and for
cach of the full number of years for which the term
of said lease may be extended , will amortize the total
cost of the erection of said building and appurte-
nances , provide an adequate maintenance fund and
in addition thereto a sum sufficient to pay the cost
of insuring the building against loss or damage by
fire and wind storm or other calamity in such sum
as may be agreed by the parties thereto .
§ 4. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of
elementary and /or high school buildings by any
county board of education or board of trustees of
independent graded common school districts and
shall not be deemed to include , alter , amend or re-
peal any other statute .
§ 5. Whereas , many of the school districts to
which this act is applicable are now without ade-
quate school buildings , thus endangering the health ,

CHAPTER 71 363
1
safety, convenience and general welfare of the stu
dents thereof , now, therefore, an emergency is here-
by declared to exist , and this act shall take effect
immediately upon its passage and approval .
Approved March 19, 1934 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That counties whether organized under the
general law or special charter law are hereby author-
ized and empowered to establish and erect ele-
mentary and/or high school buildings , together with
necessary appurtenances thereto under the provi-
sions of this act for the purpose of supplying the
boards of education or boards of trustees of such
county or independent graded common school dis-
tricts located outside of the incorporated cities of
such county , adequate buildings necessary to carry
out their corporate duties and powers .
§ 2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
acquiring any such elementary and/or high school
building by construction and appurtenances thereto ,
any such county may borrow money from the Public
Works Administration or other agency of the Fed-
eral Government and issue negotiable bonds , pro-
Ivided no such bonds shall be issued unless and until
authorized by a resolution specifying the proposed
undertaking, the amount of bonds to be issued and
the maximum rate of interest such bonds are to
bear , which shall not be more than six per cent
(6% ) per annum . Such resolution shall further pro-
vide that the proposed elementary and/or high
school buildings and appurtenances thereto are to
be constructed or acquired pursuant to the provi-
sions of this act .
3. All bonds under the provisions of this act
may be issued bearing interest at a rate not ex-
ceeding six per cent per annum , payable semi -an-
nually , and shall be executed in such manner and be
payable at such times , not exceeding thirty years
from the date thereof, and at such place or places
as such fiscal court or board of commissioners shall
determine .

signatures or counter signatures appearing on the

CHAPTER 72 .
AN ACT to authorize the University of Kentucky , any of the
State Teachers' Colleges and other state educational institu-
tions to acquire , by construction or extension , buildings to be
used in connection with such institutions and to issue bonds
therefor, payable from the revenues of such buildings , and
declaring an emergency to exist.
WHEREAS , Many of the state educational institutions of Ken-
tucky are without proper and suitable dormitories , health
buildings and other physical facilities thus endangering the
health , safety , convenience and general welfare of the stu
dents thereof ; and
WHEREAS , The existing condition of financial distress makes
it impossible for them to obtain such needed physical facili-
ties without supplementary financial assistance ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Federal Government through the Public Works
Administration of the National Industrial Recovery Act is of-
fering financial aid to the extent of a direct gift of 30 per cent
of the cost of labor and materials and a loan of the remain-
ing 70 per cent of the cost of labor and materials , at 4 per
cent interest , necessary for the erection of needed public
buildings for the use and benefit of society ; and ,
WHEREAS , There are no statutory provisions whereby boards
of regents and other governing bodies of such state educa-
tional institutions may qualify for financial assistance under
regulations for such grants and aids as is prescribed by the
Federal Emergency Administration of Public Works under the
National Industrial Recovery Act ; and ,

CHAPTER 75 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section 1893d -9 of Bald-
win's Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to
game, fish and limiting the number and size of fish which may
be taken .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 1893d -9 of Baldwin's Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is
hereby repealed , amended , and re -enacted so that
when re -enacted it will read as follows :
No person shall take , or catch , or have in his pos-
session more than ten ( 10 ) black bass , nor more
than ten ( 10 ) trout , nor more than fifteen ( 15 ) rock
bass or " goggle eye " nor more than fifteen ( 15 )
crappie in any one day , nor take , nor have in his
possession any black bass , nor trout under eleven
(11 ) inches in length , nor any crappie under eight
( 8 ) inches in length ; provided , however , that any
person having fished two days in succession may
have in possession a total not to exceed twenty ( 20 )
black bass , or trout , thirty ( 30 ) rock bass or
"goggle eye, or thirty ( 30 ) crappie .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 76 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section 1905-2 of Bald-
win's Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the license
fees for the catching of mussels .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 1905-2 of Baldwin's Kentucky Stat-
utes, Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is here-by repealed , amended and re-enacted so that when
re-enacted it will read as follows :

380 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The State Game and Fish Commission may , upon
application , issue a license to take , catch or kill
mussels . Upon making application for said license ,
the residents of this State shall pay to the State
Game and Fish Commission a fee of $ 6.00 and non-
residents shall pay to the State Game and Fish
Commission a fee of $ 50.00 . All such licenses shall
expire on the 31st day of December following their
issue .
The licenses shall be consecutively numbered as
issued , and record shall be kept thereof in the office
of the State Game and Fish Commission . Such li-
censes shall state whether it is a resident or non-
resident license , and it shall entitle the holder there-
of to operate one boat only . An operator desiring
to operate more than one boat shall be required to
secure a separate license for each boat . The holder
of a license while taking, catching or killing mussels
for commercial purposes shall have his license with
him ready for exhibition , and shall exhibit the same
when required to do so by an authorized officer .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 81 .
AN ACT for the protection of ruffed grouse and providing pen-
alties for the violation of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That no person shall in the State of Ken-
tucky shoot , kill , injure or have in possession dead
or alive any ruffed grouse , commonly known as
native Pheasant .
§ 2. Any person who shall shoot , kill , injure or
have in his possession dead or alive any ruffed
grouse , commonly known as native Pheasant , shall
upon conviction be fined not less than fifteen dollars

CHAPTER 82 385
($ 15.00 ) , nor more than one hundred dollars
($100.00) for each offense .
§ 3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 82.
AN ACT to amend and re-enact section 1938a -6, Supplement to
Kentucky Statutes , 1930 edition , relating to wild turkey , im-
ported pheasant or Hungarian' partridge.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 1938a -6 , Supplement to Ken-
tucky Statutes, 1930 edition, be amended and re-
enacted , and when amended and re-enacted shall
read as follows :
No person shall catch , kill or pursue with such
intent , or have the same in his possession after it
has been caught or killed , any wild turkey , before
the 23rd day of November , 1936 , and on and after
November 23 , 1936 , the open season for wild turkey
shall be from November 23rd to November 30th ,
both days inclusive , and no person shall on any
other day , hunt , catch , kill , pursue or have in pos-
session , dead or alive , any wild turkey , and no per-
son shall kill more than one male turkey during the
open season in any one year ; and that it shall be
unlawful to kill hen turkeys at any time ; and no per-
son shall catch or kill, or pursue with such intent, or
have the same in his possession after it has been
caught or killed , any imported pheasant or Hun-
garian partridge before the 23rd day of November ,
1936 , and on and after November 23 , 1936 , no per-
son shall kill more than two pheasants or Hungarian
Partridges in any one day when it is lawful to kill
Acts-13

386 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
them . It shall be lawful to kill imported pheasants
from November 23rd to November 30th , both days
inclusive , and on these days only each year , pro-
vided that cock birds only may be killed , and that
not more than two may be killed in any one day.
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 83 .
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Section 1938a -16 , Supplement
to Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to elk and deer ;
and providing penalties for the violation of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1938a -16 , Supplement to Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , be amended and re-
enacted , so that when amended and re - enacted it
shall read as follows :
No person shall , in the State of Kentucky , hunt ,
pursue , chase , catch , kill , injure or molest any elk ,
and no person shall , in the State of Kentucky , be-
fore December 1st , 1936 , hunt , pursue, chase , catch ,
kill , injure or molest any deer , and on and after
December 1st 1936 , the open season for killing deer
shall be from December 1st to December 5th both
days inclusive , and no person shall , during the
open season , in any one year, kill more than one
male deer , and female deer shall not be killed at
any time . Any person violating this section , or any
feature thereof , shall upon conviction , be fined not
less than fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) or more than one
hundred dollars ($ 100.00 ) for each offense , and
upon failure to pay such fine , shall be confined in
the county jail or workhouse , or placed as a laborer

CHAPTER 85 387
on any of the public works of the county in which he
is convicted , and confined for a length of time not
exceeding one day of every one and one -half dol-
lars of said fine .
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 84.
AN ACT governing the investments of funds of estates by guard-
ians and committees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Every guardian and committee shall invest the
funds of a ward's estate in such manner or in such
securities , in which the guardian or committee has
no interest , as allowed by law or approved by the
court or in annuities .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Whereas , any delay in the privilege of so invest-
ing the funds of a ward may result in a loss of the
principal or income to such ward , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist, and this Act shall take
effect upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
Approved March 19, 1934 .
CHAPTER 85.
AN ACT requiring executors , administrators , curators , trustees ,
guardians and committees to publish notice of the date of
filing of settlements , and in what court , in the newspaper of
largest circulation in the county of qualification ; and allowing
a charge against each estate for publication of the notice ;
and setting out the form of notice to be used .

388 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
All executors , administrators , curators , trustees ,
guardians and committees shall, before the filing of
their settlements , as now required by law , publish a
notice of the date on which each of their said settle-
ments will be filed , and in what court , within two
weeks before the filing of each settlement , in the
newspaper of largest circulation in the county of
their qualification , and a charge of fifty ( 50c ) cents
is allowed against each estate for the publication of
the notice for each settlement . Said notices in blank
to be in words as follows : " NOTICE . On .
the undersigned as
(Insert date )
(Qualification )
of
(Name of ward or estate )
will file a settlement in the
(Name of Court )
(Name of Fiduciary)
Approved March 19 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 86 .
AN ACT to authorize an administrator de bonis non or the suc-
cessor of an executor , administrator or personal representa-
tive , to maintain an action against his predecessor and the
sureties on his predecessor's bond and the predecessor's per-
sonal and real representative .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That an administrator de bonis non or any suc
cessor appointed in the place of an executor or ad-
ministrator or personal representative, who has

CHAPTER 87 389
resigned, been removed or whose letters have been
revoked, or who has ceased to act or has died , may
maintain an action against the former executor ,
administrator or personal representative and the
sureties on the administration bond and against the
predecessor's real and personal representatives and
for all damages or debts arising from the malad-
ministration or omission of the predecessor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 87 .
AN ACT to regulate sales of real estate held by trustees under
express trusts.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The trustee of an express trust may sell
and convey any interest in real estate held by said
trustee under an expressed trust in all cases where
the court under existing or future laws would have
jurisdiction to order the sale of said real estate or
said interest therein at judicial sale and said sales
shall be made upon such terms and conditions as
the trustee shall deem for the best interest of the
trust estate , provided , however, that no such sale
shall be consummated until same shall have been
approved by the judge of the circuit court in the
county where the land or portion thereof is located
in an equitable action filed by the trustee against
all persons in being having an interest in said prop-
erty , the petition whereof shall set forth the rea-
sons for the sale and the terms thereof and proof
taken by depositions duly filed or on oral proof be-
fore the chancellor which proof shall thereafter be
transcribed and filed in the record of the case which
proof shall not be taken until after summons shall

390 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

and until after the appointment of a guardian ad
litem for any said defendants who are under dis-
ability , and which proof shall be taken only on no-

the chancellor that the sale is advisable and the
price satisfactory , and after said guardian ad litem,
if any there be , shall have made a defense or shall
have filed a report stating that after a careful ex-
amination of the case he is unable to make a de-

CHAPTER 88 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact section 3766d -2, Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 Edition , pertaining to Kentucky State Parks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. That section 3766d - 2 , Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 Edition , be amended and re - enacted so that
when amended and re- enacted , it shall read as fol-
lows :
Said Commission shall be composed of four ( 4 )
members , viz.: ( 1 ) The Governor of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , who shall be ex-officio chairman
of the Commission by this act created ; ( 2 ) The
Dean of the Agricultural College of the University
of Kentucky ; ( 3 ) Two persons who shall be ap-
pointed by the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and who shall be over thirty ( 30 ) years
of age and be a citizen of Kentucky at the time of
their appointment and during all the time that they
shall be members of the Commission hereby created .
Said members so appointed by the Governor of the
State of Kentucky shall hold their office as members
of the Commission hereby created subject to the
will of the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, and the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky may remove them or either of them at
any time at the Governor's will.
§ 2. WHEREAS , it is of vital importance to the
people of the Commonwealth that the State Park
System be immediately improved and exploited , and
WHEREAS , under certain Federal laws , recently

392 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
passed , it is possible to obtain funds for the pur-
pose of said improvement , an emergency is declared
to exist and this law shall be effective upon its pass-
age and approval by the Governor .
3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 89 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 4880 , 4882 , 4914 and
4941 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Edition of 1930 , being
sections of the Workmen's Compensation Law of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4880 , of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Edition of 1930 , be amended by adding at
the end of said section the following words :
" and any employers and their employees engaged
in the operation of glass manufacturing plants,
quarries , sand mines or in the manufacture treating
or handling of sand may , with respect to the disease
of silicosis caused by the inhalation of silica dust ,
in like manner voluntarily subject themselves
thereto as to such disease . "
so that said section when amended will read as fol-
lows :
" This act shall apply to all employers having
three or more employees regularly engaged in the
same occupation , or business and to their em-
ployees , except that it shall not apply to domestic
employment , agriculture , steam railways , or such
common carriers other than steam railways for
which a rule of liability is provided by the laws of
the United States . Provided , however , it shall ap-
ply to the operators of threshing machines used in

CHAPTER 89 393
threshing or hulling grain or seeds . It shall affect
the liability of the employers subject thereto to
their employees for a personal injury sustained by
the employee by accident arising out of and in the
course of his employment , or for death resulting
from such accidental injury ; provided , however ,
that personal injury by accident as herein defined
shall not include diseases except where the disease
is the natural and direct result of a traumatic in-
jury by accident , nor shall they include the results
of a pre -existing disease but shall include injuries.
or death due to inhalation in mines of noxious gases
or smoke , commonly known as ' bad air, ' and also
shall include the injuries or death due to the in-
halation of any kind of gas . Any employers and em-
ployees who are by the provisions of this section
excepted from the provisions of this Act , including
employers having less than three employees , may
subject themselves thereto by joint , voluntary ap-
plication to the board , in writing , for such period
as may be stated in the application , which shall be
irrevocable during such period and effective there-
after until a written revocation be filed with the
board or the employment be terminated, and any
employers and their employees engaged in the oper-
ation of glass manufacturing plants, quarries , sand
mines or in the manufacture, treating or handling
of sand may , with respect to the disease of silicosis
caused by the inhalation of silica dust , in like man-
ner voluntarily subject themselves thereto as to
such disease . '" "
§ 2. That Section 4882 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Edition of 1930 , be amended by adding , at
the end of said section the following :
" The words ' wilful misconduct ' as used in this
section , when relating to the disease of silicosis
caused by the inhalation of silica dust , shall include

394 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) failure or omission on the part of an employee
to observe such rules and recommendations as may
be adopted by the employer and approved by the
Workmen's Compensation Board and which rules
and recommendations have been and are kept posted
in a conspicuous place in and about the plant , ( 2)
failure or omission on the part of an employee
truthfully to state to the best of his knowledge in
answer to inquiry made by the employer the place,
duration , and nature of previous employment , ( 3 )
failure and omission on the part of an employee
truthfully to furnish to the best of his knowledge
in answer to an inquiry made by the employer full
information about the previous status of his health ,
habits and medical attention that he or his blood
relatives may have heretofore received , and (4)
failure or refusal to submit to medical examination
to determine his physical condition with reference
to the disease of silicosis whenever such examina-
tion is ordered by the board , or to evade or obstruct
such examination . "
so that said section when amended shall read as
follows :
" Whereas , at the time of the injury both em-
ployer and employee have elected to furnish or ac-
cept compensation under the provisions of this act
for a personal injury, received by an employee by
accident and arising out of and in the course of his
employment , or for death resulting from such in-
jury , within two years thereafter , the employer
shall be liable to provide and pay compensation
under the provisions of this act and shall be re-
leased from all other liability whatsoever ; provided ,
however , that if injury or death result to an em-
ployee through the deliberate intention of his em-
ployer to produce such injury or death , the em-
ployee or his dependent as herein defined shall re-

CHAPTER 89 395
ceive the amount provided in this act in a lump sum
to be used , if they so desire to prosecute the em-
ployer , and said dependents shall be permitted to
bring suit against said employer for any amount
they may desire ; that if injury or death results to
an employee through the deliberate intention of his
employer to produce such injury or death , the em-
ployee or his dependents as herein defined shall
have the privilege to take under this act , or in lieu
thereof , to have a cause of action at law against
such employer as if this act had not been passed ,
for such damage so sustained by the employee , his
dependents or personal representatives as may be
recoverable at law . If a suit is brought under this
section , all right to compensation under the provi-
sions of this act shall thereby be waived and void as
to all persous , and if a claim is made for the pay-
ment of compensation or any other benefit provided
by this act , all rights to sue the employer for dam-
ages on account of such injury or death shall there-
by be waived and void as to all persons . Notwith-
standing anything hereinafter or hereafter con-
tained , no employee or dependent of any employee
shall be entitled to receive compensation on account
of any injury to or death of an employee caused by
a wilful self -inflicted injury , wilful misconduct or in-
toxication of such employee . The words ' wilful
misconduct ' as used in this section , when relating to
the disease of silicosis caused by the inhalation of
silica dust, shall include ( 1 ) failure or omission on
the part of an employee to observe such rules and
recommendations as may be adopted by the em-
ployer and approved by the Workmen's Compensa-
tion Board and which rules and recommendations
have been and are kept posted in a conspicuous
place in and about the plant , ( 2 ) failure or omission
on the part of an employee truthfully to state to the

396 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
best of his knowledge in answer to inquiry made by
the employer the place , duration , and nature of pre-
vious employment , ( 3 ) failure and omission on the
part of an employee truthfully to furnish to the
best of his knowledge in answer to an inquiry made
by the employer full information about the previous
status of his health , habits , and medical attention
that he or his blood relatives may have heretofore
received , and ( 4 ) failure or refusal to submit to
medical examination to determine his physical con-
dition with reference to the disease of silicosis
whenever such examination is ordered by the board ,
or to evade or obstruct such examination . "
§ 3. That Section 4914 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes, Edition of 1930 , be amended by adding
at the end of said section the following words :
" In cases of the disease of silicosis , caused by the
inhalation of silica dust, no application for com-
pensation shall be considered unless made within
one year after the last injurious exposure to silica
dust, '
so that said section when amended shall read as
follows :
" No proceeding under this act for compensation
for an injury or death shall be maintained unless a
notice of the accident shall have been given to the
employer as soon as practicable after the happening
thereof and unless a claim for compensation with
respect to such injury shall have been made within
one year after the date of the accident , or , in case
of death , within one year after such death , whether
or not a claim has been made by the employee him-
self for compensation . Such notice and such claim
may be given or made by any person claiming to be
entitled to compensation or by some one in his be-
half. If payments of compensation as such have

CHAPTER 89 397
been made voluntarily the making of a claim within
such period shall not be required, but shall become
requisite following the suspension of such volun-
tary payments . In cases of the disease of silicosis ,
caused by the inhalation of silica dust, no applica-
tion for compensation shall be considered unless
made within one year after the last injurious ex-
posure to silica dust."
§ 4. That Section 4941 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Edition of 1930 , be amended by adding at
the end of said section the following words :
" When application for compensation is made by
an employee for compensation for disability due to
the disease of silicosis caused by the inhalation of
silica dust , the Workmen's Compensation Board
shall , upon the application of the employer or the
employee , appoint either two , or three , disinterested
and duly qualified physicians to examine the em-
ployee ; if two are appointed , one of them shall be a
qualified radiologist , and if three are appointed ,
two of them shall be so qualified . Said physicians
shall examine the employee and certify their find-
ings to the Workmen's Compensation Board , which
findings shall state , ( 1 ) in what stage , if any , the
employee is suffering from the diseases of silicosis ;
(2 ) whether the disability of the employee is tem-
porary , partial or total , and ( 3 ) the extent , if any ,
of the impairment of capacity to work of the em-
ployee . Said physicians , so appointed , shall be al-
lowed necessary traveling expenses and reasonable
fees , to be fixed by the Workmen's Compensation
Board, but not to exceed , for each physician , the
sum of $20.00 . Said fees and expenses of the medi-
cal examiners shall be paid by the party applying
for the appointment . If compensation for such dis-
ability is awarded by the Board , the employee shall
also recover of the employer , or his insurance car-

398 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
rier liable for the compensation , such fees and ex-
penses of said medical examiners paid by him,"
so that said section when amended shall read as
follows :
" The Board , or any member thereof, may , upon
the application of either party or upon its own
motion, appoint a disinterested and duly qualified
physician or surgeon to make any necessary medi-
cal examination of the employee and to testify in
respect thereto . Said physician or surgeon shall
be allowed traveling expenses and a reasonable fee

( 2 ) whether the disability of the employee is tem-
porary , partial or total , and ( 3 ) the extent , if any,

lowed necessary traveling expenses and reasonable

CHAPTER 92 .
AN ACT regulating insurance companies ; relating to invest-
ment of capital , surplus and accumulations of insurance com-
panies ; defining what property shall be necessary for carry-
ing on its legitimate business ; providing a penalty against
directors and officers of insurance companies for the violation
of the provisions of this Act ; repealing Section Six Hundred
Thirty -two of the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition ,
the same being Section 95 at page 648 , chapter 171 of the
Acts of the 1893 General Assembly of Kentucky , and repealing

CHAPTER 92 407
�
10
and re-enacting Section Six Hundred Twenty -five of the Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition and supplements
thereto , the same being chapter 60 , page 187 , of the Acts of
the 1930 General Assembly of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section Six Hundred Thirty -two of the Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , the same be-
ing Section 95 at page 648 , Chapter 171 of the Acts
of the 1893 General Assembly of Kentucky be re-
pealed ; and that Section Six Hundred Twenty -five
of the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition
and supplements thereto , the same being chapter 60 ,
page 187 , of the Acts of the 1930 General Assembly
of Kentucky , be repealed and re- enacted so as to
read as follows :
FIRST- Domestic Insurance Companies .
The capital , surplus and accumulations of every
domestic insurance company shall be invested as
follows :
A. Securities of the United States and its Gov-
ernmental Agencies .
In bonds , notes , warrants and securities which
are the direct obligation of the United States , or
those for which the faith and credit of the United
States are pledged for the payment of principal and
interest.
B. Securities of States of the United States of
America , District of Columbia , and of the Dominion
and Provinces of Canada .
In bonds , notes , warrants and securities which are
the direct obligation of the District of Columbia , or
of any state or territory of the United States , or of
the Dominion of Canada , or any province thereof,
provided no default. in payment of interest thereon ,
or principal thereof , has occurred within five years
of the date of the investment therein, or since is-

408 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
suance , if such securities were issued less than five
years prior to the date of investment therein .
C. Securities of Counties , Cities , School Dis-
tricts , and Revenue Bonds of Publicly -owned , Con-
trolled or Operated Bridges .
(a) In bonds , notes , warrants and securities of
any county , incorporated city ; or school district in
any state of the United States , or in any province
of the Dominion of Canada , which are the direct
obligation of such county , city or school district , and
for the payment of which such county , city or school
district has lawful authority to levy taxes or make
assessments , provided the legality and validity of
any such issue of bonds has been decreed by the
court of last resort having jurisdiction so to decide ,
and which county , city or school district has not ,
within tht last five years , defaulted in payment of
interest on , or principal of , any of its bonds , notes ,
warrants or securities .
(b) In bonds which are payable from revenues
or earnings specifically pledged therefor of any pub-
lic toll bridge or bridges owned by any state or in-
corporated city , or by a legally constituted public
corporation or commission which has established
and maintained a sinking fund for the payment of
the interest on , and the principal of , any such issue ;
provided the legality and validity of any such issue
of bonds has been decreed by the court of last re-
sort having jurisdiction so to decide , and such issue
has not , within the preceding five years been in de-
fault as to interest or principal , or since issuance ,
if such securities were issued less than five years
prior to the date of investment therein .
D. Real Estate Bonds and Mortgages .
(a) In loans secured by entire mortgages , liens ,
notes , or deeds of trust on fee simple , unencumbered ,
improved real estate , or in the entire issue of bonds

M. Policy Loans .
Any life insurance company may make loans to
any policyholder upon the security of its own poli-
cies to an amount which shall be adequately secured
thereby , and shall not in any case exceed the sur-
render value specified in the policy .
N. Collateral Loans .
In loans for periods not exceeding one year upon
the pledge of any securities in which such domestic
companies are authorized by this act to invest ; pro-
vided, however , that any loan upon such a pledge
shall not exceed eighty per cent ( 80 % ) of the readily
realizable market value of the collateral at the time
of the making of such loan, nor shall any company
through the collateral pledged to it , exceed the
amounts which such company , may under the pro-
visions of this act , invest in one corporation , so
that in the stocks and securities which may be owned
and those which are pledged to it, the limitations in
this act prescribed might be indirectly evaded .
O. Securties Acquired Through Exchange , Fore-
closure or Reorganization .
Nothing in this section contained shall be con-
strued as prohibiting any such insurance company
from entering into an agreement for the purpose of
protecting the interests of the company in securi-
ties lawfully held by it or foreclosing on property
lawfully pledged to it , or for the purpose of re-
organization of a corporation which issued securi-
ties so held , and from depositing such securities
with a committee or depositories appointed under

in writing by the Insurance Commissioner. Nor shall

securities which may be distributed pursuant to any
such agreement approved as aforesaid or to any
plan of reorganization approved in writing by the
Insurance Commissioner ; provided , that if any such
securities so received shall be of the kind not other-

they shall be disposed of within four (4 ) years from

then within the time so allowed .

(b ) No funds of any insurance company or-

provided that the restriction contained in this para-

( c) No director or officer of an insurance com-

receive any money or valuable thing for negotiating,
procuring , recommending or aiding in any purchase
or sale by such corporation of any property , or any
loan from or to such company , nor be pecuniarily
interested, either as principal , co -principal , agent ,
or beneficiary , in any such purchase , sale or loan ,
nor shall the financial obligation of any such direc-
tor or officer be guaranteed by such company , in any
capacity. Any person violating any provisions of
this paragraph shall be guilty of a misdemeanor ,
and upon conviction thereof shall be fined not less.
than five hundred dollars and not more than one
thousand dollars and imprisoned in the county jail

CHAPTER 92 417
for not less than sixty days and not more than one
year for each offense .
(d ) No loan or investment shall be made by a
domestic insurance company without the unanimous
approval of its finance or executive committee , or
the approval of a majority of the directors of such
company present at a meeting of such directors ,
and the name of each director approving or dis-
approving any loan or investment so made shall be
entered by the secretary on the records of the com-
pany .
(e) No such insurance company shall be per-
mitted to invest in or loan upon its own stock , either
directly or indirectly .
Q. Investments Not Deemed Legal , When .
If the investments of any domestic insurance com-
pany hereafter made shall at the time of the mak-
ing thereof be otherwise than as authorized in this
act, such investments shall not be admitted or ac-
cepted as legal or authorized investments for such
company .
R. Prior Investments Not Affected .
Nothing herein shall be construed as affecting the
propriety or legality of an investment made by a do-
mestic insurance company which was , or is , in ac-
cordance wtih the laws in force prior to the passage
of this act or at the time of making thereof .
SECOND- Foreign Insurance Companies .
Any foreign or alien insurance company hereafter
licensed or relicensed to do business in this Com-
monwealth shall be possessed of assets of the same
general character as specified for domestic insur-
ance companies , except that investments authorized
by the law of the home state , state of United States
domicile , or country , of such company may be recog-
nized as legal investments , in the discretion of the
Insurance Commissioner .

CHAPTER 93 .
AN ACT providing for the licensing of agents of life insurance
companies and providing for the revocation thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. No person shall act within this Common-
wealth for any life insurance company as an agent
in the solicitation or procurement of applications
for insurance unless he has complied with the pro-
visions of this Act and has secured a license from
the Insurance Commissioner.
§ 2. Each applicant for such license shall file
with the Insurance Commissioner his written ap-
plication therefor on blanks and/or supplements
furnished by the Commissioner, which application
shall be signed and sworn to by the applicant and
shall give his name , age , residence , place of busi-
ness and occupation for five years next prior to the
date of application and also set forth his qualifica-
tions for such license ; namely , his familiarity with
the life insurance laws of this Commonwealth and
with the provisions of the contracts to be nego-

CHAPTER 93 419
tiated ; what insurance experience he has had , if
any ; what insurance instruction he has had or ex-
pects to receive ; whether he has been refused , or
has had suspended or revoked , a license to solicit
insurance by the insurance department or super-
vising officials of any state ; whether any insurance
company or any general agent claims such appli-
cant is indebted under any agency contract or
otherwise , and if so , the name of the claimant , the
nature of the claim and the applicant's defense
thereto , if any ; whether he has had an agency con-
tract canceled , and if so , when , by what company
or general agent and the reason therefor , and such
additional information , if any , that the Commis-
sioner may require .
The applicant shall be vouched for by an official
or by a duly authorized representative of the Com-
pany for which the applicant proposes to act , who
shall certify whether the applicant is personally
known to him , whether the applicant has been ap-
pointed an agent to represent such company , and
that such company has duly investigated the char-
acter and record of such person , and has satisfied
itself that he is trustworthy and qualified to act as
its agent and intends to hold himself out in good
faith as a life insurance agent .
If, upon the showing made , the Insurance Com-
missioner is reasonably satisfied that the applicant
is a trustworthy and properly qualified person he
shall promptly issue the license applied for .
§ 3. An agent licensed to represent any life in-
surance company doing business in this State shall
be entitled to place excess or rejected risks in any
other company lawfully doing business in this
State, with the knowledge and approval of his own
company without additional or seperate license .

420 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 4. Every license issued under this Act shall
expire annually on March 1st unless prior thereto
it is revoked or suspended by the Insurance Com-
missioner or the authority of the Agent to act for
the company is terminated .
§ 5. Every life insurance company shall , upon
the termination of the employment of any agent ,
file with the Insurance Commissioner a statement
of the facts relative to the termination of such
employment and the cause thereof . Any informa-
tion , document , record , statement or thing required
to be made or disclosed to the Insurance Commis-
sioner by this Act , shall be privileged and shall not
be used as evidence in any action or proceeding
instituted against the company or any representa-
tive thereof by or in behalf of any person who has
been licensed under the provisions of this Act.
6. The Insurance Commissioner may suspend
or revoke the license of any life insurance agent if ,
after due investigation , notice and a hearing , either
before him or his deputy , he determines that such
license has been secured by fraud or misrepresenta-
tion ; or that the agent has violated any insurance
law of this Commonwealth ; or has made any mis-
leading representations and /or incomplete and/or
fraudulent comparison of any policies or compa-
nies or concerning any companies to any person for
the purpose or with the intention of inducing such
person to lapse , forfeit or surrender his insurance
then in force ; or has made any misleading estimate
of the dividends or share or surplus to be received
on a policy ; or has failed or refused to pay or to
deliver to the company or to his principal any
money or other property in the hands of said agent
belonging to such company or principal when re-
quested so to do ; or has violated any lawful ruling
of the insurance department ; or has been convicted

CHAPTER 94 421
of a felony ; or has otherwise shown himself un-
trustworthy or incompetent to act as a life insur-
ance agent .
Before the Insurance Commissioner shall revoke
or suspend any such license he shall give to the
agent and to the company which he represents writ-
ten notice of the charges and of the hearing , not
less than ten days prior to the time set for such
hearing . Such notice shall be forwarded by regis-
tered mail addressed to the agent at his known
address , and to the company at its principal place
of business . Full opportunity shall be given at
such hearing to the agent and to the company to
appear with counsel and be heard upon such
charges .
Within thirty days after the revocation or sus-
pension of license or the refusal of the Insurance
Commissioner to grant a license , the agent or ap-
plicant aggrieved may appeal from the ruling of
the Insurance Commissioner to the Franklin Cir-
cuit Court. Appeals may be taken from the judg-
ment of said court as in other civil cases .
§ 7. In the absence of a contrary ruling by the
Insurance Commissioner , license renewals shall be
issued from year to year upon written requisition
of the company without further action on the partof the agent.
$ 8. No officer or traveling salaried employee ofany insurance company not compensated on a com-
mission basis shall be required to obtain a licenseunder this Act .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
