in
all
itsprovisions
, especially when his attention
is

462 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1
called by the District Inspector or the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals to any violations
of the laws .
The superintendent shall keep on hand at the
mine a supply of the printed rules and notices and
record books required by this Act , which record
books shall be furnished through the District In-
spector or through the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals , on request of the operator or
superintendent in writing . He shall see that said
rules and notices and record books are delivered
to the proper persons at the mine , and that they are
properly cared for , and he shall also see that the
rules and notices are posted in conspicuous places
at or near the entrance to the mine and kept in such
condition that they will always be legible .
The operator or superintendent of every mine
shall at least ten days preceding send to the Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals notices
of the following occurrences :
First- When a mine is about to be abandoned .
Second-When the working of a mine is to be re-
sumed after an abandonment or a discontinuance
for a period exceeding two months . Said mine shall
be inspected and meet the approval of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals before operations be-
gin .
Third--When any change occurs in the name of
a mine , or in the name of the operator of a mine ,
under the provisions of this Act.
$ 40. Mine Foreman and His Duties .- In order
to better secure the proper ventilation of every coal
mine , and promote the health and safety of persons
employed therein the operator or superintendent
shall employ a competent and practical inside over-
seer , to be called mine foreman , who shall be a male
citizen and resident of this state , having had at

CHAPTER 100 463
least five years practical experience in the working ,
ventilation , and drainage of coal mines and who
shall hold a certificate of competency for such po-
sition issued to him by the Department of Mines and
Minerals , after taking an examination held by the
Department of Mines and Minerals under its rules
and regulations for such examinations . When the
mine workings become so extensive that the mine
foreman is unable personally to carry out the re-
quirements of this Act pertaining to his duties , the
operator or superintendent shall employ a sufficient
number of persons having had at least three years
experience in coal mines to act as assistants to the
mine foreman, who shall act under his instructions
in carrying out the provisions of this Act . The mine
foreman shall be responsible for their conduct in the
discharge of their duties under such employment .
The duties of the mine foreman shall be to exer-
cise general supervision over the ventilation , ap-
paratus and the airways , traveling ways and work-
ing places , pumps and drainage, and shall see that
as the miners advance their excavations proper
break throughs are made as required by law to
properly ventilate the mine . He shall also see that
the workmen are provided with sufficient props ,
cap pieces , and timbers of suitable size , which shall
be delivered to the working place when requisitioned
by the workmen . He shall also see that the props are
cut or sawed ( sawn ) as square as practicable at both
ends , and as near as practicable to the proper length
required or designated for the places where they
are to be used . The mine foreman shall see that the
water is drained so near as practicable out of the
working places , and that the working places are
kept as free from water as practicable during work-
ing hours ; and the owner , lessee , or operator of all
mines to which this Act applies shall see that the

464 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
water so drained or pumped from such mines shall
be drained as directly as practicable to the adjacent
streams or water courses by means of ditches ,
flumes , pipes , sewers or other adequate provisions .
It shall be the duty of the mine foreman to see
that the break throughs are made , as required by
law ; that the ventilation shall be conducted by means
of said break throughs through the rooms by means
of check doors or curtains placed on the entries or
other suitable places , and he shall not permit any
room to be opened in advance of the ventilating
current . The mine foreman shall measure the air
current with an anemometer to be furnished by the
operator at least once each month at the inlet and
outlet and at or near the faces of the advanced
headings , and shall keep a record of such measure-
ments in a book furnished and having a form pre-
scribed by the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals . Sign boards directing the way to
outlets or escape way shall be conspicuously placed
throughout the mine .
On single -track haulage roads in mines , where
haulage is done by machines , which roads the per-
sons employed in the mine must use while perform-
ing their work or while traveling on foot to and from
their work , there shall be places of refuge on one
side not less than three feet in depth from the side
of the mine car, and four feet wide , and not more
than sixty feet apart . On all haulage roads on which
haulage is done by draft animals , whereon men are
obliged to be in the performance of their duties or
when passing on foot to and from their work , there
shall be places of refuge not less than thirty inches
in depth from the side of the mine car , and four
feet wide, and not more than sixty feet apart . Pro-
vided however , that special refuge places shall not
be required in haulways on which room necks or

CHAPTER 100 465
break throughs occur at regular intervals not ex-
ceeding sixty feet , and thus furnish places of refuge ,
or on haulways in which the track is so laid as to
give a minimum clearance on one side of not less
than thirty inches from the top of any haulage
engine or any mine car, said clearance to be on the
side of the haulway opposite that upon which electric
wires are strung , in event any such wires be strung
in the haulways . No person shall travel on foot to
or from his work upon any slope used for haulage
or upon any haulage road when other roads in
proper condition for travel are available .
On all main haulways , where hauling is done by
machinery of any kind , the mine foreman shall
provide a proper system of signals , and a conspicu-
ous light on the front and a light or marker on the
rear of every trip or train of cars when in motion
in the mine .
It shall be the duty of the operator , superin-
tendent , or mine foreman to have bore -holes kept
not less than twelve feet in advance of the face , and ,
where necessary on the sides , if the working places
that are being driven are in dangerous proximity
to an abandoned mine , or part of a mine suspected
of containing inflammable gases , or which are filled
with water . If there be a map of such abandoned
mine , or part of mine , said bore -holes shall be start-
ed when the working places shall have approached
within fifty feet of such abandoned mine , but if there
be no such map , then said bore -holes shall be started
when the advancing working places are within one
hundred feet of where said abandoned workings are
supposed to be.
It shall be the duty of the mine foreman, or the
assistant mine foreman , of every coal mine in this
state , to see that every person employed to work in
such mine shall before beginning to work therein,

466 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be instructed in the particular danger incident to
his work in such mine , and furnished a copy of the
rules of such mine .
The mine foreman or his assistants shall visit and
carefully examine each working place in the mine
each day or oftener while the miners of such places
are at work and shall direct said miners so that each
and every working place shall be secured by props
or timbers where necessary . Should the mine fore-
man or his assistants find a place to be in a danger-
ous condition , they shall not leave the place until it
is made safe , or remove the persons working there-
in until the place is made safe by some competent
persons designated for that purpose.
The mine foreman shall see that every mine lib-
erating explosive gas is kept free of standing gas in
all working places and roadways . Any accumula-
tions of explosive gas or noxious gases in the worked
out or abandoned portions of any mine shall be re-
moved as soon as possible after its discovery . All
places in live sections that are temporarily aban-
doned shall be examined as live workings by the fire
boss on regular inspections . No person who may
be endangered by the presence of said explosive gas
or noxious gases shall be allowed in that portion
of the mine until said gases have been removed . The
mine foreman shall direct and see that all dangerous
places and the entrance or entrances to worked out
and abandoned places in all mines are properly
fenced off across the openings , so that no person
can enter , and that danger signals are posted upon
said fencing to warn persons of existing danger .
Any mine in which explosive gas can be detected
with a flame safety lamp shall require the services
of a mine foreman of the first class . Any mine in
which explosive gas is not present may employ the
services of a mine foreman of the second class .

CHAPTER 100 467
The mine foreman shall give prompt attention to
the removal of all dangers reported to him by his
assistants , the fire boss , or any other person work-
ing in the mine , and in case it is impracticable to
remove the danger at once, he shall notify every per-
son whose safety is menaced thereby to remain
away from the portion where the dangerous condi-
tion exists . He or his assistants shall , at least once
every week , travel and examine all the air courses ,
roads and openings that give access to old workings
or falls , and make a record of the condition of all
places where danger has been found , with ink , or
indelible pencil , in the record book provided for that
purpose .
The mine foreman shall notify , in writing , the
operator or superintendent of the mine of his in-
ability to comply with any of the requiremnets of
these sections , and it shall then become the duty of
any operator , or superintendent , to at once attend
to the matter complained of by the mine foreman
so as to enable him to comply with the provisions
thereof .
The mine foreman shall each day enter and sign
plainly with ink or indelible pencil , in a book pro-
vided for that purpose , a report of the condition of
the mine, which report shall clearly state any danger
that may come under his observation during the
day , or any danger reported to him by his assistants
or the fire bosses . The report shall also state
whether or not there is a proper supply of material
on hand for the safe working of the mine , and
whether or not the requirements of the law are com-
plied with.
The mine foreman shall, also each day , read care-
fully , and countersign with ink or indelible pencil ,
all reports entered into the record book of the fire
bosses .

468 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
In case of death or resignation of a mine foreman ,
the superintendent or manager shall select a com-
petent and certified man , if one be available ; and if
not, he may temporarily select any other competent
man who may serve if approved by the District In-
spector and the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals until the next examination . He shall ,
while acting as mine foreman be liable to the same
penalty as the mine foreman for any violations of
this Act .
It shall be the duty of the mine foreman to report
immediately all violations of this act to the opera-
tor or superintendent , who shall in turn report such
violations to the District Inspector for the district
in which the mine is situated.
The certificate of a mine foreman may be can-
celed and revoked by the Board of Examiners , if it
shall be established in the judgment of said Board
that the holder thereof has become unworthy to hold
said certificate by reason of violation of law, in-
temperate habits , incapacity , abuse of authority ,
wilful negligence to comply with instructions to
obey the requirements of the mining laws given by
a district mine inspector or other cause ; provided ,
that any person against whom charges are made
shall have a right to appear before said Board and
defend against such charges , and he shall have
five days ' notice in writing of such charges previous
to such hearing .
§ 41. Explosives .-No miner or other employee
shall take into any mine in this state any larger
quantity of powder or other explosive than he may
reasonably expect to use in any one shift , and all
explosives shall be carried into the mine in an in-
sulated or nonconductive , canvas , wood fibre or
other approved receptacle of a capacity not to ex-
ceed one shift's supply , which shall be properly

CHAPTER 100 469
closed with an approved top . All detonators or
electric squibs shall be carried into the mine in non-
conductive wood fibre or other approved receptacles
with approved top and of a capacity not to exceed
one shift's supply . It shall be the duty of each
miner to see that all explosives are separated from
detonators , squibs or fuses by at least twelve feet in
all working places .
No shots shall be fired in any place known to lib-
erate explosive gas until such place has been prop-
erly examined by a competent person who is desig-
nated for that purpose, and no shots shall be fired
in any place where gas is detected until said gas
has been removed by means of ventilation . After
firing any shot or shots no workmen shall return
to the working place until the smoke has cleared
away ; and before starting to work he shall make a
careful examination as to the condition of the roof ,
and do what is necessary to make the place safe be
fore begining to load coal , slate or refuse.
In no case shall more than one kind of explosive
be used in the same drill hole except in strip mining
operations and every blasting hole shall be tamped ,
full from the explosive to the mouth , and no coal
dust or infammable material shall be used for tamp-
ing . Where dynamite or keg powder is used the
method and procedure shall be approved by the
District Inspector and the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals .
When fuses are used , in no case shall fuses be
used of less length than the drill hole .
No explosives or detonators shall be hauled on
any trip operated by electric haulage motors , unless
enclosed in nonconducting boxes , containers or cars
approved by the District Inspector and the Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals . Trips haul-
ing explosives shall not carry workmen other than

470 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
those operating the trip , and explosives shall not
be hauled into or out of the mine within five minutes
preceding or following any trips in which men are
handled , and when traveling with the air current ,
the explosive trip shall precede : Provided , however ,
should a more safe method of transporting and
handling explosives or detonators be made available
after proper and sufficient tests and experiments ,
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall have the authority to approve the methods to
be used .
§ 42. Every miner shall thoroughly examine the
roof and general conditions of his working place be.
fore commencing work therein and if he finds loose
rock, slate or other dangerous conditions which he
'can remedy he shall take down such loose rock or
slate or securely prop same and otherwise make his
place safe before doing any other work therein .
During the progress of his work each miner shall
carefully watch his working place and if dangerous
conditions develop he shall either make same safe
or abandon the place until it is made safe . Provid-
ed , however , where the operator elects to do the
timbering it shall be the duty of the miner to for-
sake his working place until the place has been made
safe .
§ 43. No miner , workman or other person , shall
knowingly injure any shaft , lamp, instrument , air
course , or brattice or obstruct or throw open air-
ways , or carry matches or open lights in the places
worked with safety lamps or electric lamps or
disturb any part of the machinery or appliances ,
open a door used for directing ventilation and not
close it again, or enter any part of a mine against
caution or disobey any order given in carrying out
any of the provisions of this Act , or do any other
act whereby the life or health of any person em-

CHAPTER 100 471
ployed in the mine or the security of the mine is
endangered , or enter or be about any mine while
intoxicated or under the influence of alcohol .
§ 44. Maps and Plans .- The operator or super-
intendent of every coal mine in this state shall an-
nually and begin not later than five days after the
first day of January , make or cause to be made an
accurate map or plan of the workings of such mine ,
on a scale of not more than one hundred , two hun-
dred , three hundred or four hundred feet to the inch ,
showing the area mined and the forms of the exca-
vations up to the said first day of January , together
with the location and connection of the lines of all
adjoining lands within one thousand feet of said
excavations , and the name or names of each owner
or owners , so far as known, marked on each tract ;
a true copy of which map the said operator or super-
intendent shall deposit with the Chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals within forty-five
days after said first day of January, and another
copy of which shall be kept at the office of such
mine .
But after the making and filing with said Chief
of the Department of Mines and Minerals of the
map of the mine , as required herein , the operator
or superintendent shall only be required to annually
file with said Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals , within the time herein specified , such ad-
ditional map and statement as may be necessary to
truly show the progress of the workings , the amount
and forms of excavation of said mine , and so much
of the property lines as may be within one thousand
feet of said excavation as extended , from the date
of the preceding map or survey up to the first day
of January as provided herein . The Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals shall annually ,
before the first day of January , give warning notice

472 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
that said map is required . Provided , however , that
if for good reason the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals deems it advisable for any
operator or superintendent to furnish a map or
extension other than required in this section , the
operator or superintendent shall do so forthwith .
If the operator or superintendent of any coal mine
shall fail or neglect to furnish to the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals any copy of the
map or extension thereof as provided in this Act ,
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
is hereby authorized to cause a correct survey and
map or plan of said mine or the extensions above
indicated to be made at the expense of the owner ,
lessee , or operator of such mine , the cost of which
shall be recoverable from said owner , lessee , or
operator as other debts are recoverable by law ;
and if at any time the Chief of the Department of
Mines and Minerals shall have reason to believe
that such map or plan, or the extension thereof ,
furnished in pursuance of this Article is materially
incorrect , such as will not serve the purpose for
which it was intended , he may have survey and map
or plan or the extension thereof made or corrected ,
and expense of making such survey and map or plan
or extension thereof under the direction of said
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall be paid by the owner, lessee , or operator of
the mine , and the same may be collected as other
debts are recoverable by law ; but if the map , plan
or extension as furnished by the operator or super-
intendent shall be found to be correct , the expense
of said survey and drafting of map or plan shall be
paid by the state . The correctness of each map
or plan provided for by this Article shall be certi-
fied by the person making such map or plan , as
follows :

CHAPTER 100 473
I, the undersigned , hereby certify that this map
is correct and shows all the information to the best
of my knowledge and belief required by the mining
laws of this state , and covers the period ending

this day of
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals may reject any map as incomplete , the ac-
curacy of which is not so attested . The Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals shall , upon
the application of any owner , lessee , or operator
therefor , make or cause to be made a duplicate of
any map on file in his office of any mine owned ,
leased or operated by the owner , lessee , or operator
making such application , but the cost of making such
duplicate shall be paid by the applicant therefor .
In no case shall any copy of such map be made with-
out the consent of such owner , lessee , or operator ,
except that in any case where the owner or owners
of any land adjacent or near to the land of the
owner , lessee , or operator , may file an affidavit with
the Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
showing ownership of such land adjacent or near
to the land of such owner , lessee , or operator , and
that he verily believes that such owner , lessee or
operator is encroaching or mining on his or their
land of lands , whereupon it shall be the duty of the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals to
cause to be furnished to such affiant or affiants a
duplicate of map filed by such encroaching owner ,
operator or lessee , the cost of which duplicate shall
be paid by the affiant or affiants . When any coal mine

474 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
is worked out or is about to be abandoned , or in-
definitely closed , the operator of the same shall make
or cause to be made , a final survey of such mine , to
show the entire worked-out area at the time the
mine was closed , and the results shall be duly ex-
tended on the map of the mine previously made , and
a copy thereof filed with the Chief of the Department
of Mines and Minerals .
§ 45. Adjoining Property Owners .- No owner ,
or tenant of any land containing coal shall open or
sink, or dig , excavate or work in any coal mine or
shaft on such land , within twenty-five feet of the
land dividing said land from that of another person
or persons , without the consent , in writing , of every
person interested in , or having title to , such adjoin-
ing land in possession , revision , or remainder , or
the guardians of any such persons as may be in-
fants , nor without in addition thereto the approval
of the Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals . If any person shall violate this section , he
shall be liable to the person or persons injured there-
by, who may sue for same , in the sum of five hun-
dred dollars ; provided , that nothing in this act shall
deprive such injured person or persons from pur-
suing any and all other remedies now provided or
that may hereafter be provided by law for recovery
of damages for injuries done to their property .
$ 46. Coterminous Land Owners .- The owner ,
tenant or occupant of any land on which a coal mine
is opened and worked , or his superintendent shall
permit any person interested in, or having title to ,
any land coterminous with that in which such coal
mine is , to have ingress and egress with surveyors
and assistants to explore and survey such mine at
his own expense , and not oftener than once a month
for the purpose of ascertaining whether or not the
preceding section has been violated . Every owner ,

mine foreman's certificate of the first class , and who
has had at least seven (7) years of practical ex-
perience in the mining of coal , four ( 4 ) years of
which must have been in the mines of this state ,
and who is actually employed in mining coal at the
time of appointment , and one coal operator who is
at the time of the appointment actually engaged in
the position of operating a coal mine in the state ,
and who shall be recommended by the Chief of the
Department of Mines and Minerals , subject to the
approval and appointment by the Governor for a
term of four years each shall constitute the Board
of Examiners for the application for the certificate
of qualifications to serve as district mine inspector,
mine foreman, and fire bosses in coal mines .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals shall be chairman of the Board , and whenever
it may be necessary for him to be absent from any
meeting of the board, he may designate any other
member to preside during his absence . The ma-
jority of the members shall constitute a quorum , set
authority to transact business , hold examinations
and issue certificates .
To all persons possessing the qualifications re-
quired by this certificate for appointment as dis-
trict mine inspector , mine foreman and fire boss
and who have passed a satisfactory examination be-
fore the Board of Examiners a certificate shall be
given , in such form as may be prescribed by the
Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
certifying that said applicant has passed a satis-
factory examination and possesses qualifications by
this act required of the District Mine Inspector ,
mine foreman , and fire boss . Such certificates shali
be signed by the members of the Board of Exam-
iners .

CHAPTER 100 477
$ 49. Examination Fee and Certificates .-Before
taking the examination for district mine inspector ,
mine foreman or fire boss each applicant for the
examination shall , before he is examined , pay a fee
of two dollars and fifty cents ( $2.50 ) to the Auditor
of Public Accounts of this state who shall issue him
receipt therefor , which receipt the applicant shall
present to the board of examiners before he shall
be permitted to enter the examination and all fees
so paid shall be turned into the treasury to the credit
of the State Department of Mines and Minerals .
The certificates to be awarded a successful ap-
plicant for a certificate of qualification to serve as
district mine inspector shall show upon its face that
the holder is authorized to act as district mine in-
spector .
The class of certificate which may be awarded an
aplicant who has been examined with respect to
his qualifications to serve as mine foreman shall be
determined by the board of examiners according to
the nature of the examination taken by the ap-
plicant .
The certificate to be awarded a successful ap-
plicant for a certificate of qualification to serve as
fre boss shall show upon its face that the holder is
authorized to act as fire boss .
No certificate shall be granted to any person who
does not present to the board of examiners satis-
factory evidence , in the form of affidavits made by
well known and responsible persons in the locality
whence he came , that the applicant is a man of
sobriety and good moral character , and that he has
had at least five (5 ) years practical experience at
and in coal mines . Certificates may be granted only
to persons who are bona fide residents of this State ,
or who are employed at mines within the state . The
board is authorized to decline to examine any ap-

478 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
plicant who can not readily understand the written
English language or who can not express himself
intelligently in the English language .
The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals , as chairman, shall keep a record of all pro-
reedings of the board , including the names and ad-
dresses of persons who have been granted certifi-
cates and of those who have failed to pass the
examination .
Provided however , that the passage of this Act
shall in no wise effect the certification of those per-
sons already possessing certificates of qualifications
to act as mine inspector, mine foreman or fire boss
respectively .
If any district mine inspector , mine foreman or
fire boss should happen to have destroyed or mis-
placed his . certificate of competency to act as dis-
trict mine inspector , mine foreman or fire boss he
shall apply to the Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals for a duplicate certificate . Included
with the application must be an affidavit stating in
full how the certificate was destroyed or misplaced
and if said Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals shall deem it proper he shall furnish said
district mine inspector , mine foreman or fire boss
'with a duplicate certificate , provided , however , that
the person making request for a duplicate certifi-
cate shall pay a fee of two dollars ( $ 2.00 ) to the
Auditor of Public Accounts . All fees so paid shall
be turned over to the Treasury to the credit of the
State Department of Mines and Minerals .
$ 50. Duties of Inspector of Mine Weights .—It
shall be the duty of the inspectors of mine weights
in this Act provided for to inspect and test all scales
used in connection with any commercial coal mine
in this state ( railroad scales excepted ) . Said in-
spector shall determine the mechanical condition

§ 52. Check Weighman .- That when a majority
of the miners engaged in digging or mining coal in
any coal mine in this state , request the owner or
owners , or operator of any of said mines to allow
said miners to employ , at their own expense , a per-
son to inspect the weights at said mine , and see that
all the coal digged and mined by said miner is prop-
erly weighed and accounted for , and do and perform
only such other duties as will insure that said coal
is properly weighed and correctly accounted for ,
said owner or owners , or operator or operators ,
shall permit such person to be employed by said
miners making the request . Provided , the person
so employed has the reputation of being an honest ,
trustworthy , discreet , sober , and upright man. Said
check weighman shall be duly elected by a majority
of the employees engaged in mining and loading
coal and said election shall be properly conducted
by secret ballot at the principal entrance to the
mine .
The appointment under the provisions of this
Act of each check weighman shall be approved by
the County Judge of the County on presentation of
an affidavit stating that said person has been duly

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 81 of the Acts of the 1930
General Assembly , being subsection c of Section
2739g-2, Baldwin's Kentucky Statute Service , 1932
Supplement , be repealed and re- enacted , so that
when said section is re - enacted it will read as fol-

Automobiles primarily designed for carrying
passengers and having provisions for not more

be classed as passenger automobiles and the annual
registration fees for same shall be as follows :

power , Six Dollars ( $ 6.00 ) ; for each such car hav-

marily for transportation of passengers and amending and re-

Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2739j -27-1 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's revision of the 1933 Supplement
be and the same is hereby amended and re - enacted
and as amended and re- enacted shall read as fol-
lows :

494 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

and necessity , with a seating capacity of 7 passen-
gers or less , $2.00 for each passenger seat and 50c

hicle and $ 10.00 for the bus tags issued for every

ing capacity of 8 and less than 18 , $5.00 for each
passenger seat and 50c for each 100 lbs . gross
weight of said motor vehicle and $10.00 for the tag
issued for the bus by the Commission , as provided
by law .
On each motor vehicle having a passenger seating
capacity of 18 and less than 26 , $7.00 for each pas-
senger seat and 50c for each 100 lbs . gross weight
of said motor vehicle and $ 17.50 for the tags issued
for each bus by the Commission , as provided by law .
On each motor vehicle having a passenger seating
capacity of 26 and less than 30 , $8.50 per passenger
seat and 50c for each 100 lbs . gross weight of said
motor vehicle and $ 32.50 for the tags issued for
every bus by the Commission , as provided by law .
On each motor vehicle having passenger seating
capacity of 30 or more , $ 10.00 for each passenger
seat and 50c for each 100 lbs . gross weight of' said
motor vehicle and $50.00 for the tags issued for
every bus by the Commission, as provided by law.
§ 2. If any such motor carrier has paid for the
calendar year 1934 , a tax on the passenger seating
capacity of a bus in excess of the tax herein im-
posed , then such carrier shall receive credit for the

of the calendar year after this act becomes effective

who may become permanently crippled while in the

CHAPTER 110 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the second class to establish
• and maintain colleges by levying a tax for school purposes in
support thereof .

504 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Cities of the second -class are hereby author-
ized to establish or acquire by purchase , gift , lease ,
or other lawful conveyance , municipal colleges for
the purpose of promoting public education as de-
fined in this act ; and for the support and main-
tenance thereof the general council or other leg-
islative body of such city may annually cause to be
levied and collected , a tax of not less than five ( 5 ) or
more than seven ( 7 ) cents on each one hundred dol-
lars ' ( $ 100 ) worth of property subject to taxation
for city purposes .
§ 2. A municipal college within the meaning of
this act is a college established or supported in
whole or in part by funds raised by municipal taxa-
tion and controlled by a board of trustees appointed
by the mayor and general council or other legisla-
tive body of such city and affording instruction in
the arts , sciences , and professions and conferring
such certificates of attainments as may be authoriz-
ed by other like or similar institutions of learning
above the high school grade or standing .
3. Such levy of said taxes shall be made at the
same time and in like manner as other levies for
municipal purposes , and the amount so levied shall
be placed to the credit of the municipal college fund
upon the completion of the assessment of property
for taxation , and paid as collected , subject to such
discount as is allowed on other city taxes , by the
treasurer of the city to the treasurer or other finan-
cial officer of such college for the purpose of estab-
lishing , acquiring , maintaining , and operating said
municipal colleges . The taxes authorized by this act
to be levied shall be construed to be school taxes and
to be levied for school purposes and shall be in addi-

CHAPTER 111
.
AN ACT enabling fiscal courts of counties containing cities of
the second classes
, in their discretion
, to purchase policies of
insurance of all kinds on motor vehicles and other vehicles
operated by the county
, and compensation insurance to pro-
tect employees of the county against injuries received
inthe
course of employment
; provided the insurance carrier by its
policy waives the right to deny liability on the ground
of
-

506 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
county governmental capacity ; and to pay the premiums on
all policies so purchased out of the county funds.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the fiscal court in each county having
therein a city of the second class may , in its discre-
tion, for the protection of the public and its em-
ployees , appropriate county funds to purchase pol-
icies of insurance of all kinds deemed advisable , cov-
ering motor vehicles and other vehicles operated by
the county and compensation insurance covering em-
ployees of the county receiving injuries arising out
of and in the course of employment ; provided the
insurance carrier by its policy of insurance waives
therein the right to deny its liability therein by deny-
ing the liability of the county because of its govern-
mental capacity and immunity on that account .
§ 2. An emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this act shall take effect from and after its pass-
age and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 114 .
AN ACT providing for the payment of ad valorem property
taxes of fourth class cities , in two equal installments , due as
follows : July first and December first ; providing that the
second installment shall be deemed due and payable as of
the same day as the first installment in case the first install-
ment is not paid within thirty days after its due date ; pro-
viding that all taxes shall be deemed delinquent if not paid
on or before the first day of December following their due
dates ; prescribing penalties for delinquency; providing for
part payment of taxes before or after their due dates and dis-
count on taxes paid before they become due .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The city Council , Commissioners or other
governing bodies of fourth class cities , may pre-
scribe , by ordinance , duly enacted , on or before
July first of any year , as follows , to -wit : All annual
city taxes on all property subject to general levies
for annual ad valorem taxation shall be due and
payable in two equal installments . The first one-
half of such taxes shall be due and payable on the
first day of July after the assessment ; the second
one-half on the first day of December next succeed-
ing ; provided that , if the first such installment of
such taxes is not paid on or before the first day of
August , both installments shall be deemed to have
become due and payable on the said first day of July .

CHAPTER 115 519
The amount of all taxes remaining unpaid on the
first day of the month following that in which they
have become due and payable , shall be deemed de-
linquent , and such taxes shall bear interest at the
rate of six per cent ( 6% ) per annum from such due
date until paid , and a penalty of six per centum
(6% ) additional on the taxes so delinquent shall be
paid, provided that, if all installments shall be paid
in full on or before the first day of December , such
penalty shall not apply . Any taxpayer may pay,
and the Collector is hereby authorized and required
to receive , at any time , any amount in even dollars
to be applied in part payment of the taxes of such
taxpayer that may be due and payable , or as ad-
vance payment on account of such taxes to become
due and payable . A discount of six per cent ( 6% )
per annum shall be allowed on all amounts of taxes
so prepaid, for the time from the date of such pay-
ment until the date when such taxes would other-
wise become due and payable .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws inconsistent with
this act shall not apply where the Board of Com-
missioners or other governing bodies of any city
of the fourth class , shall have by ordinance given
effect to the provisions of this act .
3. This act shall take effect on the first day of
July 1934 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 115 .
AN ACT granting to cities or towns within the Commonwealth
of Kentucky the right to lay and maintain within the right-
of-way of any public road or highway in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky pipes or conduits for the purpose of supplying
water and/or gas to such cities or towns and the inhabitants
thereof and to persons in the vicinity of such pipes desiring
to use water or gas service therefrom ; providing the terms

520 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and conditions on which such right may be acquired and en-
joyed ; repealing all laws or parts of laws in conflict there
with , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That any city or town within the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , organized and existing by virtue
of its laws , is hereby granted the right and power
to construct , lay and maintain within the right-of-
way of any public road or highway in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky such pipes or conduits , as may
be deemed necessary by the legislative body of such
city or town, for the purpose of supplying water
and/or gas to such city or town and the inhabitants
thereof and to such persons in the vicinity of such
pipes or conduits who may desire to use water
and/or gas therefrom .
The right herein granted shall apply to any road
or public highway within the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , and shall include those owned by or under
the control or supervision of any County , the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , the State Highway Com-
mission of Kentucky or of any subordinate body ,
corporation or bureau now existing or that may
hereafter be created .
§ 2. The grant and right herein provided for
shall be acquired and held under the following terms
and conditions : The legislative body of any town or
city as hereinabove mentioned shall give the County ,
the Commonwealth , the Highway Commission or
other body , corporation or bureau having the control
or supervision of the road or highway affected not
less than 20 days ' previous written notice of its in-
tention to lay and maintain water and/or gas pipe
or pipes in the right -of -way of such road or high-
way, stating the name or location of such road or
highway , the contemplated use of such pipe or pipes ,

CHAPTER 115 521
the beginning and ending points of such pipe line
or lines , and , as nearly as practicable, the approxi-
mate location of the center line of such pipe line or
lines , the approximate width of the right -of-way to
be occupied by said line or lines, the approximate
depth same are to be buried in the ground , size of
pipe or pipes , and shall accompany such notice with
a map or plat showing the location , course and dis-
tance of such pipe line or lines .
At the expiration of said 20 -day period said city
or town may proceed to construct and lay said pipe
line or lines in such right -of -way and to do any and
all things necessary to complete said work and may
maintain said line or lines in said right -of- way
thereafter , but only after the execution and ap-
proval of the bond as hereinafter provided .
No such pipe line shall be built or maintained so
as to interfere permanently with the proper or
reasonable use , maintenance or enjoyment of any
public road or highway or of any pipe or pipes ,
bridge , culvert , telegraph , telephone or electric
service poles or lines or of any railway or of any
other structure or appurtenance lawfully main-
tained in , on or under the right -of-way of such road
or highway .
Any court of competent jurisdiction may enjoin
any improper of unlawful use of the right herein
granted .
Any town or city using or employing the grant
herein conferred may conduct and maintain water
and/or gas pipes under and across any public road
or highway , and for the purposes of this Act may
temporarily disturb such road or any appurtenance
or structure existing in connection therewith or in
said right -of-way , but it shall not block the ordi-
nary vehicular and pedestrian travel thereon ,
though it may provide , at its own cost and expense

522 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and at its own risk , suitable detours for travel dur-
ing the construction of its pipe line or lines . Such
city or town shall promptly restore, at its own cost
and risk, all injury to any road or highway or any
structure therein caused by laying its pipe or pipes
and shall be liable for all damage and injury to any
person , firm , corporation , County , the Common-
wealth or any State Commission or agency arising
in any manner from its exercise of any of the rights
herein conferred .
Before doing any work as herein provided , such
city or town shall file with the Fiscal Court of the
County or Counties in which it may be proposed to
construct said pipe line or lines a bond to the Com
monwealth of Kentucky , for the use and benefit of
whom it may concern , in a penal sum to be fixed by
such Fiscal Court or Courts , with surety approved
by such Court or Courts , conditioned that such city
or town will faithfully comply with each and all of
the terms and conditions of this Act , that it will be
liable for any negligence on its part and that it will
promptly restore all damage and injury occasioned
by the exercise of the rights herein conferred , and
as provided by this Act .
§ 3. All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed to the extent of such con-
flict .
§ 4. Whereas , several cities and towns of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky are in urgent need of
a supply of water and the most feasible and practi-
cable routes for the laying and maintenance of the
water supply lines is along the rights -of -way of
certain roads and highways in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and in order to conserve and promote
the health , safety , convenience and general welfare
of the inhabitants of such cities and towns , an
emergency is hereby declared to exist, and this Act

CHAPTER 117 523
shall take effect immediately upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved March 19 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 116 .
AN ACT for the benefit of Mrs. Alice Moneymaker , widow of
W. L. Moneymaker , who lost his life in the service of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , W. L. Moneymaker was a guard at the State peni-
tentiary at Eddyville , and while on duty was brutally assaulted
and fatally injured by a convict ; and ,
•WHEREAS , the said W. L. Moneymaker was receiving , at the
time of his death , a salary of one hundred ( 100 ) dollars per
month for his services as guard ; and ,
WHEREAS , he left a widow, Mrs. Alice Moneymaker , and two
minor children , namely , Woodrow Moneymaker and Mary
Belle Moneymaker ; and ,
WHEREAS , the said Woodrow Moneymaker , who has since
died, was a helpless invalid , and the said Mary Belle Money-
maker , now of age , was and is a semi -invalid ; and ,
WHEREAS , the family of the said W. L. Moneymaker was left
in indigent circumstances ; now , therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts is di-
rected to draw his warrant upon the State Treas-
urer for the sum of One Thousand ( 1,000 ) Dollars
in favor of the said Mrs. Alice Moneymaker.
$ 2. That this Act shall take effect from and
after its passage .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 117 .
AN ACT for acquiring , reconstructing , repairing and maintain-
ing private passways in this Commonwealth .

524 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That where two or more persons in this
Commonwealth have heretofore acquired or may
hereafter acquire by purchase , or otherwise , real
estate of sufficient width , but not to exceed twenty
feet , for the purpose of a passway to benefit their
lands along side of , or near the proposed passway ,
it shall be the duty of the owners of such passway
who are benefitted thereby to pay all the costs ex-
pended for said passway or the opening of same ,
and fixing the same for travel .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of those who own such
passways to bear jointly all the necessary expense ,
of repairing , reconstructing or maintenance , includ-
ing the necessary fencing thereof , to cause the nec-
essary work to be done , and the owners thereof to
pay jointly the expenses incurred and the collection
of such indebtedness may be enforced by a proceed-
ing in a court of equity and the lands benefitted by
those who such passway will be subjected to a lien
for the due portion of such expense that may be nec-
essarily incurred ; and by a proceeding in a court of
equity a sufficiency of the property of the delin-
quent owner may be sold as will be necessary to pay
his or her, or their portion of such assessment .
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 118 .
AN ACT providing that the state shall furnish books for mar-
riage licenses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 119 525
It shall be the duty of the county court clerks to
procure a record book in which shall be kept a rec-
ord of all the marriages , and said book shall be
paid for by the state in the manner provided in
Section 388 of Baldwin's Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition . Providing said books shall be
so furnished when and as the books now on hands in
the various county clerks ' offices of the state are ex-
hausted .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 119 .
AN ACT entitled An Act to require the State Inspector and
Examiner to inspect and examine into the management , con-
duct and condition of all counties of this Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the State Inspector and Examiner be
and he is hereby authorized and required once in
each year to inspect and examine into the books ,
accounts and papers of all county officers receiving
and/or disbursing public moneys belonging to the
counties , and he shall at all times have access to the
papers , books and records of any public officer of
such counties receiving and/or disbursing public
moneys of the counties . Said inspection shall be
made at time the Inspector and Examiner makes the
inspection for the benefit of the State and the County
shall bear its proportionate part of the actual ex-
pense of the inspection .
§ 2. If any such officer shall directly or indirectly
prevent or attempt to prevent or obstruct an ex-
amination into his official conduct , or the conduct or
condition of the office in his charge , or with which
he is connected , by the State Inspector and Exam-
iner , as herein provided, he shall be deemed guilty

526 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
of a high misdemeanor , and upon indictment and
conviction in the Franklin Circuit Court shall be
fined Five Hundred Dollars ( $500.00 ) .
§ 3. If any person shall prevent or attempt to
prevent or obstruct any investigation provided for
herein by said Inspector and Examiner , he shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and , upon convic-
tion , be fined One Thousand Dollars ($1,000.00 ) .
§ 4. The State Inspector and Examiner shall ,
within a reasonable time after the examination of
each county , make a written report to the Governor
of his work in each investigation , and to the County
Judge of the county which he has examined , calling
attention in specific terms to any mismanagement ,
misconduct or misapplication or illegal appropria-
tion or extravagant use of money received and/or
disbursed by any officer of the county so examined .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 120 .

of nudist colonies in this Commonwealth ; providing for the
payment of a license fee ; providing for penalty for the vio-
lation of this Act ; repealing all laws in conflict .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Nudists , as defined in this Act, means any
person or group of persons who display any part
of their private person naked before persons of the
opposite sex, not their husband or wife , at their
solicitation or with their consent for religious or
health purposes .
§ 2. Hereafter , any person, firm , corporation or
organization desiring to own or operate a nudist
colony must first secure a license therefor from the

thereon of One Thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) Dollars .

persons living in a nudist colony might be assured ,

made of brick , stone or cement around the premises
on which the colony is located .

CHAPTER 122.
AN ACT to repeal and re-enact section 3 of chapter 62 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1906 , being an Act entitled :
"AN ACT to authorize and empower railroad companies to des-
ignate persons to act as railroad police , providing for their
appointment by the Governor and defining their duties , pow-
ers and liabilities ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 3 of chapter 62 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of 1906 , now section 779a -3 , Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , be repealed and re - en-
acted so that when so re-enacted said section will
read as follows :
1. Every person so appointed to act as such
policeman shall , before he enters upon the dis-
charge of the duties of his office , execute bond , with
good security, conditioned for the faithful perform-
ance of his duty as such policeman , and take and
subscribe an oath of office and the oath required by
the Constitution of this State . Said bond shall be
executed in the county court of the county in which
such person resides , and shall be approved by the
judge thereof , who shall administer the oath of
office herein required , all of which shall be duly en-
tered of record by the clerk of said court . The exe-

CHAPTER 123 529
cution of said bond and the taking of the oath of
office as aforesaid shall be endorsed upon the com-
mission of the person so qualifying . The several po-
licemen so appointed and commissioned shall , in and
throughout the counties through which said railroad
operates , severally possess and exercise the power
of sheriffs and constables in making arrests , and the
service of process in criminal and penal prosecu-
tions , and shall be subject to all the liabilities of
such sheriffs or constables while in the discharge
of their duties as such policemen and for which
their security shall be responsible upon their bond :
Provided , however , that the powers herein con-
ferred upon said railroad policemen shall only be
exercised by them in the arrest and prosecution of
persons for public offenses committed upon or
about railroad property , to which extent the said
railroad policemen shall possess all the powers of
sheriffs and constables throughout the counties
through which the said railroad operates .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 123 .
AN ACT permitting S. G. Profitt as next friend of Marie Helton ,
an infant , to sue the Kentucky State Highway Commission
for her alleged wrongful injuries and providing for payment
thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That : Whereas , one Marie Helton , an infant
under the age of 21 years , and a resident of Harlan
county, Kentucky , was struck and seriously and per-
manently injured by a truck owned and operated at
the time by the State Highway Department of Ken-
tucky, on March the 19 , 1933 , while walking along

530 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the side walks of the city of Harlan, Kentucky , on
her way to church, and
Whereas , S. G. Profitt , a male citizen and over the
age of 21 years , and under no legal disability , is
qualified and acting as the next friend of the said
Marie Helton ,
And , whereas , no compensation has been received
by her , or through her next friend , S. G. Profitt , for
the alleged wrongful injuries as aforesaid .
Therefore , be it enacted by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky that said S. G.
Profitt , as next friend of Marie Helton , be and he is
hereby empowered to file a civil suit in the Harlan
Circuit Court against said Kentucky State Highway
Commission for the alleged injuries of said Marie
Helton and be and he is hereby empowered and per-
mitted to prosecute said civil action in the Civil
Courts of this State until finally terminated or other-
wise adjusted .
If, and when a recovery against the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or, and State Highway Com-
mission, is had , the amount of said recovery is
hereby appropriated and the auditor of public ac-
counts is hereby directed to draw his warrant upon
the treasury for the payment of same , that the re-
covery herein shall be limited to and not exceeding
$6,000.00 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 128 .
AN ACT to provide for the fixing of the salaries of deputy
sheriffs , deputy county court clerks and deputy circuit court
clerks in counties having a population of seventy -five thou-
sand (75,000) , and up to two hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in counties having a population of sev-
enty -five thousand ( 75,000 ) or over and up to two
hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) , the salary of the chief
deputy sheriff shall be fixed at a reasonable amount ,
not to exceed twenty-five hundred dollars ( $2,500-
.00 ) a year , and the salary of each of the other dep-
uty sheriffs shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding two thousand dollars ( $ 2 ,-
000.00) a year .
The salary of the chief deputy circuit court clerk
in said counties shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount, not exceeding twenty-five hundred dollars
($2,500.00 ) a year , and the salary of each of the
other deputies in the office of the circuit court clerk
shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding
two thousand dollars ( $ 2,000.00 ) a year .
The salaries of the first four deputy county court
clerks in said counties shall be fixed at reasonable
amounts , which need not be the same and none of
which shall exceed twenty-five hundred dollars ( $ 2 , -
500.00 ) per year . The salaries of the other deputies
in the office of the county court clerk shall be fixed
at reasonable amounts , none of which shall exceed
two thousand dollars ($2,000.00 ) per year .
$ 2. The salaries of the above named officers
shall be regulated and fixed within the limits herein
prescribed by an order entered upon the order book
of the circuit court and county court, and signed by
a majority of the judges of said courts ; and a certi-

540 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fied copy of said order shall , as soon as entered , be
forwarded to the Auditor of Public Accounts , as
shall a copy of any subsequent changes made there-
in.
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are to the extent of such conflict hereby repeal-
ed .
Approved March 2, 1934 .
CHAPTER 129.
AN ACT providing for the consolidation of the office of jailer
with that of sheriff in each county of the State .
§ 1. The office of jailer is hereby consolidated
with that of sheriff , in each county of the state ,
under the provisions of Section 105 of the Constitu-
tion . There are hereby transferred to and vested in
the sheriff , all the powers and duties heretofore au-
thorized by law to be exercised or performed by the
jailer . Wherever in any law of the State, reference
is made to the jailer , such reference shall be deemed
to apply to the sheriff , except where the context re-
quires otherwise .
§ 2. All laws and parts of laws in conflict with
this act , are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict .
3. This act shall take effect at the expiration
of the term of the jailers in office when the act is
passed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 131 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Sections 125b -1 and 125b -5 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 edition , both of which Sections
affect the office of Commonwealth's Attorney in districts
containing counties in the Commonwealth containing a popu-
lation of One Hundred and Fifty Thousand ( 150,000 ) or over .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections 125b -1 and 125b -5, Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby
are repealed and re -enacted , to read as follows :
125b-1 . That in all Counties of this Common-
wealth having a population of One Hundred and
Fifty Thousand ( 150,000 ) or over , there is hereby
created the office of District Detectives to the Com-
monwealth's Attorney ; said District Detectives
shall be three in number and shall be appointed by
the Commonwealth's Attorney for a term of four
(4) years , but may be removed at any time by said
Commonwealth's Attorney . And said district detec-
tives shall be paid a salary not to exceed twenty-
four hundred dollars ( $ 2,400.00 ) annually for each
detective .
§ 125b -5 . That the necessary expenses incurred
by the Commonwealth's Attorney in Counties in
this Commonwealth containing a population of more
than One Hundred and Fifty Thousand ( 150,000 )
when engaged in the discharge of his official duties

544 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be paid out of the county levy . It shall be the
duty of the Fiscal Court to order payment of the ex-
penses mentioned herein upon presentation of a bill
therefor by the Commonwealth's Attorney .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

1932 , being an Act to further regulate motor trucks , semi-
trailer trucks , semi -trailers and trailers , and enlarge the pow-
ers of the State Highway Commission , and cities and incor-
porated towns in respect thereof ; to provide for the enforce-
ment of the act and penalties for the violations thereof ; said
paragraph now being section 2739g -99 , of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's revision of the supplement to 1933 .

sion, and cities and incorporated towns in respect
thereof ; to provide for the enforcement of the Act
and penalties for the violations thereof ; said para-
graph now being Section 2739g- 99 , of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision of the Supple-
ment to 1933 , be and the same is hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 133 .
AN ACT providing that no conduct or representation made by
any officer or officers of any county , municipality or taxing
district of this Commonwealth shall be received as evidence
against or serve to estop any county , municipality or taxing
district thereof from contesting the legality of any tax or

CHAPTER 134 545
indebtedness authorized or created in excess of the consti-
tutional or legal limits relating to such indebtedness .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That no conduct , statement or representation by
any officer or officers of any county , municipality , or
taxing district in this Commonwealth , shall be re-
ceived as evidence against or serve to estop such
county, municipality or taxing district from con-
testing the legality of any tax or indebtedness
authorized or created by such county , municipality
or taxing district in excess of the constitutional or
legal limits relating thereto .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 134 .
AN ACT to repeal Chapter 142 , of the Acts of 1930 , entitled "An
Act relating to Chiropractors ; creating the office of and pro-
viding for the appointment of a State Supervisor of Chiro-
practors ; prescribing his qualifications , duties , and salary,
and authorizing him to employ assistants , and appropriating
funds to carry this act into effect ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 142 , of the Acts of 1930 , be
and is hereby repealed . Said Chapter reads as fol-
lows :
(1 ) For the better enforcement of Chapter 123 ,
of the Acts of 1928 General Assembly (being Sec-
tion 2643a-1 to Section 2643a - 16, inclusive , Kentucky
Statutes ) , there is hereby created the office of State
Supervisor of Chiropractors .
(2) The Governor shall within thirty days after
this act takes effect appoint one of the three mem-
Acts- 18

546 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
bers of the Kentucky State Board of Chiropractic
Examiners , who shall be known as State Supervisor
of Chiropractors . He shall be appointed and hold
office for a period of two years and until his succes-
sor is appointed and qualifies , and may concurrently
hold both offices . The State Supervisor of Chiro-
practors shall receive an annual salary of $ 2,400.00 ,
payable monthly out of the State Treasury , on a
warrant drawn by the Auditor . The said Super-
visor may have and use the same office at the State
Capitol Building provided for the Kentucky State
Board of Chiropractic Examiners by Chapter 123 ,
Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 .
(3) The duties of the State Supervisor of Chiro-
practors shall be to visit any or all Chiropractors
in this State ; to investigate all such practitioners
as he or the Kentucky State Board of Chiropractic
Examiners may deem advisable ; to ascertain wheth-
er the method of such practitioner is in violation or
compliance with law ; to render all possible aid to
the State Board of Chiropractic Examiners in en-
forcing the provisions of Chapter 123 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1928 ; and to make quar-
terly reports of his investigations and findings to
the Kentucky State Board of Chiropractic Examin-
ers for its consideration .
The State Supervisor of Chiropractors shall be
reimbursed for his necessary expenses out of the
Treasury upon warrant drawn by the Auditor upon
a presentation to him of an itemized statement of
such expenses , verified by the State Supervisor and
approved by the other two members of the Kentucky
State Board of Chiropractic Examiners .
(4) The State Supervisor of Chiropractors is
authorized to employ such clerical and field assist-
ants as may be authorized by the Kentucky State
Board of Chiropractic Examiners , and the salary

CHAPTER 135 547
and expenses of such assistants shall, to the extent
of the appropriation hereby made and not other-
wise expended , be payable out of the State Treasury
upon a warrant drawn by the Auditor , upon the
presentation to him of an itemized statement of such
salary and expenses verified by the claimants and
approved by both the State Supervisor of Chiro-
practors and the other two members of the Ken-
tucky State Board of Chiropractic Examiners . The
State Supervisor or his duly appointed assistants
are hereby given the right and authority to admin-
ister oaths , take affidavits , and examine witnesses in
matters pertaining to their duties herein.
(5 ) To carry into effect the provisions of this
act there is hereby appropriated out of available
funds in the State Treasury , annually , the sum of
$5,000.00 and no more .
(6) The various provisions of this act are
deemed separable and if any provision herein is
declared unconstitutional , it shall not affect the
validity or force of any other section or part here-
of not declared unconstitutional .
(7) An emergency is declared to exist and this
act shall be effective as a law from and after its
approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 135 .
AN ACT to Amend , Revise and Re-enact Chapter 116 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1930 , now Section 1761-1 Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1933 Supplement relating to
salaries of Commissioner and Deputy Commissioners of Cir-
cuit Courts in counties having a population of two hundred
thousand inhabitants or more , composing a separate Judi-
cial District and having six Circuit Judges , or more , clari-
fying said Act by making definite and certain the powers and
authority of said Commissioner , Court , Auditor and Treas-

548 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
urer in reference to the provisions of said Act requiring the
Commissioner to pay annually , in January , to the Auditor the
surplus fees in his hands after paying the salaries of said
Commissioner and his deputies and the expenses of said office
and permitting said Commissioner to make up out of the sur-
plus fees of any year any shortage in his salary or the sal-
aries of his deputies of any previous or subsequent year , and
providing the specific method and manner by which the pur-
pose of said act shall be carried out and made effective .
WHEREAS it is the purpose of said Act to enable said Court
to be served at all times by an efficient Commissioner of said
Court and an efficient organization of deputies and assistants
under said Commissioner to properly and with dispatch per-
form the duties of said office as the interests of the public
and of the litigants in said Court and the proper functioning
of said Court require , and , to that end , said Commissioner is
permitted by said Act to make up out of surplus fees col-
lected by him in any year any shortage in his salary , or the
salaries of his deputies , of any previous or subsequent year ,
and
WHEREAS said Act further provides that such surplus , after
the payment of his salary and those of his deputies and the
expense of his office , shall , annually in the month of Jan-
ary, be paid by said Commissioner to the Auditor of the Com-
monwealth , and
WHEREAS by reason of the extraordinary volume and charac-
ter of the business and litigation in said court handled
through said Commissioner during the past ten years or more ,
said Commissioner has been enabled to accumulate and pay
and has paid to the Auditor each year , pursuant to said Act
surplus funds remaining in his hands at the end of each of
said respective years , from fees earned by him, no part of
which has been drawn upon or used by said Commissioner ,
and
WHEREAS a doubt exists in the minds of the Auditor and
Treasurer of the Commonwealth and of said Commissioner
concerning their respective powers and duties under said
Act and as to the methods intended thereby , by and through
which the purposes of said Act may be accomplished so as
to enable such Commissioner to make up out of said surplus
fees of any year such shortage as may exist or occur from
time to time after said surplus has been paid or turned over
by him to said Auditor as provided by said Act ;
NOW , THEREFORE , to clarify said Act by making definite and
certain the powers , authority and duties of said Commis-

CHAPTER 135 549
sioner of said Court and of the Auditor and Treasurer of the
Commonwealth in reference thereto and by providing the spe-
cific method and manner by which the purposes of said Act
shall be carried out and made effective .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 116 of the Acts of the General Aș-
sembly of 1930 , now Section 1761-1 Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1933 Supplement , be and
the same is hereby amended , revised and re- enacted ,
so that the same when so amended , revised and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
(1 ) That the salaries of the Commissioner , the
Chief Deputy Commissioner, and the Deputy Com-
missioners of Circuit Courts in counties having a
population of two hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) in-
habitants , or more , comprising a separate Judicial
District, and having six ( 6 ) Circuit Judges , or more ,
are hereby fixed , as follows :
The salary of the Commissioner shall be the sum
of Five Thousand ( $5,000.00 ) Dollars per annum ;
the salary of the Chief Deputy Commissioner shall
not exceed the sum of Thirty -six Hundred ( $ 3.-
600.00 ) Dollars per annum ; and the salary of each
other Deputy Commissioner shall not exceed the
sum of Twenty -four Hundred ( $ 2,400.00 ) Dollars
per annum ; said salaries to be fixed by the said Cir-
cuit Court in General Term ; said salaries to be paid
out of the fees of said office of Commissioner, as
now provided by law . Said Commissioner shall , in
suitable books kept in his office , keep and enter there-
in a true and faithful account of the amounts earned
by him, or due him , for official services rendered to
any person , company , or corporation ; when and to
whom such service was rendered ; and what part
thereof has been paid , and when paid ; and shall
annually in the month of January, report to the

550 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Auditor , under oath , the amount received by him on
account of his official duties or position , from all
sources during the preceding year , as well as the
amount paid out by him for necessary deputies or
assistants , and for the expenses of conducting his
office , giving separately the amount he has paid out
on account of each item ; and if it shall appear from
said annual statement , to the Auditor that any such
Commissioner has received as compensation , on ac-
count of his official services from all sources , more
than Five Thousand ( $5,000.00 ) Dollars during such
year , after the payment of the salaries of his neces-
sary deputies or assistants and the legitimate ex-
pense of conducting his office , then such Commis-
sioner shall, with such statement , pay to the Auditor
the amount so received in excess of said Five Thou-
sand ($5,000.00 ) Dollars , and the salaries of his
deputies and the expenses of his office ; and any
such Commissioner who shall fail to comply here-
with shall upon conviction , be fined not less than
One Hundred ( $ 100.00 ) Dollars nor more than Five
Hundred ($500.00 ) Dollars ; and , upon conviction ,
shall be removed from office . But nothing here con-
tained shall be construed to prevent said Commis-
sioner from making up out of the surplus of any
year any shortage in his salary , or the salaries of
his deputies , of any previous or subsequent year .
(2 ) Whenever such shortage shall occur or exist ,
and the Commissioner shall have in his hands no
sufficient surplus fees to make up said shortage , he
shall thereupon , immediately after the last day of
the month in which said shortage occurs , file in said
Court , in General Term , a statement under oath
showing said shortage , and the amount thereof , and
the Court shall thereupon , by its order in General
Term , any four of said judges concurring , order
and direct the Auditor to issue , and he shall there-

CHAPTER 136 551
upon immediately issue , his warrant on the Treas-
urer to pay , and the Treasurer shall thereupon im-
mediately pay to said Commissioner the amount of
said shortage named in said order out of the funds
which , prior to the date of said warrant had been
paid by said Commissioner to the Auditor pursuant
to this Act, whether before or after this amendment ,
revision and re-enactment thereof, and by said
Auditor paid into the Treasury .
( 3 ) If any clause , sentence , paragraph or part
of this Act shall for any reason , be adjudged by any
Court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid , said
judgment shall not affect , impair , or invalidate the
remainder of said Act, but shall be confined in its
operation to the clause , paragraph , or part thereof
directly involved in the controversy in which said
judgment shall have been rendered .
(4 ) Inasmuch as a proper administration of
justice through the proper functioning of said Court
demands the continued and uninterrupted main-
tenance of an efficient Commissioner's office an
emergency is hereby declared to exist and therefore
this Act shall take effect immediately from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor as pro-
vided by law .
(5 ) All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 136 .

they shall also appoint an Assistant Superintendent

fications as required of the Superintendent of Pub-
lic Printing who shall assist the said Superintendent

of the Superintendent shall perform the duties im-
posed upon the Superintendent under the provi-
sions of this chapter . When this Amendment be-
comes effective the board of printing commission-
ers shall appoint an Assistant Superintendent of
Printing to serve until the time for appointment of
a successor to the present Superintendent of Public
Printing , who shall perform the duties and receive
the compensation provided for such Assistant
Superintendent herein. ”
So that when said language is stricken from said
section and same is amended and re -enacted it shall

66

and six , appoint as superintendent of public print-
ing , for a term of four years , a practical printer or

less than ten years ' experience in typesetting , esti-
mating the cost of plain , job , rule , tabular and ma-
chine composition ; press work , both cylinder and

1930 Edition , be amended and re-enacted by strik-
ing therefrom the following language :

shall receive as compensation for the performance
of his duties , as set out in this chapter , the sum of
eighteen hundred dollars ($ 1,800.00 ) payable month-
ly out of the state treasury . "
So that when the section is amended and re-
enacted and said language is stricken therefrom it
shall read as follows :
" The superintendent of public printing shall re-
ceive as compensation for the performance of his
duties as herein set out and such other duties as
may be assigned to him by the board of printing
commissioners , within the meaning of this act , the
sum of twenty-four hundred dollars ( $2,400.00 ) per
annum , payable monthly out of the state treasury .
3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
