CHAPTER 146 659
All the shipments of spirituous , vinous or malt
liquors to be paid for on delivery , commonly called
"C. O. D. shipments " , into any county , city , town ,
district or precinct where said act is in force shall be
unlawful and shall be deemed sales of such liquors
at the place where the money is paid or the goods
delivered ; the carrier and his agents selling or de-
livering such goods shall be liable jointly with the
vendor thereof.
§ 7. The provisions of this act shall not apply to
any manufacturer or wholesale dealer , who , in good
faith , and in the usual course of trade, sells , by the
wholesale , in quantities of not less than five gallons ,
delivered at one time , not to be drunk on the
premises ; nor shall the provisions of this act apply
to dispensaries , hotels , restaurants or clubs , unless
it be written in the petition , notice and order for the
election that provisions of this law and prohibition
shall apply to dispensaries , hotels , restaurants or
clubs ; if not so written , then licensed dispensaries ,
hotels , restaurants or clubs may sell for medicinal
purposes in the manner provided in this act .
§ 8. It shall be unlawful to sell by wholesale any
spirituous , vinous , malt or other intoxicating
liquors , regardless of the name by which it is called
(except manufacturers selling liquors of their own
make at the place of manufacture to a wholesale
dealer or a licensed retail dealer ) in any county ,
city , town, district or precinct where the sale of such
liquor has been prohibited by vote of the people
under the local option law. Any person violating
this act shall be deemed guilty of violating the local
option law , and shall be subject to trial and punish-
ment according to the provisions of the same and its
amendments .
§ 9. The county court shall not make the order
for said election until the persons signing the peti-

660 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion have deposited with the county judge , in money ,
an amount sufficient to pay for printing or posting
advertisements as provided for , and the fees of the
clerk making entries in the order book .
§ 10. (a) No election in any town, city , district
or precinct of a county shall be held , under this
article on the same day on which an election for the
entire county is held. When an election is held in an
entire county and a majority of the legal votes cast
at said election are against the sale, barter or loan
of spirituous , vinous , malt or other intoxicating
liquors , then it shall not be lawful to sell , barter or
loan any such liquors in any portion of the county .
If, at such an election for the entire county , the ma-
jority of the legal votes cast are in favor of the sale ,
barter or loan of any such liquors , said election shall
not operate to make it legal to grant license to sell,
barter or loan such liquors in any territorial division
of such county from which the sale , barter or loan
has been excluded by an election held under this
article but the status of such territorial division
shall remain as if no such election had been held .
(b) No election shall be held in any election pre-
cinct under this act on the same day on which an
election is held for the district or city of which the
precinct is a part . If at an election held for such
entire district or city, the majority of legal votes
cast shall be in favor of the sale , barter or loan of
spirituous , vinous , malt or other liquors , then the
status in the several precincts thereof shall remain
as it was before said election ; but if the majority
should be against the sale , then the sale , barter or
loan of such liquors shall be unlawful in every por-
tion of said district or city .
§ 11. If, at any election provided for in this act,
a majority of the legal votes cast are against the
sale , barter or loan of said spirituous , vinous or

CHAPTER 146 661
malt liquors , the sale , barter or loan of any such
liquors shall be unlawful in such county , city , town,
district or precinct, as the case may be, except as
provided , unless at a subsequent election held under
this act a majority of the legal votes cast shall be in
favor of the sale of said liquors .
If , at any election provided for in this act , any
person shall vote who is not entitled to vote under
the laws of this Commonwealth at elections for
county officers , he shall be punished as for a viola-
tion of the general laws on elections ; and all general
laws to protect the purity of elections shall be ap-
plicable to this act .
12. The election or elections herein provided
for shall not be held for any county , city , town , dis-
trict or precinct oftener than once in every three
years .
§ 13. It shall be the duty of the judges of the cir-
cuit courts to give this act in charge to the grand
juries of the counties within their jurisdiction .
§ 14. Any election held under this law may be
contested as provided for in this section :
1. The contest shall be heard and determined by
the same board which , by law , is authorized and em-
powered to hear and determine a contest of an elec-
tion for county officers ; and the same provisions of
the statutes shall apply to the contest of any election
held under this law as are provided for the contest
of any election for county officers , except as herein-
after provided .
2. Any number of the citizens and legal voters ,
but not less than ten of the county , city, town, dis-
trict , or precinct in which the election has been held ,
shall have the right to contest any election held
under this law, and shall be designated the contest-
ants. Such contestants shall, within ten days after
the final action of the examining board, file in the

662 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
office of the clerk of the county court a written state-
ment of the grounds of the contest , and shall cause
a copy thereof to be served on the county judge , and
shall give notice thereof by written or printed notice
to be posted at the courthouse door of the county ,
and in three or more public places in the county ,
city, town , district or precinct in which the election
has been held , and shall cause the same to be pub-
lished in some newspaper of the county , when pos-
sible , for two consecutive issues , commencing not
later than the first issue of the paper after filing the
statement . When a notice of the contest shall be
executed on the county judge , the certificate shall
not be recorded .
3. Any number of the citizens and legal voters ,
not less than ten , of the county , city , town , district
or precinct in which the election has been held , may
resist the contest by filing in the office of the clerk of
the county court a statement controverting the
grounds of the contestants , and may state any addi-
tional grounds to sustain the election , and they shall
be designated as the contestees .
4. Notice for the taking of depositions or other
proceedings in such contest may be executed on the
person whose name appears first as contestants or
contestee , which shall be deemed notice to all his as-
sociates . In case the required number shall fail to
appear as contestees , ex parte testimony shall be
competent before the contesting board .
5. The trial of the case shall be on the fourth
Monday after the filing in the county clerk's office of
the grounds of the contest ; but the board , for good
cause , may allow further time .
6. The decision of the board shall be given in
writing and signed in triplicate . One copy shall be
delivered to the contestants , and one copy to the con-
testees , and the other shall be delivered to the coun-

CHAPTER 146 663
ty clerk of the county in which the contest is pend-
ing , which shall be entered on the order book of the
county court ; and if the decision of the board be that
a majority of the legal votes cast at the election were
against the sale of such liquors , the entry of such
decision shall have the same effect as the recording
of the certificates of the examining board as herein-
before provided .
15. The contestants or contestees shall have
the right to appeal from the decision of the board to
the circuit court of the county where the contest is
pending, in the same way as appeals are taken from
the quarterly court to the circuit court ; an appeal
may also be taken from the circuit court to the court
of appeals .
§ 16. The cost of the contest shall be adjudged
against the unsuccessful parties .
ARTICLE IX
§ 1. All of Chapter 33 , Act of General Assembly
of 1922 , approved March 22 , 1922 , being Sections
2554a - 1 , 2554a - 2 , 2554a -3 , 2554a -4 , 2554a - 5 , 2554a - 6 ,
2554a -7, 2554a -8, 2554a - 9 , 2554a - 10 , 2554a -11 ,
2554a - 12 , 2554a - 13 , 2554a14 , 2554a -15 , 2554a -16 ,
2554a -17 , 2554a - 18, 2554a -19 , 2554a -20 , 2554a -21 ,
2554a-22 , 2554a -23 , 2554a -24 , 2554a - 25 , 2554a -26 ,
2554a -27 , 2554a -28 , 2554a -29 , 2554a -30 , 2554a -31 ,
2554a -32 , 2554a - 33 , 2554a - 34 , 2554a -35 , 2554a -36 ,
2554a -37 , 2554a -38 , 2554a -39 , 2554a -40 , 2554a -41 ,
2554a -42, 2554a -43 , 2554a - 44 , 2554-45 , 2554a -46 , and
2554a -47 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition ,
is hereby repealed .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with the
provisions of this Act are hereby repealed .
ARTICLE X
§ 1. The articles and sections of this Act are
severable , and if any of its articles or sections shall

664 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be held unconstitutional , such article or section held
to be invalid , may be rejected without affecting the
remainder of the Act and the decisions of the courts
shall not affect or impair the remaining articles or
sections of this Act . It is hereby declared as a
legislative intent that this Act would have been
adopted had not such unconstitutional articles or
sections been included therein .
It is hereby declared to be the legislature's inten-
tion in enacting this Act, that each article and sec-
tion is separate and severable , and that each article
and section would have been enacted separately .
ARTICLE XI
Whereas , the repeal of the Eighteenth Amend-
ment to the Constitution of the United States has
created an emergency which requires immediate
control of intoxicating liquors , and
Whereas , the present state revenue does not meet
the needs for the maintenance and development of
the State Government and its agencies and the
State's credit is threatened with impairment , an
emergency is declared to exist and this act shall be-
come a law and be effective on its passage and ap-
proval or non-action by the Governor , as provided
in the Constitution of Kentucky .
Approved March 17 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 147.
AN ACT making Jury Claims pay taxes due the State by the
holder of the jury claims.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the jurors serving on the Grand Jury
and the Petit Jury in this State shall receive $3.00

CHAPTER 148 665
per day for such service and if the State is unable to
pay in cash upon the completion of such jury service
the jury claim shall be good for State taxes due by
the holder of such jury claims and the Sheriff or
other collector shall accept them at face value on
any and all taxes due the State of Kentucky , pro-
vided , however , that such jury claims be honored
only on behalf of said juror and provided further ,
that this act shall only apply to jury claims bearing
date after July 1 , 1934 .
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict with the
above are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 148 .
AN ACT amending and re -enacting Section four thousand nine-
teen , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's nineteen thirty Edition
( Baldwin's Revision , nineteen thirty -three Supplement ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section four thousand nineteen , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's nineteen thirty Edition (Bald-
win's Revision , nineteen thirty -three Supplement ) ,
be amended by repealing said section in its entirety ,
and re-enacting it to read as follows :
An annual ad valorem tax for state purposes of
five (five ) cent each one hundred dollars ( one hun-
dred dollars ) of value of all real estate directed to
be assessed for taxation , as provided by law and
fifty cents upon each one hundred dollars (one hun-
dred dollars ) of value of all other property directed
to be assessed for taxation , as provided by law , shall
be paid by the owner , person or corporation as-
sessed ; except a tax at the rate of one-tenth of one
per cent ( one-tenth of one per cent ) shall be paid

666 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
annually upon the amount of deposits in any bank ,
trust company , or combined bank and trust com-
pany, organized under the laws of this State , or in
any national bank of this State , as now provided by
law ; and also including building and loan associa-
tions incorporated under the laws of this State , and
making loans to members only, which association
shall be assessed except as otherwise provided by
law in the manner and at the rate now set forth in
Chapter fourteen of the Acts of the extraordinary
session of the general assembly of nineteen seven-
teen ( Sections four thousand nineteen a- six, four
thousand nineteen a-seven , and four thousand nine-
teen a-eight ) .
The aggregate amount of taxes realized under
this Act shall be paid into the State Treasury and
credited to the General Fund to be distributed and
applied as may be provided by law.
All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith to
the extent of such conflict hereby are repealed .
Approved March 15 , 1934.
CHAPTER 149.
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , providing for an ex-
cise tax on the manufacturing business or occupation of manu-
facturing and /or selling and / or transporting distilled spirits ;
blending and /or rectifying , and / or mixing same or with other
distilled spirits , for such uses and purposes as may be per-
mitted by the laws of the State , providing for the adminis
tration of this Act by a Permit Department of the State Tax
Commission of Kentucky from which permits to engage in said
business or occupation shall be obtained before engaging in
same and providing funds for the expense thereof ; providing
for the regulation of and a license tax for transporting dis-
tilled spirits purchased without the State ; providing for dis-
tribution of said license or excise taxes ; providing penalties
for violation of its provisions and repealing Chapter 4 , Acts
of the General Assembly, Special Session , nineteen hundred

CHAPTER 149 667
thirty -three, and enacting in lieu thereof the following Act
and further repealing certain other Acts or Statutes and all
laws inconsistent herewith ; and declaring an emergency to
exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter Four of the Acts of the Special Ses-
sion of the General Assembly of 1933 be repealed ,
amended and re-enacted , so that when so repealed ,
amended and re - enacted , it shall read as follows :
1. "Person " as used in this Act shall mean
every corporation , association , co -partnership or
individual .
2. Every person engaged or proposing to en-
gage in manufacturing , selling and transporting dis-
tilled spirits in this State , for such uses and pur-
poses as may be lawful , shall obtain from the State
Tax Commission a permit to engage in the business
or occupation of manufacturing , transporting and
selling distilled spirits for said purposes and shall
at the time of the issual of said permit , pay to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , in addition to the other
license taxes provided by law , an excise tax of five
cents (five cents ) for each proof gallon of distilled
spirits for which said permit is issued , but all per-
mits heretofore issued under the Special Act of the
General Assembly of 1933 , effective September 26 ,
1933 , shall remain in full force , and effect , unless
otherwise provided by law heretofore passed .
3. (a ) Every owner or proprietor of a bonded
warehouse in the State in which distilled spirits are
stored , shall pay to the Commonwealth of Kentucky
a license tax of fifty cents ( 50c ) on every proof gal-
lon of distilled spirits manufactured prior to Jan-
uary 1 , 1928 , and two cents (2c ) on every proof gal-
lon of distilled spirits manufactured from January
1, 1928 , to the effective date of the repeal of the 18th

668 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Amendment , to-wit : December 5 , 1933 , which is
liable for tax to the Federal Government , as shown
by its official records , and the amount of tax due the
State shall be thereby fixed and measured by the
State Tax Commission .
The license tax collected under the provisions of
this Section insofar as same shall accrue prior to
July 1 , 1934 , shall be paid into the Treasury of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky through the Auditor
and credited to a fund to be used for the relief of the
poor and needy persons in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , to be expended at the discretion of the
Governor of the Commonwealth .
(b) Every person who is a proprietor of a bond-
ed warehouse , in which are stored such distilled
spirits as are described and defined in the foregoing
sub -section of this section , shall , on the first day of
January , May and September , each year , hereafter
and until said distilled spirits have been removed
from such warehouse , make a report to the Auditor
of Public Accounts in writing , sworn to by the per-
son making the report , or by its principal officer or
manager , if a corporation . Such report shall show
in proof gallons the quantity of such spirits on
which the Government tax has been paid , or has be-
come due , or transferred under bond , or removed
from the warehouse during the preceding four
months , and shall at the time of making said report,
pay to the Treasurer , through the Auditor the afore-
said tax per proof gallon, on such distilled spirits
reported on as above directed . All such distilled
spirits shall be subject to said tax and the State
shall have a lien for license tax due , which shall fol-
low any change of ownership .
§ 4. Every person engaged , or proposing to en-
gage in the business or occupation in this State of
blending, rectifying or mixing distilled spirits , and

CHAPTER 149 669
.
the sale and transportation of same , for such uses
and purposes as may be lawful , shall obtain from the
State Tax Commission a permit to blend , rectify
or mix distilled spirits and to sell and transport
same for said purposes . For the privilege of blend-
ing, rectifying or mixing said spirits and selling and
transporting same shall in addition to the license
tax provided by law pay an excise tax of five cents
(5c ) for each proof gallon of distilled spirits , blend-
ed , rectified or mixed , is hereby imposed ; provided ,
however, that said license fee ( five cents ) shall not
be required to be paid for such distilled spirits used
in said blending, rectifying or mixing .
(a ) As were heretofore manufactured in this
State or shall hereafter be manufactured in this
State , or
( b) Which have borne the tax imposed under
Sections 3 or 6 of this Act.
It shall be the duty of any person engaging in the
business or occupation of blending , rectifying or
mixing distilled spirits , as above set out , to report
on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month ,
in such form and stating such facts as may be pre-
scribed by the State Tax Commission , the amount
of distilled spirits , blended , rectified or mixed dur-
ing the preceding month , and at the time of making
said report , pay the said license tax for said privi-
lege as herein provided .
§ 5. ( a) It shall be unlawful for any person to
ship or transport or cause to be shipped or trans-
ported into the State of Kentucky , any distilled
spirits from points without the State , without first
having obtained a permit from the State Tax Com-
mission , and no railroad company or express com-
pany shall receive for shipment or ship into this
State any package or receptacle containing distilled
spirits , unless a copy of said permit showing that

670 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
payment of such taxes as are required by law have
been made shall accompany such shipment . Said
permit shall be in such form as may be prescribed
by the State Tax Commission , and all such ship-
ments into the State shall be governed by such rules
and regulations as may be promulgated by said
Commission, but said railroad or express company
shall not be required to obtain any permit to trans-
port distilled spirits , but shall be subject to all rules
and regulations promulgated by the State Tax Com-
mission .
(b ) It shall be unlawful for any person who is
permitted by law to manufacture and/or sell ,
and/or transport distilled spirits , to transport or
cause to be transported distilled spirits by any
means of transportation as may be prescribed by
the rules and regulations of the Tax Commission ,
other than /except , such spirits may be transported
by truck or wagon from and to freight or express
depots , to and from the place or places of business
of said permitees and upon the premises of said
permitees and from and to one place of business to
another place of business of said permittee , pro-
vided that the owner of truck or wagons transport-
ing distilled liquor as aforesaid , excepting trucks
and wagons owned and operated by a railroad or
express company , or by the person permitted by law
to manufacture and/or sell , and/or transport dis
tilled spirits shall procure a permit to engage in said
transportation and shall execute a bond satisfactory
in amount , form and as to surety , to be approved
by the State Tax Commission , conditioned upon the
lawful transportation of such distilled spirits .
$ 6. Every person who purchases distilled
spirits and has same shipped into the State of Ken-
tucky from points without the State , as provided in
Section 5 , hereof, shall at the time said permit is

CHAPTER 149 671
issued , pay a license tax thereon at the rate of five
cents (5c ) per proof gallon of such distilled spirits
contained in said shipment , provided , however , that
with respect to such distilled spirits so shipped into
the State as are used for blending, rectifying and
mixing purposes , such person so using same shall
not be required to pay the tax for the privilege of
blending , rectifying or mixing as is provided for in
Section 4 of this Act, to the extent such distilled
spirits so brought into the State is used for blend-
ing, rectifying and mixing purposes .
§ 7. It is hereby declared not to be the purpose
of this Act to impose a duplicate or double license
tax on the manufacture , sale , or blending , rectifying
or mixing , or transportation in or into this State of
distilled spirits , as measured by the quantity of dis-
tilled spirits , manufactured , sold , blended , rectified
or mixed or transported in or into this State in con-
formity thereto , and any other license tax imposed
by law heretofore passed is hereby declared to be
privilege tax or permit fee .
$ 8. This Act shall be administered and enforced
by the State Tax Commission through a department
hereby created to be known as the Distilled Spirits
Department , which said Tax Commission is hereby
authorized and directed to issue permits as provided
for in this Act , for the manufacture , sale and trans-
portation of distilled spirits and to enforce the
license tax provision of same and the collection of
the license taxes imposed hereby and to promulgate
reasonable rules and regulations for said purposes .
9. After payment of all expenses incurred by
the Tax Commission , in the performance of its
duties under this Act , all license taxes collected
under this Act except those accruing prior to July
1 , 1934 , Section 3 a , hereof , shall be paid into the
General Fund and , one-half of same shall be used

672 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to retire outstanding state warrants , and the other
one-half for general (fund ) purposes .
§ 10. On the failure of any person , liable there-
for, to pay the taxes imposed herein within 15 days
after the same has become due , he or they shall be
deemed delinquent , and a penalty of 20 % on the
amount of license tax due shall attach , and the
Auditor shall at once cause such proceeding to be
instituted for the collection of such license , with
such interest and penalties as may be provided by
law for the collection of other taxes .
§ 11. Any person who shall violate any of the
provisions of this Act shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor and upon conviction be fined not less than
$ 500.00 nor more than $ 1,000.00 , and any permit
which has been issued to such person so convicted ,
shall be revoked and may not be again issued to such
person for a period of two years .
12. No suit shall be maintained in any court
to restrain or delay the collection or payment of the
tax levied by this Act . The aggrieved taxpayer shall
pay with or without protest the tax as and when
required and may at any time within two years
from the date of such payment sue the Common-
wealth through its agent , the Auditor of Public Ac-
counts , in an action at law in any court, state or
federal , otherwise having jurisdiction of the par-
ties and subject matter , for the recovery of the tax
paid with legal interest thereon from the date of
payment . If it is finally determined that said tax
or any part thereof was wrongfully collected , for
any reason , it shall be the duty of the Auditor of
Public Accounts to issue his warrant on the Treas-
urer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the
amount of such tax so adjudged to have been wrong-
fully collected together with legal interest thereon .
The Treasurer shall pay the same at once out of the

CHAPTER 149 673
general expenditure fund of the State in preference
to other warrants or claims against the Common-
wealth . A separate suit need not be filed for each
individual payment made by any taxpayer , but a
recovery may be had in one suit for as many pay-
ments as may have been made .
§ 13. The provisions of this Act are severable ,
and if any of its provisions or any penalty provided
by this Act shall be held unconstitutional , such pro-
vision or the particular penalty held to be invalid
may be rejected without affecting the remainder of
the Act or such part of the penalties as are not held
to be invalid , and the decisions of the courts shall
not affect or impair the remaining provisions of this
Act or any of the other penalties anywhere in this
Act provided . It is hereby declared as a legislative
intent that this Act would have been adopted had
not such unconstitutional provisions of such invalid
penalties been included therein .
14. The following acts or statutes are hereby
repealed : ( 1 ) Chapter 166 of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of 1926 , as amended by Chapter 138
of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 , being
Sections 4214a- 1 to 4200 -a - 8 , inclusive , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , imposing a license
tax on proprietors of a bonded warehouse .
( 2 ) Chapter 4 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1933 , Special Session , being Sections 4214a - 1
to 4214a -8 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1933 Sup-
plement , imposing a license tax on the privilege of
manufacturing distilled spirits and the withdrawal
of spirits theretofore distilled .
(3) The Acts of the General Assembly , 1906 ,
Special Session , being Sections 4114a to 4114 -g ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , imposing
a license tax on the business or occupation of com-
Acts- 22

674 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pounding , rectifying , adulterating or blending dis-
tilled spirits .
(4) Chapter 165 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly , 1926 Session , being Sections 4214b -1 to
4214 -bq- 1-0 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edi-
tion , imposing a license tax of 50c per pint upon
retail sales .
( 5 ) That the sub- o - section of subdivision 2 ,
Article 12 , of Chapter 22 , of the Acts of the General
Assembly, 1906 Session , relating to and entitled
"Wholesale Liquor Dealers ' , being an unnumbered
sub - section of 4224 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 Edition , relating to and entitled " License tax
to be paid by ; liquor dealers who rectify , compound
or blend ; sale of wine , ale , mineral waters " , be, and
the same is hereby repealed in so far as it relates
and imposes a license tax on the sale by wholesale
of distilled spirits or compounded , rectified , or
blended distilled spirits .
( 6 ) All laws in conflict herewith .
§ 15. Whereas , the present state revenue does
not meet the needs for maintenance and develop-
ment of the State Government , and its agencies and
the State's credit is threatened with impairment , an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become a law and be effective on its passage and
approval or non -action by the Governor , as pro-
vided by the Constitution of Kentucky .
Approved March 17 , 1934 .
Ruby Laffoon , Governor of Kentucky .

.

, Kentucky Statutes

, of

CHAPTER 152.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 4036 Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the lien of a purchaser at
an invalid tax sale , amending and re-enacting the same as
to enforcement of such llen .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4036 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby amended by
adding thereto the following proviso :
Such lien may be enforced against such property
by action as are other liens , at any time after the
invalid sale but prior to the expiration of the lien
by reason of limitation , whether the purchaser be
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and other taxing
districts or any other person , firm or corporation .

CHAPTER 153 677
Section 4036. Lien of Purchaser of Sale Set
Aside ; Enforcement .- Whenever any person shall
purchase property sold for delinquent taxes , and
the sale shall be set aside because of any irregular-
ity, except where owner has paid his taxes and has
receipt for same , the purchaser shall have a lien on
the property for the amount of taxes and cost paid
by him , and for which the property is liable , with
legal interest from the time of such employment
which may be recovered from the owner of the prop-
erty or person owning the same . Such lien may be
enforced against such property by action as are
other liens , at any time after the invalid sale but
prior to the expiration of the lien by reason of limi-
tation , whether the purchaser be the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and other taxing districts or any other
person , firm or corporation .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

others ; defining the organization, powers and duties of of
ficers , departments , boards , commissions , and other agencies

WHEREAS , many of said boards , officers , commissions and

powers , involving a needless expenditure of public funds ; and
WHEREAS , by merging some , abolishing others and creating
certain others by a combination of two or more existing agen-
cies , or boards , a more efficient and economical governmental
service can be rendered the State , now, therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I- SHORT TITLE AND DEFINITIONS
§ 1. Short Title . This Act shall be known as
the " Administrative Reorganization Act ."
§ 2. Definitions of Terms . In this Act, except
where the context requires otherwise , the words and
expressions defined in this section shall be held to
have the meaning here given to them :
" Administrative Department " means any agency
of the State named in this Act as an administrative
department .
"Agency" means any State officer , department ,
board , commission , institution , division , or other
person or functional group heretofore existing or
hereby or hereafter created , that is authorized to
exercise , or that does exercise any function of gov-
ernment of the State .
" Constitution " means the Constitution of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 155 683
"Division " means a branch of an administrative
department established hereunder or by administra-
tive action under authority hereof, for the conve-
nient performance of functions committed to a de-
partment by ths Act.
"Employee" means the lawful incumbent of a
position as defined herein .
" Functions " means jurisdiction , powers , duties ,
rights and obligations conferred or imposed by law ,
or exercised , performed , or discharged by adminis-
trative authority and not in contravention of any
provision of law.
"Independent agency " means any agency of the
executive department of the State government that
is not a part of an administrative department .
"Law" means the general and special laws of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
"Position " means any office or employment , or
combination of them , in the State service , involving
an aggregation of duties to be performed and re-
sponsibilities to be exercised by one person .
"State" means the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
"State service " means the organization of the
legislative , executive and judicial departments of
the State government , as defined by the Constitu-
tion , taken together .
ARTICLE II- ADMINISTRATIVE DEPARTMENTS
AND AGENCIES
§ 1. Departments Specified . All executive and
administrative functions of the State shall be di-
vided , in the manner hereinafter provided , among
the Governor and the Administrative departments
and independent agencies hereby created and estab-
lished , which shall be as follows :

ARTICLE III-THE GOVERNOR
1. The Governor shall continue to exercise all
the powers , duties and functions as now vested in
his office by the Constitution and laws of the State
with such modifications and additions as may be
provided by law.

CHAPTER 155 685
ARTICLE IV-DEPARTMENT OF STATE
§ 1. The head of the Department of State shall
be the Secretary of State , who shall exercise all
the powers , duties and functions , as now vested in
his office by the Constitution and laws of the State
with such modifications and additions as may be
provided by law.
§ 2. All functions heretofore vested in the Audi-
tor of Public Accounts in relation to the Land Office
are hereby transferred to and vested in the Secre-
tary of State .
ARTICLE V- DEPARTMENT OF LAW
§ 1. The head of the Department of Law shall
be the Attorney General who shall exercise all the
powers , duties and functions now vested in his of-
fice by the Constitution and laws of the State with
such modifications and additions as may be pro-
vided by law.
§ 2. Uniform State Law Commission . The Uni-
form State Law Commission is hereby abolished .
ARTICLE VI-DEPARTMENT OF THE TREASURY
§ 1. The head of the Department of the Treas-ury shall be the Treasurer who shall exercise all
the powers , duties and functions now vested in his
office by the Constitution and laws of the State , with
such modifications and additions as may be pro-
vided by law.
§ 2. The Treasurer shall , in addition to the
above enumerated duties , perform the duties now
imposed and vested in the Deputy Commissioner of
Insurance and the Auditor of Public Accounts , as
provided by Sections 762-1 to 762-15 , inclusive , re-
lating to the custody and deposit of stocks , bonds ,

686 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
notes , real estate , mortgages or other evidences of
indebtedness or ownership of property by Insur-
ance Companies doing business in this State , and
to this end , Sections 762-1 , 762-2 , 762-3 , 762-4 , 762-5 ,
762-6 , 762-7 , 762-8 , 762-9 , 762-10 , 762-11 , 762-12 ,
762-13 and 762-15 of the Kentucky Statutes are
hereby repealed , amended and re -enacted and when
repealed , amended and re -enacted shall read as fol-
lows , to -wit :
" Every Insurance Company doing business in the
State of Kentucky , and now required by law or
hereinafter required by law to make deposits of
stocks , bonds , notes , real estate , mortgages or other
evidences of indebtedness or ownership of property
for the protection and benefit of its policy holders ,
shall make such deposits with the State Treasurer
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , who shall hold
them in his official capacity for the security and
benefit of the policy holders of the company mak-
ing such deposits and also as trustees for the In-
surance Company.
The Insurance Commissioner shall value said se-
curities , properties or evidence of indebtedness at
their fair value when deposited , and if at any time
in the opinion of the Commissioner of Insurance ,
the fair value of said securities or properties shall
have declined below the value first fixed , the Com-
missioner of Insurance shall, in writing by regis-
tered mail , notify the Insurance Company at its
home office of said fact and shall require it within
thirty days to deposit other securities or properties
whose fair value shall be determined by the Insur-
ance Commissioner and shall in no event be less
than the fair value of the securities when first
deposited .

CHAPTER 155 687
If any such Insurance Company declines or fails.
to make the deposit as herein provided , its right to
do business in the Commonwealth of Kentucky
shall cease upon written notice to it by either the
Commissioner of Insurance or the Treasurer , and
the Commissioner of Insurance , in charge of insur-
ance , shall notify all agents of said Company au-
thorized to act for it of that fact and shall also im-
mediately notify the Insurance Commissioner of all
States where the company is authorized to do busi-
ness of that fact .
The Treasurer may designate the following
places in the Commonwealth of Kentucky where the
securities or properties herein authorized to be de-
posited shall be kept , to -wit : At least one and not
more than three depositories shall be selected in
every County containing a city of the first class
and one depository shall be designated in the city
where the State Capitol is located and such securi-
ties or properties shall be kept in the vault of some
solvent bank or trust company at these places and
none of the securities or properties thus deposited
shall be removed from said vault of said bank or
trust company unless upon the written order of the
acting President and Secreteary or Treasurer or of
the Directors of the Company making the deposit ,
approved by the Treasurer or upon the order or by
authority of some court of competent jurisdiction ,
and the receptacle or box in which such securities
or properties are kept shall not be opened or any
securities or properties withdrawn therefrom or de-
posited therein without the Treasurer or his repre-
sentative and an authorized representative of the
Insurance Company being present and consenting
thereto .

of the Insurance Company owning such securities

Company.
Each and every Insurance Company depositing

debtedness .
The Treasurer and the Insurance Commissioner

showing the kind of property or securities deposit-
ed by each Insurance Company , the face value
thereof, the value as determined by the Insurance
Commissioner, the date and where deposited and
when withdrawn in the manner prescribed by the

a complete record showing the name of the com-

to withdraw securities , the company withdrawing

urer to deliver to any Insurance Company , the se-

ARTICLE VII-DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC ACCOUNTS
The head of the Department of Public Accounts
shall be the Auditor who shall exercise all the pow-
ers , duties and functions now vested in his office
by the Constitution and laws of the State , with such
modifications and additions as may be provided by
law .
ARTICLE VIII-DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE , LABOR
AND STATISTICS
§ 1. The Department of Agriculture , Labor and
Statistics shall consist of the Commissioner of Agri-
culture, Labor and Statistics , the Board of Agricul-
ture and such subordinate divisions as may from
time to time be established under the provisions of
this Act. It shall have and exercise all of the ad-

CHAPTER 155 693
�
ministrative functions of the State in relation to
the promotion of the interest of Agriculture in all
of its branches . The conservation of natural re-
sources , including forests and wild life , and the pro-
tection of the interests of labor , except such func-
tions as are vested in the Workmen's Compensa-
tion Board . The Commissioner of Agriculture , La-
bor and Statistics shall also exercise all the powers ,
duties and functions now vested in his office by the
Constitution and laws of the State .
$ 2. State Board of Agriculture . The State
Board of Agriculture as now constituted shall be
continued , but is hereby attached to and made a
part of the Department of Agriculture , Labor and
Statistics . It shall continue to manage and control
the State Fair and to preform such other functions
as provided by law.
3. Livestock Division . There shall be in this
Department , a Division of Livestock , which division
shall have charge of all matters relating to live-
stock , and the Board of Veterinary Examiners and
the Livestock Sanitary Board shall be continued as
provided by law and be a part of this division .
4. Game and Fish . The Game and Fish Com-
mission is hereby transferred to the Department of
Agriculture , Labor and Statistics and attached
thereto and made a division thereof , and shall con-
tinue to exercise all of the functions , powers and
duties vested in said Commission by law.
The Commissioner of Agriculture , Labor and
Statistics shall be an ex-officio member of the Game
and Fish Commission . The present members of
said Game and Fish Commission shall continue in
office until their respective terms expire and their
successors shall be appointed in the manner pro-
vided by law .

694 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ARTICLE IX-DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION
The head of the Department of Education shall
be the Superintendent of Public Instruction , who
shall exercise all powers , duties and functions vest-
ed in his office by the Constitution and laws of the
State .
There is hereby created the Department of Edu-
cation , which shall consist of the State Board of
Education and the Superintendent of Public In-
struction , as provided by law , which said Depart-
ment of Education shall have and exercise all ad-
ministrative functions of the State in relation to
the management and control of the public common
schools , of vocational education and rehabilitation ,
and of the State institutions of higher education for
negroes . The University of Kentucky , the Eastern
Kentucky State Teachers College , the Western
Kentucky State Teachers College , the Morehead
State Teachers College , and the Murray State
Teachers College , Board of Commissioners of the
Kentucky School for Deaf , Board of Visitors for
the Blind, with their governing boards , and the
State Textbook Commission, as heretofore consti-
tuted , shall be included in the Department of Edu-
cation and shall cosntitute divisions thereof, but
shall continue to exercise all the functions conferred
upon them by law in relation to their respective
institutions .
There are hereby abolished the following :
Board of Trustees of Kentucky State Industrial
College for Colored Persons .
The Vocational Education Board .
Kentucky Educational Commission .
Board of Trustees of West Kentucky Industrial
College and all functions heretofore vested in said
above named boards and commissions are hereby

CHAPTER 155 695
transferred to and vested in the Department of
Education , as herein constituted .
ARTICLE X- DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH
§ 1. The Department of Health shall have and
exercise all administrative functions of the State in
relation to food and drug control , sanitation and
vital statistics , the appointment and control of the
County Board of Health , the prevention and con-
trol of communicable diseases , and the licensing and
regulating the practice of the profession and occu-
pations for the protection of public health , and all
other functions of the State in relation to public
health, except as herein otherwise provided .
§ 2. The Kentucky Crippled Children's Com-
mission , the State Board of Examiners for Nurses ,
the Board of Barber and Beautician Examiners , the
Board of Chiropractor Examiners , the State Board
of Dental Examiners , the Board of Embalming of
Kentucky and the Kentucky Board of Pharmacy
are transferred to and shall be included in the De-
partment of Public Health and constitute divisions
thereof , but each of said boards shall continue to
exercise all of the powers , rights and functions with
relation to their respective businesses or profes-
sions , and their successors in office shall be appoint-
ed in the manner and form as provided by law , but
shall exercise no other powers or functions with re-
lation to said Department of Health .
§ 3. The head of the Department of Health shall
be the Commissioner of Health . The State Health
Officer , now in office , shall be the first Commission-
er under this Act , and shall so serve until the ex-
piration of the term for which he was appointed
State Health Officer , and his successors shall be
selected as provided by law. Each successor so

ARTICLE XII-DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE AND
TAXATION
1. The Department of Revenue and Taxation
shall exercise all of the administrative functions of
the State in relation to the State revenue and tax
laws , licensing and registering of motor vehicles ,
the equalization of tax assessments , the assessment
of public utilities for tax , the assessment of fran-
chises , superivision of tax collections and all other
administrative functions of the State in relation to
revenue and taxation .
$ 2. State Tax Commission . The State Tax
Commission shall be continued as heretofore and
shall have all the powers , duties and functions vest-
ed in said State Tax Commission under the Con-
stitution and laws of the State , with such modifica-
tions and additions as herein provided .
The present members of the State Tax Commis-
sion shall continue in office to the end of their re-
spective terms , and their successors shall be ap-
pointed in the manner and form as provided by law .
The Tax Commission shall have power to create
such divisions within the Department as may be
necessary for the proper and efficient functioning
of the Department of Revenue and Taxation .
The§ 3. State Athletic Board of Control .
State Athletic Board of Control shall be included
in the Department of Revenue and Taxation and
constitute a division hereof . Said Board shall be
governed as provided by law and the successors of
the present members of said Board shall be appoint-
ed as so provided .
ARTICLE XIII-DEPARTMENT OF HIGHWAYS
The Department of Highways shall consist of the
State Highway Commission of Kentucky as consti-

CHAPTER 155 699
tuted , and same shall continue to function as pre-
scribed by law .
ARTICLE XIV-DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WELFARE
§ 1. Department of Public Welfare . The De-
partment of Public Welfare shall be continued with
such modifications and additions as may be pro-
vided by law .
The Ken-§ 2. Kentucky Children's Bureau .
tucky Children's Bureau is hereby abolished , and
all duties and functions heretofore performed by
said Bureau are hereby transferred to and vested
in the Department of Public Welfare , and said De-
partment shall have a division to perform the func-
tions of the Kentucky Children's Bureau , and the
present Commissioner in charge of said Bureau is
transferred to the Department of Public Welfare .
ARTICLE XV-DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE
§ 1. Department of Insurance . There is hereby
created a Department of Insurance , consisting of a
Commissioner of Insurance and a deputy Commis-
sioner , who shall be appointed by the Governor of
the Commonwealth on the effective date of this Act ,
to serve during the will and pleasure of the Gover-
nor , and their successors in office shall be appointed
to hold office in like manner. The Commissioner of
Insurance and Deputy Commissioner shall be citi-
zens of Kentucky , at least 30 years of age ; and the
qualifications for such appointments shall include
four years of responsible experience in the manage-
ment of substantial public or private enterprises .
The Commissioner of Insurance shall receive an an-
nual salary of Five Thousand ( $ 5,000.00 ) Dollars
per year ; and the deputy Commissioner shall re-
ceive a salary of Four Thousand, Two Hundred

700 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
($4,200.00 ) Dollars per year, payable monthly as
other state salaries are paid .
The Commissioner of Insurance and the Deputy
Commissioner shall qualify by taking the consti-
tutional oath of office and by executing bonds to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky for the faithful per-
formance of the duties imposed upon them by law ,
which bonds shall be renewable annually or oftener
if required by the Governor . In the case of the
Commissioner of Insurance the bond shall be in the
penal sum of $ 100,000.00 ; and the Deputy Commis-
sioner shall execute bond in the sum of $ 50,000.00 .
The premiums on said bonds shall be paid as other
expenses of the Department .
§ 2. Department of Insurance . All functions ,
duties and authority heretofore vested in the Audi-
tor of Public Accounts , Insurance Department , the
Insurance Commissioner of the State of Kentucky ,
the Auditor of Public Accounts as Fire Marshall
and Superintendent of Fire Insurance Rates , as to
the supervision of fire insurance , rate making , and
for the examination and prohibiting rebating and
discrimination in fixing and collecting fire insurance
rates , requiring companies to maintain and cooper-
ate in maintaining and operating rate -making bu-
reaus , inspecting and surveying by such bureaus ,
and all other acts and duties as provided by Chap-
ter 16 of the Acts of 1920 General Assembly being
Section 762b -1 to 762 - b -36 inclusive and Sections
744 to 762 inclusive , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition, and all other laws relating thereto , are
transferred to and vested in the Department of In-
surance .
ARTICLE XVI-DEPARTMENT OF MINES AND MINERALS
§ 1. There is hereby created a Department of
Mines and Minerals which shall consist of a Chief

CHAPTER 155 701
Inspector of Mines and such Deputy Inspectors as
provided by law .
§ 2. Division of Geology . There is hereby cre-
ated in this Department a Division of Geology
which shall be in charge of a geologist who shall
have pursued the study of geology in some standard
college or university for a period of four years , and
in addition thereto have had not less than four
years of practical experience in field and laboratory
work as such . Said geologist shall be designated as
State Geologist . Said geologist , the chief mine in-
spector and the deputy inspector shall be appointed
by the Governor for a term of not exceeding four
years and may be removed by the Governor at his
pleasure . The salary of said State Geologist shall
not exceed the sum of Two Hundred and Fifty Dol-
lars ( $ 250.00 ) per month . He shall be responsible
to the Department of Mines and Minerals .
§ 3. Bureau of Mineral and Topographical Sur-
vey. The Bureau of Mineral and Topographical
Survey as provided by Chapter 26 , page 250 , of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1932 is hereby
abolished , and all the duties and functions hereto-
fore vested by law in the said Bureau of Mineral
and Topographical Survey are now transferred to
and vested in the Department of Mines and Min-
erals .
ARTICLE XVII-DEPARTMENT OF FINANCE AND
BUDGETARY CONTROL
§ 1. There is hereby created a Department of
Finance and Budgetary Control which shall consist
of the Governor , Chairman of the State Tax Com-
mission , Auditor of Public Accounts , State Inspec-
tor and Examiner , and the Secretary of the Execu-
tive Cabinet . The Governor shall be ex- officio chair-

702 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
man , and the Secretary of the Governor's Execu
tive Cabinet shall be Secretary .
$ 2. Functions and Duties . All functions here-
tofore vested in the Budget Commission are hereby
transferred to and vested in the Department of
Finance and Budgetary Control , which department
shall have and exercise all the administrative func-
tions of the State , except as otherwise provided
herein , in relation to :
(a) Developing financial policies and plans for
consideration by the Governor and the General As-
sembly as the basis for his budget report and the
recommendations to the General Assembly . Pre-
paring the detailed budget for submission to and
consideration by the General Assembly .
(b) Coordinating and supervising the fiscal af-
fairs and fiscal procedures of the State to insure the
carrying into effect of the definite financial policies
and plans approved or prescribed by the General
Assembly .
(c) Fiscal reporting .
$ 3. The State Budget Officer shall in person
and with such assistance as the Department of Fi-
nance and Budgetary Control may provide and
under the direction of the Department :
(a) Keep in continuous touch with the opera-
tions , plans and needs of the several agencies of the
State , and with the sources and amounts of revenue
and other receipts of the State .
(b ) Appraise the quantity and quality of serv-
ices rendered by each agency , and the needs for
such services and for any new services .
(c ) Prepare the biennial budget report for the
Governor's approval and submission to the General
Assembly .
(d ) Prepare a State Budget for each fiscal year.

CHAPTER 155 703
( e) Recommend transfers between appropria-
tions under the provisions of law, to become effec-
tive upon approval by the Board of Finance and
Budgetary Control .
(f) Examine and recommend work programs
and periodical allotments to the several agencies ,
and changes therein , subject to the approval of the
Board of Finance and Budgetary Control .
(g) Develop plans for improvements and econo-
mies in organization and operation of the several
agencies, and install such plans as approved by the
respective heads of agencies or as directed by the
Governor , the General Assembly or the Board of
Finance and Budgetary Control .
(h) Devise and prescribe the forms of opera-
tion reports to be required periodically from the
several agencies and require the several agencies to
make such reports .
(i) Edit , verify , and reduce to concise and read-
able form, every annual , biennial , or other report or
publication of any kind proposed by any agency
to be printed out of public funds .
(j ) Examine and approve all statements and re-
ports on the financial condition , and estimated fu-
ture financial condition and operations of the state
goverment and the several agencies , before any such
reports are released to the Governor , to the Gen-
eral Assembly , or for publication ; receive and deal
with all requests for information as to financial
conditions and operations of the State ; prepare
such unit costs and cost statistics as may be re-
quired from time to time.
(k) Advise and assist the Board of Finance and
Budgetary Control , the Governor , and the General
Assembly and the Appropriations Committees there-

704 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of , on request , in any matters relating to the func-
tions of the department .
(1) Recommend a unified and integrated system
of accounts covering the form of all accounts to be
kept and all financial reports to be rendered for the
State as a whole and any of its agencies .
(m ) Keep a summary of the financial transac-
tions of all the agencies of the State .
ARTICLE XVIII-DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC PROPERTY
§ 1. There is hereby created a Department of
Public Property which shall consist of the Gover-
nor , who shall be Chairman of the Department , the
Auditor , the Treasurer , the Secretary of State , the
Attorney General, a member of the State Highway
Commission , a member of the Department of Pub-
lic Welfare , the Superintendent of Parks, and the
Commissioner of Purchases when designated and
appointed by the Governor .
$ 2. The Sinking Fund Commission , the State
Printing Commission , the Kentucky State Park
Commission, the State Purchasing Commission , and
the office of the Custodian of Buildings are hereby
abolished and all the duties and functions hereto-
fore vested by law in the said Sinking Fund Com-
mission, the State Printing Commission , the Ken-
tucky State Park Commission , the State Purchas-
ing Commission , and the office of the Custodian of
Buildings are transferred to and vested in the De-
partment of Public Property. The Perryville Bat-
tlefield Commission , the old Kentucky Home Com-
mission, the Library Commission , the Jefferson
Davis Monument Commission , the George Rogers
Clark Commission , the Kentucky National Park
Commission, the Board of Examiners and Registra-
tion of Architects , and the Kentucky Progress Com-

ARTICLE XIX-DEPARTMENT OF BANKING AND
SECURITIES
The Banking and Securities Department of Ken-
tucky shall continue as provided by law .
ARTICLE XX
§ 1. The Department of Library and Archives
shall consist of the Kentucky Library Commission

CHAPTER 155 707
and the State Librarian , who shall be the head of
the Department of Library and Archives . The pres-
ent State Librarian shall hold office until the end
of the term for which she was elected , thereafter
the State Librarian shall be appointed by the Gov-
ernor and shall hold office at the pleasure of the
Governor .
The State Historical Society shall be attached to
and affiliated with the Department to the extent and
in the manner that may be agreed upon by said So-
ciety and this Department .
All functions heretofore vested in the Kentucky
Library Commission , the Library of the Common-
wealth , Trustees of the Library and the State Li-
brary are hereby transferred to and vested in the
Department of Library and Archives , provided that
the Law Library heretofore included in the Library
of the Library of the Commonwealth shall be trans-
ferred to the jurisdiction of the Court of Appeals .
At the expiration of the term of the present libra-
rian, the Court of Appeals shall appoint a Librarian
who shall be in charge of the Law Library .
ARTICLE XXI-INDEPENDENT AGENCIES
§ 1. The Railroad Commission , the Workmen's
Compensation Board , the Board of Election Com-
missioners , the State Racing Commission , the Board
of Bar Examiners , and Judicial Council shall con-
tinue and remain independent agencies and perform
all the functions and duties vested in them by law .
ARTICLE XXII- STATE INSPECTOR AND EXAMINER
§ 1. The State Inspector and Examiner shall re-
main an independent auditing agency exercising all
the powers and duties of that office provided by law.
Any powers conferred upon said State Inspector

708 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and Examiner by this Act shall be in addition to
those now conferred by law .
§ 2. State Board of Accountancy . The State
Board of Accountancy as now constituted shall be
continued , but is hereby transferred to and made a
division of the office of the State Inspector and
Examiner .
ARTICLE XXIII —THE EXECUTIVE CABINET
The Governor , Secretary of State , Auditor , At-
torney General , Chairman of the State Highway
Commission , Superintendent of Public Instruction ,
Commissioner of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics ,
Treasurer , Chairman of the State Tax Commission ,
Adjutant General , Chairman of the Department of
Public Welfare , Commissioner of Insurance and the
Commissioner of Banking and Securities shall con-
stitute the Governor's Executive Cabinet . They
shall meet at least once monthly at such times and
places as may be provided by rule , and at other
times and places upon the call of the Governor .
The Executive Cabinet shall consider such mat-
ters involving general administrative policies and
procedure , relations between departments and other
agencies and desirable measures for cooperation be-
tween departments and other agencies as the Gov-
ernor , the Secretary of the Executive Cabinet , or
any member may place before it , and shall advise
and consult with the Governor at his request upon
all matters affecting the welfare of the State and
relating to the several departments and agencies .
The Governor shall be Chairman of the Executive
Cabinet. The Executive Cabinet and the Secretary
thereof, shall be deemed to be attached to the office
of the Governor and not constitute a separate de-
partment or agency of the State .

CHAPTER 155 709
There shall be a Secretary of the Cabinet who
shall be appointed by the Governor upon the basis
of his qualifications therefor . The qualifications for
such appointment shall include four (4 ) years of
responsible experience in the management of sub-
stantial public or private business enterprises . The
Secretary of the Executive Cabinet shall prepare all
matters for consideration by the Executive Cabinet .
The Secretary of the said Cabinet shall also be the
Secretary of the Department of Finance and
Budgetary Control and of the Department of Public
Property. His salary shall be fixed by the Governor
within the constitutional limits , and he shall hold
office at the pleasure of the Governor . He shall keep
the minutes and records of the Executive Cabinet .
He shall make continuous studies and accumulate
and compile information for the use of the Execu-
tive Cabinet and the members thereof and the Gov-
ernor relative to the business management and the
operation of the State government , and shall cur
rently supply the members with copies of available
reports and documents and other material relating
to the conditions and problems of the State , the dif-
ficulties that individual departments and agencies
have to overcome , the manner in which the problems
of each department and other agencies relate to
those of other departments and of other agencies ,
and to the State government , and the methods by
which similar problems have been met and solved in
other jurisdictions . He shall assist the Governor in
handling and in securing a coordination of the ef-
forts of the several departments and other agencies
with respect to matters of administration which do
not pertain specifically to individual departments or
other agencies .
The Secretary of the Executive Cabinet shall not
hold office in the organization of any political party .

710 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
He shall deal solely with the administrative and
operating processes and procedures and such facts
relative thereto as may be of use in the carrying out
of any administrative policies that may be adopted .
It is hereby declared to be the policy of the State
to regard the office of the Secretary of the Executive
Cabinet as a continuing one , the primary purpose
of which shall be to place at the disposal of each
succeeding administration of the State government ,
the accumulated knowledge and experience of the
encumbent relative to the course of business man-
agement , and the program of the State, the past
action in such matters and the reasons therefor , and
the studies of the projects and undertakings that
the State has in progress at any time.
It shall be the duty of the Secretary of the Execu-
tive Cabinet to investigate the merit of service of
the employees in the several departments in the
State Government and , whenever , in his judgment
any one or more employees in any department are
not rendering meritorious service or not needed to
properly carry on the functions of such department ,
it shall be his duty to file a complaint with the Execu-
tive Cabinet, setting out his findings with reference
to such employee or employees , and the Executive
Cabinet shall hear and determine whether or not
such employee or employees are rendering meritori-
ous service and are needed in such department to
properly carry on its functions , and shall recom-
mend to the Governor what disposition should be
made with reference to such employee or employees
and thereupon, the Governor may, upon recom-
mendation of a majority of the members of the Cab-
inet either discharge or continue such employee or
employees as in his judgment the best interests of
the State may require .

CHAPTER 155 711
ARTICLE XXIV- GENERAL PROVISIONS
§ 1. Transfer of Functions . Whenever functions
heretofore vested in , or exercised by, any agency
are by this Act transferred in whole or in part to
and vested in a department or other agency created
or continued by this Act, the department or other
agency in which such functions are vested shall be
the successor to the agency in which such functions
have heretofore been vested and every act done in
the exercise of such functions by or under the
authority of the department or other agency to
which such functions are hereby transferred , shall
have the same force and effect as if done by the
former agency . Whenever such former agency is
referred to , or designated in any law , contract or
document , such reference or designation shall be
deemed to refer to the department or other agency ,
hereunder to which the functions of such former
agency so referred to or designated have been trans-
ferred and vested by the terms of this Act .
§ 2. Transfer of Records and Equipment . Upon
the taking effect of any transfer of functions pro-
vided by this Act , all books , papers , records , prop-
erty and pending business theretofore made , used ,
acquired or conducted by any previously existing
agency in the exercise of functions are hereby trans-
ferred to and vested in the agency succeeding to
such functions .
§ 3. Transfer of Officers and Employees . All
officers and employees whose functions are trans-
ferred hereby from an existing agency and vested
in a different agency , created or continued by the
terms of this Act, shall be transferred with their
functions to the department in which their functions
or the major part thereof are hereby vested , and
shall so far as practicable continue to perform their
usual duties until the expiration of their respective

712 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
terms for which they were appointed or elected , but
the head of each department shall have power to
abolish unnecessary positions and employments to
transfer employees between positions , and to change
the duties , titles and compensations of existing po-
sitions and services , and to discharge any such em-
ployees not satisfactory to the head of such depart-
ment .
§ 4. Transfer of Appropriations . Whenever
functions heretofore vested in any agency are trans-
ferred by the terms of this Act and vested in a dif-
ferent agency , the balances of appropriations to the
credit of the agency from which such functions are
transferred available for expenditure in the exer-
cise of such functions shall be transferred to the
credit of the agency to which such functions are
hereby transferred , and shall be made available
under the provisions of this Act, for expenditure
by such agency in the exercise of such functions in
accordance with the law. :
Where two or more functions provided for by
existing appropriations are not segregated to the
purposes for such function , but are divided between
two or more agencies , the divisions of such ap-
propriations shall be determined by the Governor ,
with the approval of the Department of Finance
and Budgetary Control .
5. Heads of Departments . The heads of de-
partments provided herein shall have direction and
control of their respective departments , and , sub-
ject to the provisions of this Act, shall through such
departments exercise such powers and perform such
duties as vested by law in the several departments
under their direction and control and in any agency
thereof . Except as otherwise expressly provided by
this Act, the heads of the departments shall be ap-
pointed by the Governor on the basis of their merit

CHAPTER 155 713
and fitness to perform the duties and exercise the
responsibilities of their respective offices .
§ 6. Governor May Remove Officers . The Gov-
ernor shall have the power to remove , for cause
deemed sufficient by him, the head or deputies of
any departments , who are subject to appointment
by him.
§ 7. Termination of Conflicts Between Depart-
ments . In all cases where question arises between
departments or other agencies or officers or boards
thereof as to their respective functions , or where
departments or other agencies issue conflicting
orders or make conflicting rules , the Governor , with
the advice of the Executive Cabinet , shall determine
the questions and action shall be had in accordance
with the decision .
§ 8. Administrative Rules . The Governor , with
the approval of the Executive Cabinet, may pre-
scribe such rules for the conduct of the administra-
tive departments , not inconsistent with law , as he
may deem necessary or expedient . The heads of
each department may likewise provide such rules
as they may deem necessary and expedient for the
work of their departments and for making effective
the provisions of law not inconsistent herewith or
with the general rules prescribed by the Governor
§ 9. When the Act Is Effective . The provisions
of this Act shall be and become effective at 12 o'clock
midnight , June 30 , 1934 .
$ 10 . Inconsistent Laws Repealed . It is the pur-
pose of this Act to provide a uniform , integrated
and comprehensive plan of organization for the
executive departments of the Government of the
Commonwealth for the exercise of all executive and
administrative functions of the State . All laws and
parts of laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed
to the extent of such conflict .

714 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 11. Constitutionality . If any part or portion
of this Act shall be held unconstitutional or invalid
for any reason , the remainder of the Act shall never-
theless remain in full force and effect .
Approved February 28 , 1934 .
Ruby Laffoon , Governor of Kentucky .

mortgage a bridge to secure bonds issued for the purchase
thereof.

CHAPTER 157 .
AN ACT amending sections 2492 , 2493 , 1494 and 2495 , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition ; providing additional security
for the payment of claims for labor , materials , and supplies
furnished for use on certain public works ; prescribing the
procedure for perfecting and enforcing liens to better secure
the payment of such claims ; defining the terms "labor ," "ma-
terials ," and "supplies " as used in the Act ; providing for the
discharge of such liens on public funds by payment of such
claims out of funds due the contractors ; and repealing all
laws and parts of laws in conflict therewith to the extent of
such conflict.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 2492 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition, be amended by repealing said
section in its entirety and re-enacting it to read as
follows :
All persons who perform or furnish labor , ma-
terial or supplies for the construction or improve-
ment of any canal , railroad , bridge , public highway
or other public improvement in this Commonwealth ,
by contract , expressed or implied , with the owner
or owners thereof, or by subcontract thereunder ,
shall have a lien thereon , and upon all the property
and franchise of the owner or owners thereof , ex-
cept as to property owned by the Commonwealth of
Kentucky or any county or municipality and in all
cases where the property improved is owned by the
Commonwealth or by any county or municipality
such persons shall have a lien on the funds due the
contractor from the owner of the property improved .
Such lien shall be for the full contract price of such
labor , materials and supplies so furnished or per-
formed and shall be prior and superior to all other
leins thereafter created thereon ; but any person
undertaking or expecting to perform or funrish
labor , materials or supplies , in the manner provid-

CHAPTER 157 719

berein provided , by filing in the clerk's office of
each county , wherein he shall have so undertaken
to perform or furnish labor , materials or supplies ,
except as hereinafter provided , a statement in writ-
ing , stating that he has so undertaken and expects
to perform or furnish labor , materials or supplies ,
and the price at which same is to be furnished , and
the lien for labor performed , material or supplies
furnished thereafter shall relate back and take ef-
fect from the date of the filing of such statement ;
provided that as to all original construction such
liens shall be prior to all liens theretofore or there-
after created on the part so constructed and on no

5. That section 2495 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition, be amended by repealing said
section in its entirety and re -enacting it to read as
follows :
Liens required under this Act shall be enforced
by proper proceedings in equity, to which other lien-
holders must be made parties ; but such proceedings
must be begun within six months from the filing of
the claim in the county clerk's office as herein pro-
vided , except such proceedings as are required by
the next preceding section to be filed within the time
therein provided .
§ 6. The failure to file or assert any lien author-
ized to be filed or asserted under the provisions of
this Act shall in no way operate to release any other
security which any such claimant may have for the
payment of such claim, it being intended by this
Act to provide additional security for the protec-
tion and payment of such claims .
7. The word " labor " as used herein shall be
construed to include work done by teams , and by
trucks , whether the owner furnishes a driver or not,
and all labor ordinarily coming within the definition
of said word labor , except it shall not be construed
as including work done by any officer , superintend-
ent or stockholder of any company , firm or corpo-
ration , even though such officer , superintendent or
stockholder may also perform manual labor on the
public work . The term " materials " shall be con-
strued as including all materials of every kind or
character which may be used in the public improve-
ment and which remain as a part of the completed

"materials ” nor " supplies " shall be construed to

any lien or claim asserted under this Act when it is
shown by evidence that the items embraced in the

ticular project or public work , such evidence shall
make out a prima facie case that such items were
used in the performance of the contract .
§ 9. If any person , firm or corporation shall file

nection therewith , which claims for damages may

of this Act shall be adjudged by any court of compe-
tent jurisdiction to be invalid or unconstitutional ,
such judgment shall not affect , impair , invalidate or
uullify the remainder of this Act , but the effect

§ 11. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-

pealed .

CHAPTER 158 .
AN ACT to provide for the licensing of road , highway and
bridge contractors by the State Highway Commission ; pro-
viding for an investigation and examination of such con-
tractors ; providing for the adoption and enforcement of rules
and regulations by the Commission governing persons , firms ,
associations and corporations , having contracts or bidding
for cotracts with the State Highway Commission , to con-
struct, reconstruct or maintain state roads , highways or
bridges ; empowering the Commission to grant certificates of
eligibility to contractors and providing for the revocation of
such certificates ; providing for an appeal from the decision
of the Commission to the Franklin County Circuit Court , or
any other court of competent jurisdiction ; providing for the
naming of all subcontractors when contracts are sublet ; au-
thorizing the Commission to make public the qualified con-
tractors ; persons , firms , associations or corporations who are
eligible and authorized to do business with the State Highway
Commission ; providing nature of contracts to which this act
shall apply and providing for the levying of a license tax to
carry out the provisions of this act .

CHAPTER 158 727
.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Every person , firm , association or corpora-
tion , from and after the effective date of this act ,
who shall seek to procure , bid upon , or offer to bid
upon any contract with the State Highway Commis-
sion , for the construction or maintenance of any pub-
lic road or highway , or any section thereof ; or any
bridge or bridge approach , or any section thereof ,
before procuring a proposal form , or submitting any
bid to obtain such contract shall apply for and pro-
cure a certificate of eligibility and qualification from
the Commission to bid on such work .
All applications for a certificate of eligibility shall
be made to the Commission in writing , and the Com-
mission shall act upon the application within a
period of thirty ( 30 ) days from the date when the
application is filed .
2. The authority to determine who shall be
eligible to bid upon any contract with the Commis-
sion for the construction, reconstruction or main-
tenance of any public road or highway , or section
thereof ; or any bridge or bridge approach , or sec-
tion thereof , shall be and is hereby vested in the
State Highway Commission . The Commission is
hereby authorized and empowered to adopt such
rules and regulations and make such investigations
and examinations as it may deem fit and necessary
in order to determine the financial status , experi-
ence , responsibility , business ability , previous rec-
ord , sufficiency of plant and equipment , and any
other pertinent facts and information necessary for
the Commission to determine the eligibility of the
proposed bidder .
The Commission shall establish rules and regu-
lations governing the application for the certificate
of eligibility , the means and methods of obtaining

728 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
information relative to the applicant , and the grant-
ing of, or refusal to grant , a certificate of eligibility ,
and to this end may require a financial statement of
the applicant as often as the Commission may deem
necessary , and at least once in every twelve ( 12 )
months verified by the applicant , or if a corpora-
tion by the authorized official thereof .
§ 3. After obtaining such information as the
Commission may deem necessary relative to the
fitness and qualification of the applicant , the Com-
mission shall proceed to determine whether or not
the applicant is eligible to bid upon road or high-
way or bridge contracts within the meaning of this
act . All applicants for qualification shall be prompt-
ly notified by the Commission of its final action on
their applications .
Any applicant aggrieved by the decision of the
Commission may, within ten ( 10 ) days after receiv-
ing notification of such decision request in writing
a reconsideration of his application by the Commis-
sion , and may submit additional evidence bearing
on his qualifications . The Commission shall there-
upon again consider the application , and may either
adhere to , reverse , or modify its previous decision .
The Commission shall act upon any request for re-
consideration within thirty ( 30 ) days after the filing
thereof, and shall forthwith notify the applicant of
the action taken .
Upon being notified of the final action of the Com-
mission upon such reconsideration, any applicant
who is still aggrieved by the decision of the Com-
mission may , within ten ( 10 ) days after receiving
notification of such decision , take an appeal there-
from to the Franklin County Circuit Court , or any
other court of competent jurisdiction . Such appeal
shall be perfected by the filing of a bond with the
clerk of said court in such amount as the clerk may

In the event the Commission shall determine that

provided , said Commission shall reject such applica-
