and said applicant shall not thereafter be entitled
to make application for a certificate of eligibility
until such time as he is financially and in all other
ways qualified , provided , however , that a certificate
of eligibility shall not be denied to any applicant
upon the sole issue of residence or nationality .
§ 4. A certificate of eligibility may be revoked
by the Commission only after a written notice is
given to the qualified bidder and an opportunity
given to him to be heard by the Commission . This
notice shall not only be given to him in writing but
shall also state and enumerate the grounds on which
the revocation is proposed to be issued.
§ 5. No bidder shall be given a certificate of
qualification unless his financial statement and the
investigation made by the Commission shall show
that he possesses net current assets or working
capital sufficient in the judgment of the Commission
to render it probable that he can satisfactorily exe-
cute his contracts and meet obligations therein in-
curred . All applications for qualification shall ex-

to the financial status , assets , liabilities of the ap-

surety companies , dealers in material , equipment or
supplies , or other persons having business trans-
actions with the applicant , and shall expressly
authorize all such financial institutions or persons to
furnish any such information requested from them
by the Commission .
The Commission may in its discretion make pub-

Where the applicant is a foreign corporation the
application shall be accompanied by a certificate
from the Secretary of State that such corporation
is authorized to do business in the state wherein the
corporation is incorporated .
6. This act shall be administered without ref-
erence to the residence of applicants , and its pro-
visions and rules and regulations formulated by the
State Highway Commission and adopted pursuant
thereto shall apply equally and the same to resi-
dents and non - residents of the State of Kentucky .
7. Before making an award the Commission
may require of the bidder to name the subcontractor
whom such bidder intends to employ for the various
divisions of the work bid upon.
$ 8. The Commission shall not be authorized to
consider any bid filed with it by any person , firm ,
association , or corporation who had not been quali-
fied under the terms and provisions of this act . Bids
from unqualified bidders which are discovered by
the Commission prior to their reading thereof shall
be returned to the bidders without being read . In
the event the Commission finds subsequent to the
opening of bids that facts exist which would dis-
qualify the lowest bidder, or that such bidder is not
responsible and competent , the Commission is
hereby directed to reject such bid, despite the pre-
vious qualification of the bidder . It shall be un-
lawful to award any contract to any bidder not quali-
fied to bid thereon at the time such bids are opened .
The provisions of this act shall not apply to the
purchase (direct ) of equipment , materials and sup-
plies by the Highway Commission except as tocrushed stone in lots of more than one thousand

732 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(1,000 ) tons and bituminous materials in tank car
lots .
§ 9. In order to provide revenue needed to carry
out the provisions of this act and to make the neces-
sary examinations and investigations , as to the eligi-
bility of proposed bidders ; every person , firm , as-
sociation , or corporation who applies for , and is
granted, a certificate of eligibility as provided for in
this act , shall pay to the State Highway Commission
or to its agents thereof, an annual license fee of fifty
($50.00 ) dollars , the fee to accompany any applica-
tion filed with the Commission , and said license shall
expire on December 31st of the same year in which
it is granted. If during the month of January fol-
lowing the expiration of the certificate of eligibility ,
the holder of the certificate expiring desires to have
it renewed he may do so by the payment of a renewal
fee of ten ( $ 10.00 ) dollars , provided the application
for renewal accompanied by the payment of ten
($10.00 ) dollars is made before February first ( 1st )
following the date of expiration of the certificate of
eligibility . If the application for renewal is not
made before February first ( 1st ) following such
expiration then such person , firm , association or
corporation making application for renewal shall
pay fifty ( $ 50.00 ) dollars and be subject to all the
provisions and requirements of this act relative to
securing an original certificate of eligibility . In
order to carry out the provisions of this act efficient-
ly and effectively, and to set the machinery into
action, all applications for a certificate of eligibility
made during the remainder of this year ( 1934 ) , shall
pay a fee of fifty ( $50.00 ) dollars .
Said funds shall be used by the Commission to de-
fray the expenses necessary to carry out the pro-
visions of this act , and at the end of each fiscal year ,
any unexpended balance in said fund shall be paid

CHAPTER 159 733
into the State Treasury and credited to the road
fund.
In the event a certificate of eligibility is not
granted , then the Commission shall return the fee to
the applicant .
§ 10. All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed to the extent of such con-
flict .
§ 11. If any of the provisions of this act shall be
held to be invalid or unconstitutional such finding
shall not affect any other provisions of this act , it
being the intention of the General Assembly that in
enacting this act each section shall be considered
alone and separately ; and if any provision contained
in any specific section of this act shall be held invalid
or unconstitutional , said fact shall not affect the re-
maining portion of the act , it being the intention of
the General Assembly to enact each section and
provision of this act , separately .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 159 .
AN ACT providing for the construction , operation and mainte-
nance of a bridge across the Kentucky River at Frankfort ,
Kentucky, and providing the time , manner and method of
construction , operation and maintenance thereof .
WHEREAS , under the provisions of Section 255 of the Consti-
tution of Kentucky the seat of government of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky is in the City of Frankfort , Franklin
County , Kentucky , and ,
WHEREAS, the Commonwealth of Kentucky has constructed
and is now maintaining a system of State primary highways
and bridges ; and ,
WHEREAS , a number of the most important trunk highways of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky now under State maintenance
pass through the City of Frankfort ; and
WHEREAS , the present bridge across the Kentucky River in
the City of Frankfort connecting St. Clair and Bridge Streets
has been used to carry motor traffic over such state and na-

734 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tional highways passing through Frankfort until said bridge
has become insufficient to accommodate the public travel-
ing upon the trunk highways of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky through the City of Frankfort , and it is inadequate for
that purpose ; and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth of Kentucky has property situ-
ated in the City of Frankfort and Franklin County, of the
value of several million dollars not subject to taxation by
either the City of Frankfort or the County of Franklin ; and,
WHEREAS , the financial condition of both the City of Frank-
fort and the County of Franklin is such that neither said city
nor county can raise by taxation the money to construct said
bridge; and ,
WHEREAS , it is necessary that said bridge be constructed in
order that the citizens , officials and employees of this State
may safely use the aforesaid highways in coming to and going
from the seat of this government in the transaction of busi-
ness ; and ,
WHEREAS , the City of Frankfort being the seat of the govern-
ment and the capital of this Commonwealth , it is deemed by
the General Assembly to be necessary for such new bridge
to be constructed and maintained for the general public wel-
fare , free of tolls or other charges , and in order to better
utilize the property and facilities of the Commonwealth situ-
ated in the City of Frankfort ; Now , Therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Governor of this Commonwealth is
hereby authorized and empowered to designate in
writing to the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky the time when and the place at which a new
bridge is to be built over the Kentucky River , at
Frankfort , Kentucky , and upon the receipt of his
designation by the State Highway Commission , said
Commission is authorized and directed to acquire
by gift , purchase , condemnation , or otherwise , the
necessary rights of ways for the construction , main-
tenance and operation of a modern bridge across the
Kentucky River , in the City of Frankfort , Franklin
county , Kentucky , and to construct , operate and

CHAPTER 159 735
bride's
maintain such bridge free of tolls or other charges
except as hereinafter provided . Said Commission
is authorized and directed to make or cause to be
made all necessary surveys , plans and specifications
for the construction of said bridge , and to do all
things reasonably necessary to carry out the intent
and purpose of this act , and to proceed as speedily
as possible with the construction of same .
§ 2. Within thirty days after the plans and
specifications for said bridge shall have been com-
pleted the Commission shall advertise for and re-
ceive bids for the construction of said bridge in ac-
cordance with the plans and specifications therefor
and shall award the contract upon the approval of
the Governor for the construction of said bridge to
the lowest and best bidder or bidders . The Com-
mission shall require the successful bidder or bid-
ders to execute good and sufficient bond or bonds to
be approved by the Commission to guarantee the
faithful performance of the contract and the prompt
payment of all bills which may be incurred for labor ,
materials and supplies furnished for use in the per-
formance of such contract or contracts . The con-
tract to be awarded by the Commission shall require
that the construction of the bridge be completed and
ready for traffic within such time as may be fixed
by the Commission , and approved by the Governor .
§ 3. Said bridge shall be of such design and such
width as may in the judgment of the Commission be
adequate for present and future necessities , and on
each side of the roadway of said bridge there shall
be constructed a sidewalk for foot passengers of not
less than five feet in width . The Commission shall
have power and authority to provide in the plans
and specifications for the construction of said bridge
and to construct a single track for the operation of
street cars over said bridge and may enter into a

736 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
contract or contracts with any person , firm or corpo-
ration having authority to operate street cars or
street railway in the City of Frankfort giving such
person , firm or corporation the right to operate
street cars or street railway over and across said
bridge upon the payment to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky of such reasonable annual charges as the
Commission may determine .
§ 4. The costs of the construction , maintenance
and operation of said bridge , shall be paid out of the
State Road Fund in the same manner as the cost of
construction of State primary roads is paid . The
necessary rights of way for the construction , main-
tenance and operation of said bridge shall be fur-
nished and paid for by Franklin County . The City
of Frankfort , Kentucky , is empowered and author-
ized to contribute to Franklin county a portion of
the costs of the rights of way for construction , main-
tenance and operation of said bridge .
§ 5. The term " bridge " as used in this act shall
be construed to mean the bridge proper and such
approaches thereto as may be reasonably necessary
to properly connect said bridge proper with the
sreets or highways in the City of Frankfort leading
thereto . The term "Commission " as used in this
act shall be construed to mean The State Highway
Commission of Kentucky
$ 6. There shall be erected by the Commission
at each end of said bridge a proper monument or
inscription , the form and size of which to be ap-
proved by the Governor , and the State President
of the American War Mothers of Kentucky and in-
scribed thereon the following :
CAPITAL BRIDGE
Erected pursuant to an Act of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky enacted in 1934 , as a Memorial
to the Men and Women of Kentucky who rendered

CHAPTER 160 737
service to their country in the Army, Navy and other
other Military Service during the World War and
other previous wars, in grateful appreciation oftheir Patriotism and Sacrifices .
7. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with, to the extent of such conflict , are repealed only
so far and to the extent this Act is in conflict withsame .
Approved March 14 , 1934 .

CHAPTER 161 .
AN ACT to regulate traffic over and upon toll bridges and fer-
ries ; making it unlawful for any person to pass over or upon
any toll bridge or ferry without paying the required toll ; mak-
ing it unlawful for any person to park any vehicle upon any
toll bridge , and also prescribing penalties for the commis-
sion of offenses defined in this act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to pass over or upon any part of any toll
bridge or ferry , upon which bridge or ferry tolls are
at such time collected , without paying to the col-
lector of tolls then stationed at such bridge or ferry
at the time of such passage , in legal tender , such
amount of toll as may be prescribed by law or regu-
lation for passage over such bridge or ferry , includ-
ing vehicular traffic over or upon any part of such
bridge or ferry ; provided that no such toll shall be
charged to children going to or returning from
school . Neither shall such toll be charged for school
busses or other vehicles used exclusively in the
transportation of school children to and from school .
2. That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to park any vehicle upon any toll bridge at
any time.
3. Any person or persons found guilty of
violating any of the provisions of this act shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction thereof
shall be fined not less than $10.00 nor more than
$100.00 for each offense .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 549 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION memoralizing Congress , That it
is the sense of the members of the Kentucky Legislative
General Assembly, the Senate and House concurring, that the
Government of the United States should perform its solemn
promise and place American agriculture on the basis of
equality with other industries by providing an adequate sys-
tem of credit, and that adequate legislation to that end should
be adopted at the earliest possible date .
WHEREAS , unless immediate relief is given , hundreds of
thousands of farmers will lose their farms and their homes
and millions more will be forced into our cities and villages ,
and the army of unemployed will necessarily increase to
alarming proportions ; and
WHEREAS , the price of agriculture products during recent
years has in fact been far below the cost of production ; and
WHEREAS there is no adequate way of refinancing existing
agricultural indebtedness and the farmers are at the mercy
of their mortgagees and creditors throughout this State and
nation ; and
WHEREAS , Senate Bill 457 , introduced in the Senate of the
United States by Senator Lynn J. Frazier of North Dakota ,
provides for the liquidating and refinancing of agricultural
indebtedness and provides for a reduced rate of interest for
the same through the Federal Farm Loan System and the
Federal Reserve Bank System ; and
WHEREAS , the provisions of this bill will have a vital effect
upon the agricultural industry of the State of Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , at the present time many loans relating to the
agricultural industry should bear a reduced rate of interest ;
and-
WHEREAS , agriculture is the basic industry of this country
and there can be no sound business prosperity until agricul-
ture is put on an equality with other industries : Now, there-
fore ,

998 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That it is the sense of your memorialists , the
members of the Kentucky Legislative General As-
sembly , the Senate and House concurring , that the
Congress of the United States should enact the pro-
visions of the said Senate bill 457 ; and
Be it Further Resolved , That a copy of this memo-
rial, duly authenticated , be sent by the clerk of the
Kentucky Senate to the Senate and House of Rep-
resentatives of the United States and to each of the
Senators and Representatives of Kentucky in Con-
gress , and to United States Senator Lynn J. Frazier
the Senator who introduced the bill .
Approved February 8, 1934.

CHAPTER 551 .
WHEREAS , the building of a power dam is being contemplated
by the United States Government on the Tennessee River ,
and
WHEREAS , the building of such a power dam would be of great
benefit to the citizens of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Now , therefore , be it resolved by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That it is the sense of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that the building of
such a power dam will be of great and lasting bene-
fit to the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
Be it Further Resolved that the true and proper
place for locating said power dam is on the Tennes-
see River about forty miles south of Paducah , Ken-
tucky, and near Aurora , Kentucky , and
Be it Further Resolved that each member of Con-
gress from Kentucky be , and are hereby requested
to assist the citizens of this Commonwealth in bring-
ing about the construction of said power dam at
said location, and
Be it Further Resolved that the Clerk of the Sen-
ate send a copy of this resolution to each Senator
member of Congress from Kentucky , and to the
members of the Tennessee Valley authorities .
Approved February 8, 1934 .

CHAPTER 554 1001

CHAPTER 556 .
RESOLUTION to Honorable Henry A. Wallace , Secretary of
Agriculture , Washington, District of Columbia .
WHEREAS , it has been called to my attention that the Ken-
tucky Game and Fish Commission has requested that the
Secretary of Agriculture , under the powers granted him by
the President of the United States , purchase sub -marginal
lands in Ballard , Livingston , Carlisle , and McCracken coun-
ties , for the purpose of establishing a game refuge for migra-
tory birds on their flights to and from the South , and

1004 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , there is considerable acreage of these sub -marginal
lands in the aforesaid counties , and whereas we feel that it
would be beneficial , not only for the protection of migratory
birds , but would be beneficial to the citizens of aforesaid
counties that this refuge be established , and whereas , we feel
that it would meet with the approval of the citizens of afore-
said counties , and would be of material benefit to land owners
of said marginal tracts , for the establishment of said haven
and refuge for said migratory birds, now,
Therefore , be it resolved , the Senate and House
of Representatives of Kentucky concurring therein ,
That a copy of these resolutions be sent to the
Honorable Henry A. Wallace , Secretary of Agricul-
ture of the United States of America , petitioning
him to purchase and establish said refuge for
the
purpose set out in this resolution
.
Approved March
17, 1934
.

CHAPTER 560 .
SENATE JOINT RESOLUTION . WHEREAS , the Congress of
the United States has heretofore passed what is known as the
Adjusted Compensation Act for the benefit of World War
Soldiers , Sailors , Marines and Nurses , and has under said
Act issued Adjusted Compensation Certificates to said World
War Veterans , which said certificates are due in Nineteen
Hundred and Forty -five ; and Whereas , largely on account of
the depression , a great number of those holding these Ad-
justed Compensation Certificates have found it necessary to
borrow money thereon , and are required to pay interest upon
money , which the Government has acknowledged that it owes
them ; and
WHEREAS , the meeting for the relief of the said veterans and
their families is greater today than ever in history , and prob-
ably greater than it will be in Nineteen Hundred and Forty .
five, when many of them will be dead , and unable to enjoy
the benefits of the money due them ; and ,

1008 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , it is the policy of the President and the Congress
of the United States to increase the spending power of the
Nation , and the citizens thereof, through various agencies
established by the Congress , and for this purpose , the expen-
diture of large sums from public funds has heretofore been
authorized by Congress ; and ,
WHEREAS , no better way is known for the accomplishment of
this purpose than the payment in cash of the face value of the
said Adjusted Compensation Certificates at the present time,
which, while serving the purpose of paying off an existing
government indebtedness , will serve to circulate cash in every
community , and city of the land no matter how small or how
great , thereby increasing the spending power of the people
in every community , town and city ; and ,-
WHEREAS , by doing this , the Government will recognize a just
obligation to its former soldiers , and bring happiness and
some degree of prosperity to millions of soldier homes ;
Therefore be it Resolved by the Senate of Ken-
tucky , the House of Representatives concurring
therein :
Section One . That the President of the United
States , the Congress thereof , and especially the
Senators and Representatives from Kentucky be and
they are hereby respectfully memorialized , peti-
tioned and urged to use their best efforts and vote
for the passing of a bill now pending in Congress
providing for the immediate payment of the Ad-
justed Compensation Certificates due the World
War Veterans .
Section Two . The Clerk of the Senate is hereby
directed to send a certified copy of this Resolution
to the President of the United States , to the Clerk of
the Senate and of the House of Representatives of
the Congress of the United States , and a copy to
each Senator and member of the House of Rep-
resentatives from Kentucky .
Approved March 6, 1934.

CHAPTER 562 .
JOINT RESOLUTION for the payment of B. P. Walker , Gov-
ernor's agent , for his expenses and charges incident thereto ,
in the extradition matter of M. A. Brown , fugitive from jus-
tice , Grand Rapids , Michigan , November , 1933 , amounting to
$ 83.70 .
THAT WHEREAS , on November 11 , 1933 , the Grand Jury , in
and for the Knox Circuit Court found a true bill of indictment
against M. A. Brown for deserting and abandoning his law-
ful wife in Knox County and returning without her , and
against her wishes , to his home in Grand Rapids , Michigan ,
and on application of the duly elected and acting District
Attorney , Phil L. Senters , made and presented to his ex-
cellency the Governor , on the seventeenth day of November ,
1933 , a demand upon the Governor of the State of Michigan ,
that said M. A. Brown be turned over to B. P. Walker , for
transmission to the State of Kentucky , here to be dealt with
according to the laws of this State in such cases , and that

1010 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
whereas , said Walker proceeded to Grand Rapids , Michigan ,
at his own expense , secured the lawful arrest of said Brown
and that on demand a hearing was had before the Attorney
General , acting for said Governor of the State of Michigan ,
wherein, on recommendation of said Attorney General , the
Governor of the State of Michigan refused to honor said ex-
tradition demand and refused to permit said Walker to re-
turn M. A. Brown as he was commissioned to do , and with-
out fault on his part.
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

to draw his warrant upon the treasury for the sum
of $ 83.70 , payable to B. P. Walker , Governor's
agent , for his services in the extradition matter of
M. A. Brown , a fugitive from justice , Grand Rapids ,
Michigan , incurred during the month of November ,
1933 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 566 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Luther Cooper , as guardian or next
friend of Estel Cooper , an infant eleven years of age , to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission, or either .
WHEREAS , on the 15th day of November , 1933 , Estel Cooper ,
an infant eleven years of age , of Revilo , McCreary County ,
Kentucky , was run over and injured on Highway Number 27 ,
at Revilo in McCreary County , Kentucky , wherein it is claimed
that while the said Estel Cooper was walking the highway,
he was struck and injured , one leg having to be amputated
and receiving other injuries , by a truck operated by one of
the employees of the State Highway Commission , and that
said employee was guilty of negligence which caused and
brought about the injury of the said Estel Cooper , therefore ,

1014 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Luther Cooper , as guardian or next friend
of Estel Cooper , an infant , be , and he is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either , in the McCreary Circuit Court , the county of
the residence of the said Estel Cooper , for damages
for the loss of earning power , for physical suffering
and for hospital bills of said Estel Cooper, which
were brought about and caused by the negligence
of the employee of the State Highway Commission .
Said suit shall be for any amount not exceeding the
sum of $ 6,000.00 , and in the event any judgment is
recovered by said guardian or next friend in said
suit for the injuries to said Estel Cooper , or same
is compromised or settled , said judgment shall be
paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts, by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer , and paid out of the
General Fund .
Either party to said suit may appeal from any
judgment which may be entered therein as in any
other civil suit , and the case may be settled and
adjusted, with the consent and approval of the At-
torney General of Kentucky , in the same way as any
other civil case.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 567 .
RESOLUTION for the relief of C. M. Greene , for mileage and
expenses by him as agent for the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky.
WHEREAS , C. M. Greene in February 10 , 1931 , was duly ap
pointed by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as
agent of the Commonwealth to go to Sacramento , California ,
to present requisition papers of this Commonwealth to the

CHAPTER 568 1015
Governor of California and to bring back S. S. Little , a fugi-
tive from the justice of this State , under indictment for felony
in Daviess County , and
WHEREAS , said C. M. Greene duly performed his duty in the
premises and presented the requisition papers of this Com-
monwealth to the Governor of California but who refused to
honor said requisition and as a result the said C. M. Greene
could not bring back to Kentucky said fugitive from justice .
WHEREAS , the said C. M. Greene , in performing his duty as
agent aforesaid incurred expenses and traveled 513.5 miles ,
the regular compensation for which , including actual ex-
penses , would be $380.45 ; and
WHEREAS , the Statute of this State as construed by the Court
of Appeals did not authorize the payment of said expenses
because the said fugitive was not brought back to the State ;
Therefore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate and House of Repre-
sentatives :
That the said abount of $ 380.45 be and is hereby
appropriated to be paid to the said C. M. Greene ,
and the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed to
draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for said
amount and deliver the same to C. M. Greene for
the purpose aforesaid .
Approved February 16, 1934 .
CHAPTER 568 .
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION requesting prompt ratification
of the Great Lakes -St . Lawrence Seaway Treaty .
WHEREAS , there is now before the Senate of the United States
a treaty between the United States and the Dominion of
Canada , which provides the plans , allocation of tasks , estimate
of costs , and commits the two nations to increasing the use-
fulness of our inland seas by replacing obsolete equipment ,
standardizing of depths and increasing capacity of chan-
nels , locks and canals in the St. Lawrence River to meet pres-
ent day needs , so that the Great Lakes ports may be opened
to the uninterrupted movement of ocean -borne commerce ;
and !

1.016 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
·
WHEREAS , an oceanway to the mid -continent , besides con-
tributing importantly to national efficiency , is absolutely es
sential to place the interior of the United States on a world
competitive basis ;
Therefore , Section 1, Be it resolved by the House
cf Representatives of the State of Kentucky , the
Senate concurring therein :
That the 1934 session of the General Assembly ,
hereby memoralizes the Senate of the United States
for the speedy commencement of construction of the
Great Lakes -St . Lawrence seaway project , and
urgently requests the members of the United States
Senate and the Senators from Kentucky in particu-
lar, that they not only support immediate ratifica-
tion of the said seaway but that they use every legiti-
mate effort to impress upon their colleagues the
need for lifting the competitive position of mid-
western agriculture and industries in world trade ,
and as a major part in the President's public works
program for the relief of the unemployment .
Section 2. The Clerk of the House of Representa-
tives is hereby instructed to send copies of this
resolution to each member of the Senate of the
Congress of the United States .
Approved January 23, 1934.
CHAPTER 569 .
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for the appointment
of a Commission to arrange the suitable and proper celebra-
tion of the Two Hundredth Anniversary of the birth of Daniel
Boone .
WHEREAS , the year of 1934 marks the Two Hundredth Anni-
versary of the birth of the great American , Daniel Boone , and
WHEREAS , the exploration , settlement , and early defense of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky can be attributable to an
appreciable degree to the work of the said Daniel Boone ;

CHAPTER 570 1017
Therefore , be it resolved by the House of Repre-
sentatives of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the
Senate concurring therein :
That the Commonwealth of Kentucky do partici-
pate in the celebration of the Two Hundredth Anni-
versary of the Birth of Daniel Boone and that for the
purpose of preparing and executing a plan or plans
of such celebration a State Commission of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky is hereby established , the
same to be known as the Deniel Boone Bicentennial
Commission , and to be composed of 15 citizens of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky appointed by the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , with
said governor an ex-officio member of the same .
Said Commission shall serve without compensation
and shall be empowered and authorized to raise
funds for said celebration and the participation
therein by subscriptions, donations , contributions ,
and other legal means , within and without the
boundaries of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . All
other existing commissions and agencies of the said
Commonwealth of Kentucky which are qualified and
prepared to assist in the said Bicentennial Celebra-
tion are hereby given power to aid the said Commis-
sion in its work . Nothing in this resolution shall be
construed as confining the celebration of the said bi-
centennial solely to the confines of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky . Said commission shall choose
'a Chairman and Secretary from their number and
shall keep a record of their proceedings .
Approved January 30 , 1934 ,
Ruby Laffoon, Governor of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 571 .
RESOLUTION ( substitute for House Resolution No. 10 ) , au-
thorizing Mrs. Elizabeth Burk and the personal representa-
tive of Mrs. Edna Ross , respectively to file suits against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the State Highway Com-
mission , or either .
WHEREAS , on November 28 , 1932 , on the Ida May State road
between Beattyville and Ida May , in Lee County , a truck
owned by the State of Kentucky , then being operated and
used by the State Highway Commission in hauling a load of
crushed rock by an employee of the State Highway Commis-
sion in repairing the State Highway aforesaid , overtook and
ran said truck on and against and permanently injured Mrs.
Elizabeth Burk and killed Mrs. Edna Ross , both of Lee
County, at the same time , and whereas the driver of said
truck has been indicted and convicted for reckless driving
indicating a just cause and clear grounds for this resolution :
NOW in order that they may have their " day in court" to de-
termine by judicial action the question of negligence , if any ,
causing said injury and death aforesaid ,

1020 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assemblyof the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Mrs. Elizabeth Burk, in her own right
and name , and the personal representative of Mrs.
Edna Ross , deceased , be and they are hereby em-
powered and authorized to file and prosecute ap-
propriate actions , severally and respectively, against
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State High-
way Commission , or either, for the purpose of de-
termining the liability of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either, for such injury and death , if any there be ;
and in the event any judgment in either action is
recovered by said Elizabeth Burk and the personal
representative of Edna Ross , or either of them , or
same are compromised or settled , same shall be
paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer and paid out of the
general fund .

any judgment which may be entered therein as in
any other civil suit and the case may be settled and
adjusted with the consent and approval of the At-
torney General of Kentucky in the same way as any
other civil case .
§ 3. Said action may be brought in any county
of Kentucky having jurisdiction of the parties and
subject matter . The limit of liability as to Mrs.
Elizabeth Burk shall be six thousand ($ 6,000.00 )
dollars and as to Mrs. Edna Ross shall be four thou-
sand ($4,000.00 ) dollars .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 572 .
RESOLUTION authorizing F. Daniel to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or either .

CHAPTER 572 1021
WHEREAS , on the 6th day of October , 1932 , F. Daniel , of
Paintsville , Kentucky , whilst traveling upon the Mayo Trail
was injured by a large truck , parked on a curve without any
lights , no warning or signal of any kind being given or to
warn the public of any danger when approaching said truck ,
and as a result of said truck being so parked the said F.
Daniel was injured , said truck being the property of the
State of Kentucky and being used and operated at the time
by the employees of the State Highway Commission in the
performance of their duties , and
WHEREAS , his chest was broken , crushed and bruised , legs
cut and lacerated , and he was otherwise seriously , painfully
and permanently injured , disabling and incapacitating him
from manual labor for the remainder of his life , and
WHEREAS , said F. Daniel is now 51 years of age and is unable
because of said injuries , to do manual labor, and was at the
time of said injuries earning $300.00 per month , and was re-
quired to expend the sum of $1,000.00 for medical attention ,
hospital bills , etc. , and
WHEREAS , he has sustained these serious and permanent in-
juries by reason of the alleged carelessness and negligence
of the employees of the State Highway Commission in the
conduct of the State's business , therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That F. Daniel be , and he is hereby authorized and
permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the State Highway Commission , or either, in the
Circuit Court of the county of his residence for such
damages as he may have suffered , if any , by reason
of any injury received by him through the careless-
ness or negligence of the State Highway Commis ..
sion , its agents , or employees . Said suit shall be for
any amount , not exceeding the sum of Six Thousand
( $6,000.00 ) Dollars , and in the event any judgment
is recovered by said Daniel in said suit for injuries
to himself , or same is compromised or settled , same
shall be paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by
warrant drawn on the State Treasurer , and paid
out of the General Fund .

1022 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 2. Either party to said suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be entered therein as in
any other civil suit , and the case may be settled and
adjusted with the consent and approval of the At-
torney General of Kentucky in the same way as any
other civil case.
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 574 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION , directing the Governor to pro-
claim January 31st of each year " Stephen Collins Foster Me-
morial Day " for the observance and commemoration of the
distinguished service which he rendered this Commonwealth
in the writing of its official song , "My Old Kentucky Home " ;
and
WHEREAS , the 31st of January , 1953 , A. D. , is the date of
copyright and first published issue of this musical composi-
tion now accredited to be America's greatest song of the
home , the heart and the fireside ; and
WHEREAS , by ageing tradition and common concent , this great
song which is known and loved by every American , has been
translated into many foreign languages , and has been sung
in many lands and climes throughout the world , is closely
associated with "Federal Hill " -the Old Kentucky Home State
Park- near Bardstown , Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS it is fitting that the recurring anniversary of the
day of issuance of this great song which has immortalized
Kentucky throughout the civilized world should be com-
memorated with suitable public and private musical exercises
in recognition of the lasting gifts of creative genius ,
Now therefore , be it resolved by the General As-
sembly of the State of Kentucky :
That the Governor of the State of Kentucky is
authorized and directed to issue annually a proc-
lamation calling upon all officials of the Government
and all citizens of the Commonwealth to take
cognizance of January 31st each year as " Stephen
Collins Foster Memorial Day " and to observe this
day in the schools , churches and other suitable
places with appropriate historical and memorial

1024 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ceremonies in commemoration of the Common-
wealth's deathless song " My Old Kentucky Home ."
Approved February 8, 1934 ,
Ruby Laffoon , Governor of Kentucky .

........ day of
A RESOLUTION authorizing S. M. Green , his personal Repre-
sentative , executor or administrator to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky, or either of them , and Statute of Limitation not to
apply until and from the passage of this resolution.
WHEREAS , on the ......, 1931 , the State High-
way Commission of Kentucky was engaged in Simpson
County, Kentucky , in the construction of a certain roadway
known as the Franklin and Morgantown road ; and
WHEREAS , it is alleged that it negligently left an open exca-
vation or ditch in said road without any guards , lights , signs
or notices of its existence to the traveling public ; and
WHEREAS , S. M. Green , driving his automobile on said road
ran into and fell into said excavation and ditch and was
seriously and permanently injured thereby, about his head ,
body , arms , limbs, and has suffered serious and great dam-
age ; therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That S. M. Green , of Franklin , Kentucky , be and
be is hereby authorized, empowered and permitted
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission of Kentucky or either of
them , in the Simpson Circuit Court for such dam-
ages as he may have sustained , if any, by reason of
his having driven or fallen in the said excavation or
ditch in said road , caused by the negligence of the
agents , servants or employees of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and/or the State Highway Commis-
sion , or either of them .
And in such suit he is permitted to show all the
facts and circumstances as to whether or not , the

CHAPTER 575 1025
employees or any employee of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky or the State Highway Commission ,
were negligent with reference to said ditch or ex-
cavation or negligent in any manner whatsoever
and/or in failing to properly notify the public trav-
eling on said road of such ditch or excavation or
any other negligence on the part of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or the State Highway Commis-
sion, their agents , servants or employees ; and if a
jury shall decide that there was negligence on the

monwealth of Kentucky or the State Highway Com-
mission, and that as a direct and proximate result

or administrator in said suit , as damages , same shall

rant drawn on the State Treasurer .

Highway Commission , or either of them , and the said

CHAPTER 576 .

wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission of

Said resolution is as follows , viz.:
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission of Kentucky con-
structed a public highway on Project No. 11 , in Ohio County ,
Kentucky , which work was begun in May , 1925 , and com-
pleted during said year , and whereas said grade and drain of
said road was constructed by Ellis & Smeathers , contractors ,

wealth of Kentucky , and whereas said contractors acting pur-
suant to their contract with the State Highway Commission

lessly constructed and maintained as not to have a sufficient

age the lands owned by E. G. Barrass on the south side of

trating in these small openings with such force as to wash

way Commission , its agents and employees the said E. G.

the agents , servants and employees of the State of Kentucky
at said hospital, said Grow was injured about the head , body
and limbs ; and , whereas , if such damage and injuries were
sustained through the negligence of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , the Central State Hospital at Lakeland , the State
Prison at Frankfort , or any agents , servants or employees of
said Commonwealth of Kentucky at said institution , a reason-
able amount should be paid the said Charles Grow by the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
Therefore , be it resolved by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the said Charles Grow be and he is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court for such
damage as he may have sustained , if any, by reason
of injury to his head , body and limbs , caused by the
negligence of the Central State Hospital at Lake-
land , or the State Prison at Frankfort , their agents ,
servants or employees ; and in the event any judg-
ment is recovered by the said Grow in said suit ,
same shall be paid by the Treasurer of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky on warrants from the
Auditor , to be paid out of the general fund , and both
the said Grow and the Commonwealth of Kentucky
shall have the right to appeal said case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 580 .
JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of the City of Stanford ,
Ky., and authorizing the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky to ascertain the cost of construction of Federal High-
ways Nos . 27 and 168 , through a portion of the corporate

CHAPTER 580 1031
limits of said City , over the routes hereinafter described and
pay the amount thereof to the City of Stanford .
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission has constructed
State Highway No. 27 , from the southern corporate limits of
the limits of the city along and over Somerset Street to Main
Sreet in said city, and has constructed State Highway No.
168 , along and over east Main Street in said city to the east
side of Whitley Avenue , and ,
WHEREAS , the City Council of said city , without electing to
act under and in pursuance of the terms and conditions and
provisions of Chapter 178 of the Acts of 1926 , has constructed
or caused to be constructed , by Ordinances duly enacted ,
streets running through said city , upon the ten year bond
plan as provided by Kentucky Statutes , Section 3706 , of a
type of construction different from the type constructed by
the Highway Commission within the city on the streets above
named and that so constructed by the city, being that por-
tion of State Highway No. 168 beginning at the city limits
on Danville Avenue and along said Avenue to Main Street ,
thence along Main Street to the west side of Whitley Avenue ,
a distance of six thousand and eighty -three ( 6,083 ) feet , and
that portion of State Highway No. 27 beginning at the inter-
section of Lancaster Street with Main Street and along said
Lancaster Street towards northern city limits , a distance of
twenty-five hundred and thirty -two ( 2,532 ) feet , and ,
WHEREAS , the City of Stanford desires to avail itself of the
provisions of Chapter 178 of the Acts of 1928 , now section
4356 -t-8, Kentucky Statutes , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the Senate ( the House of Repre
sentatives concurring therein ) :
That the Commonwealth of Kentucky , by and
through its agent , the State Highway Commission
of Kentucky , be and it is hereby authorized to ascer-
tain the actual cost of the construction of Federal
Highways Nos . 27 and 168 , over the routes within
the corporate limits of the City of Stanford , here-
inabove described , and having done so , the State
Highway Commission of Kentucky be and it is
hereby authorized to enter into a contract with said
City, wherein and whereby the Commonwealth of

1032 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Kentucky by and through its agent , the State High-
way Commission of Kentucky , as party of the first
part , binds itself and agrees to pay to the City of
Stanford , as party of the second part, such sum as
the State Highway Commission of Kentucky , may in
its discretion determine to be the actual cost of the
construction through the said city of 6,083 feet and
2,532 feet , of the same type of road already con-
structed by said Commission on Federal Highways
Nos . 27 and 168 , within the city limits as herein-
above set forth and of the same width , to -wit : 18
feet , and providing that the City shall pay or as-
sume the cost of the additional cost of construction ,
by reason of the higher type of road within the limits
of said city along said portions of said State High-
ways , Nos . 27 and 168 , and the Auditor of Public
Accounts , upon the execution of said contract and
with the filing of same in the office of the Secretary
of State , and upon presentation to the Auditor of
Public Accounts of a certified copy thereof, be and
he is hereby authorized, draw his warrant upon the
Treasurer of the State of Kentucky , payable to the
order of the City of Stanford , for such sum , as may
be decided upon as provided in said contract and as
provided in Chapter 178 , of the Acts of 1928-now
section 4356 -t-8, Kentucky Statutes .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 581.
RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Lane Taulbee Holliday, of
Insko , Morgan County, Kentucky , to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and/or the State Highway Commission to test
the validity and to construe a deed of date September 15,
1926 , to the Commonwealth of Kentucky and/or the State
Highway Commission and for damages for the use of the
property belonging to her and damage to lot and buildings ;
and ,

CHAPTER 581 1033
WHEREAS , during the year 1933 , the State Highway Commis-
sion constructed and caused to be constructed , a road , High-
way No. 28 , over the property claimed by and in the posses-
sion of Mrs. Holliday , near Insko , Morgan County , Kentucky ;
and ,
WHEREAS , the vendor of Mrs. Holliday , Hugh Armstrong had
signed and acknowledged a deed for right of way through
said property ; and ,
WHEREAS , there are erasures in said deed , and said deed was
not recorded until more than four years after the purchase of
said property by Mrs. Holliday and there have been three
or more surveys of the right of way through said property ;
and ,
WHEREAS , it is claimed by Mrs. Lane Taulbee Holliday that
the deed was not delivered to the State Highway Commis-
sion or the Commonwealth of Kentucky until about four
years after it was signed and acknowledged and after her
purchase of the property , and that the erasure of a provi-
sion therein for the protection from damages for the build-
ings was made after the said deed was signed and acknowl-
edged and after her purchase of the property , and that the
road was not constructed on the right of way as surveyed
and referred to in the deed for the right of way and that the
description of the said right of way is so indefinite and un-
certain as to be of no value in determining its location ; and
WHEREAS , the property and the buildings thereon have been
seriously damaged by the construction of said road , and Mrs.
Holliday is claiming damages from Morgan County and the
State Highway Commission and / or the Commonwealth of
Kentucky ; now therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That Mrs. Lane Taulbee Holliday , of Insko ,
Morgan County , is hereby authorized and permitted
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and/or the
State Highway Commission to test the sufficiency ,
validity , and construction of the deed from Hugh
Armstrong and wife to the State Highway Commis-
sion and/or the Commonwealth of Kentucky , signed
and acknowledged on September 15 , 1926 , and for
any damages she may have sustained and that may

1034 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be chargeable against the said State Highway Com-
mission and/or the Commonwealth of Kentucky , if
any, by the construction of said road over the prop-
erty claimed and in her possession , near Insko , Ken-
tucky , not to exceed the sum of $1,500.00 .
§ 2. Either party to said suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be rendered therein as in
other civil cases . The controversy may be settled
and adjusted in the same way as other civil cases .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 582 .
A JOINT AND CONCURRENT RESOLUTION providing for
the payment of money to G. W. Lawrence , A. J. Bauer , Dan
Mahoney, Joe Rosson , Robert Baker, Dennis Rath , Robert
Pulliam and Forrest Sanford for extra services in caring for
the offices in the State Capitol building during the present
session of the Legislature , and Mrs. Ollie Brightwell and Mrs.
Alice Allnut , employees , engaged as matrons of the State
Capitol.
WHEREAS , the aforesaid persons , employees of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , have been required to perform overtime
and extra services because of the present session of the
General Assembly.
Now therefore , be is resolved by the House of
Representatives of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Senate of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky concurring therein :
That G. W. Lawrence , A. J. Bauer , Dan Mahoney,
Joe Rosson , Robert Baker , Dennis Rath , Robert
Pulliam , Forrest Sanford , Mrs. Ollie Brightwell and
Mrs. Alice Allnut are each hereby allowed the sum
of one dollar $1.00 per day for each day of the pres-
ent session of the General Assembly , which allow-
ance shall be in addition to the salaries they now re-
ceive for their services .

CHAPTER 584 1035
The appropriation hereby made is to be paid out
of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise ap-
propriated , and the Auditor of Public Accounts is
hereby ordered and directed to draw his warrant
upon the Treasurer to each of the above named per-
sons for the amount due them as herein provided .
Approved March 19, 1934 .
CHAPTER 583 .
JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of Jodie Oliver , Howard
Black and Browning Green , employees engaged to keep the
State Capitol clean .
WHEREAS , the above named employees have had their work
materially increased during the present session of the Gen-
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and have
worked over time to keep the State Capitol clean for said
session ;
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That said Jodie Oliver , Howard Black and Brown-
ing Green , be each allowed the sum of $ 1.00 per day
for each day of the present session of this General
Assembly , which allowance shall be in addition to
what they now receive for their services , as above
set out .
All of said amounts to be paid by the Treasurer
on warrant drawn on him by the Auditor of Public
Accounts out of any funds not otherwise ap-
propriated .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 584 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION of the House of Representatives
and the Senate of Kentucky , providing for the appointment of
a committee to investigate the desirability of securing the

1036 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
birthplace of Ollie James in Crittenden County , to be used
for a State park .
WHEREAS , Ollie James is now regarded as one of the nation's
ablest statesmen and one of Kentucky's noblest sons ; and
WHEREAS , he was born in Crittenden County, of native Ken-
tucky parents , and the house in which he was born is still
standing and in good repair ; and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth of Kentucky may be able to
secure the said birthplace of Ollie James for posterity by
creating there a State park,
Now be it resolved by the General Assembly of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That a committee of five persons be appointed by
the Governor of the Commonwealth to investigate
the desirability and advisability of securing the
birthplace of Ollie James for a State park .
Be It Further Resolved , That after said investi-
gation shall have been completed , the said commit-
tee shall make a report to the Governor of the Com-
monwealth and the State Park Commission .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 585 .
RESOLUTION authorizing E. E. Tarter of Hickman County to
sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway
Commission .
WHEREAS , during the years 1927 and 1928 the State Highway
Commission of Kentucky built a highway thru Hickman
County , known as Ü. S. 51 , which highway crosses Obion
Creek ; and
WHEREAS , a bridge was constructed over said Creek by the
State Highway Commission and a levee was constructed to
protect the highway, which was found insufficient to pre-
vent an overflow of the highway ; and
WHEREAS , in 1930 the State Highway Commission , thru its
contractor , Cox & Frasier of La Center , Kentucky , raised
said levee about four ( 4 ) feet higher and left an opening
therein about one hundred and thirty ( 130 ) feet long north
of said Creek , which causes an overflow at said point , cover-

CHAPTER 586 1037
ing a sixty ( 60 ) -acre field belonging to the said E. E. Tarter ,
which has made it impossible for him to cultivate said land
and has caused deep gullies to wash therein , leaving nothing
but the sub -soil ;
NOW , THEREFORE , to determine the liability of the State
Highway Commission and the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
causing said water to flow upon his said land and wash the
same ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That E. E. Tarter, of Hickman County be, and he
hereby is empowered and authorized to file and
prosecute an action in the Hickman Circuit Court
Against the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the
State Highway Commission , to determine the liabil-
ity of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission for the alleged injury to his
said land as aforesaid . Provided , however , that the
amount of recovery for any injury which the said
Tarter shall have sustained shall not exceed Four
Thousand ( $4,000.00 ) Dollars .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 586 .
RESOLUTION authorizing William Cox to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either .
WHEREAS , on or about the seventeenth day of May, 1933 ,
William Cox , a resident of Cooper , Wayne County , Kentucky ,
was run over and injured on the highway leading from Mon-
ticello to Cooper , in Wayne County , Kentucky , wherein it is
claimed that while the said William Cox was walking the
highway he was struck and injured , both of his legs being
broken, and receiving other injuries , by a truck operated by
one of the employees of the State Highway Commission , in a
very careless , negligent and reckless manner , the employee
driving the truck at an excessive rate of speed , on the wrong
and improper side of the road , and while he was in an intoxi-
cated condition , therefore ,

1038 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That William Cox be , and he is , hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or either ,
in the Wayne Circuit Court , the County of the resi-
dence of said William Cox for damages for the loss
of earning power, physical suffering , and for hos-
pital and medical bills of the said William Cox ,
which were all brought about and caused by the neg-
ligence of the employee of the State Highway Com-
mission. Said suit shall be for any amount not ex-
ceeding the sum of six thousand ( $ 6,000.00 ) dollars ,
and in the event any judgment is recovered by said
William Cox in said suit for injuries to himself , or
same is compromised or settled , said judgment shall
be paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by war-
rant drawn on the State Treasurer and paid out of
the General Fund .
Either party to said suit may appeal from any
judgment which may be entered therein as in any
other civil suit, and the case may be settled and ad-
justed with the consent and approval of the At-
torney General of Kentucky in the same way as any
other civil case.
Approved March 19, 1934 .
CHAPTER 587.
RESOLUTION providing for furnishing Kentucky Directories .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That the State Librarian is hereby author-
ized and directed to purchase at not exceeding two
dollars per copy five hundred and fifty copies of the

CHAPTER 588 1039
book entitled " Kentucky Directory 1934 " for the
use of the Courts , state and county officers and mem-
bers of the General Assembly . The Librarian will
furnish the members of the General Assembly and
such officers as may be designated by the Presiding
officers of the respective Houses a copy of same and
will furnish the state officials with copies thereof
and will send a copy thereof to the Clerk of the
County Court of each county in the state to be kept
in his office . The Librarian will furnish to the
Librarian of each state and territory of the union
a copy of said book in exchange for similar books
from said states and territories , and a copy to the
Librarian of Congress , and deliver to the Secretary
of State fifty copies to exchange .
§ 2. It being necessary that the officers should
have the books herein provided for , an emergency is
declared and this resolution shall take effect upon
its adoption and approval by the Governor .
Approved March 27 , 1934 .
CHAPTER 588 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION for help for enrolling clerks
for Senate and House.
WHEREAS , it will necessitate the employment of additional aid
by the enrolling clerks of the House and Senate in order to
expedite the business ,
Now therefore, be it resolved by the Senate , the
House of Representatives concurring therein :
§ 1. That the enrolling clerks of the House and
Senate be authorized and directed to employ all
necessary assistants who shall be entitled to 35c per
page for all bills enrolled by them ; and the clerks ,
House and Senate , are directed to issue their vouch-
ers to the Auditor of Public Accounts who shall draw
his warrant in their favor for the amounts certified

1040 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
by the respective clerks on itemized accounts ren-
dered . Said amounts shall not include any charge
by either the enrolling clerk of the House or Senate
for any work done by them .
§ 2. Provided , however , that not more than
$400.00 shall be expended for this purpose in either
house .
§ 3. It being necessary to have the work herein
provided for to be done at once , and the officers
charged with the duty are not able to perform the
volume of work personally , an emergency is declared
and this resolution becomes in force and effect upon
its adoption .
Approved March 17 , 1934.
